via `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts:768-779`, with tests in `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.model-override.test.ts:105-131`.

What I could *not* find is any heartbeat-specific tool toggle or tool allow/deny override. The heartbeat path still just calls `getReplyFromConfig(...)` with `isHeartbeat: true` plus those lightweight options; there is no `heartbeat.tools`-style switch in the runner or schema path that I found.

So I’d classify this as a valid enhancement request, but the fix seam is probably lower than the scheduler. The likely implementation point is wherever heartbeat options are translated into the agent-runner request, so a heartbeat can override tool policy before tools are exposed, rather than trying to strip tools after the fact.

Short version: valid request, current tree already has lighter-weight heartbeat controls, but not a true “disable tools for heartbeat” control yet.

- Maintainers can comment `@clawsweeper explain` to ask for more context, or `@clawsweeper stop` to stop active automation.

</details>

**Summary**
Keep open: current main and the latest release still lack a heartbeat-scoped tool toggle, and ordinary heartbeat turns only set `disableTools` for due-commitment delivery. The issue is a narrow feature/config addition with a clear existing runtime seam.

**Next step**

**Security**
Needs attention: The issue itself is security-sensitive because heartbeat turns cannot currently withhold execution-capable tools from weak local models.

<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Add documented `heartbeat.tools: false` support for defaults and per-agent heartbeat config, map it to `disableTools: true` with an empty skill filter during heartbeat reply construction, and preserve the current tool-capable default behavior.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes for the config gap: current-main source shows a strict heartbeat schema with no `tools` key, and ordinary heartbeat reply options do not set `disableTools` except for due commitments. I did not live-reproduce the reported local-model tool loop.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

Yes. Plumbing a boolean heartbeat config into the existing `disableTools` seam is the narrowest maintainable solution; a richer allowlist can be a later extension if maintainers want it.

  Confidence: 0.86

- **current_schema_missing_tools:** `HeartbeatSchema` is strict and lists heartbeat config fields through `skipWhenBusy`, with no `tools`, `disableTools`, or allowlist field. ([`src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts:40`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/2c8f78e723ec/src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts#L40), [2c8f78e723ec](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/2c8f78e723ec))

- Labels: docs, cli, size: S, triage: refactor-only, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, status: 📣 needs proof

Body:
```markdown
## 요약

이 PR은 local one-shot model run correctness를 복구하기 위한 승격 패치셋이다.

포함된 변경은 두 가지다.

1. `infer model run --local`의 기본 동작을 stateless로 변경
   - 기본 one-shot은 ephemeral session을 사용
   - explicit `sessionId/sessionKey`가 있을 때만 reuse가 살아 있음

2. session store / transcript fallback root를 profile-scoped로 정렬
   - local one-shot 실행이 global `~/.openclaw/...`가 아니라
     profile/agent-dir 기준 persistence root를 사용

추가로, 위 두 패치가 `Orin-25`, `Orin-26` canary에서 노드 독립적으로 재현되었다는 기준선 문서를 함께 기록했다.

## 배경

기존 local one-shot model run은 암묵적으로 main session을 재사용할 수 있었고,
transcript fallback도 global root에 붙을 수 있었다.

그 결과:
- text / vision cross-turn contamination
- local canary와 unrelated global session의 결합
- one-shot probe semantics와 실제 persistence scope의 불일치

가 발생할 수 있었다.

이번 변경으로 기본 semantics를 다음처럼 고정한다.

- one-shot 기본: stateless
- 연속성: explicit opt-in
- persistence root: profile-scoped

## 포함 커밋

- `4a41951056` `fix(cli): make local model run stateless by default`
- `05b1862690` `fix(sessions): keep local transcript fallback profile-scoped`
- `b3392f307a` `docs(refactor): record local model run promotion baseline`

## 범위

포함:
- local model run one-shot session semantics
- transcript fallback persistence scope
- promotion baseline / patchset docs

제외:
- discovery/provider/plugin fast-path
- TTS/tool/silent-reply canary workarounds
- performance-only canary gates

## 검증

Focused tests:
- `src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts` 관련 신규 assertion 통과
- `src/config/sessions/transcript.test.ts` 관련 신규 assertion 통과

추가 참고:
- `transcript.test.ts` 전체 파일 런에서 기존 file mode (`0o600`) 가정 1건 실패가 남아 있으나,
  이번 패치와는 무관함

Canary validation:
- `Orin-25`, `Orin-26` 둘 다에서 재현 확인
- text only: 정상
- vision only: 정상
- mixed `vision -> text`: 정상
- explicit same-session reuse: 정상
- profile-local transcript/store 생성 확인

## 위험도

낮음.

이 PR은 local one-shot correctness와 persistence scope만 다룬다.
성능용 canary 우회나 provider/plugin shortcut은 포함하지 않는다.

```

GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 39583 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/39583 - Title: Fix double JSON-serialization of vLLM content blocks - State: OPEN - Author: gambletan - Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary - When `extractTextFromChatContent` receives a string that is a JSON-stringified array of content blocks (e.g. from vLLM openai-completions), it now detects and parses those blocks instead of returning the raw JSON string. This prevents downstream `JSON.stringify` from double-serializing the content. - Added comprehensive tests for `extractTextFromChatContent` covering plain strings, arrays, JSON-stringified arrays, and edge cases. Closes #38543 ## Test plan - [x] New unit tests in `src/shared/chat-content.test.ts` (11 tests passing) - [ ] Verify vLLM openai-completions responses render correctly without nested JSON 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-08T07:16:31Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a double JSON-serialization bug in `extractTextFromChatContent` by detecting when a string input is a JSON-encoded array of content blocks (as emitted by vLLM's openai-completions path), parsing it, and recursively processing it as an array — preventing downstream `JSON.stringify` from re-serializing the already-stringified content. - The fix correctly handles the common case (all-text content block arrays from vLLM) and is well-tested for that scenario. - **Logic gap in mixed-array detection**: The `every()` guard requires `"text" in item`, which means any JSON-stringified array that contains non-text blocks (e.g. `{"type":"image",...}`) will fail detection and be returned as a raw JSON string. The existing array path already skips non-text blocks gracefully, but this shortcut in the string path is inconsistent with that behaviour — dropping the `"text" in item` requirement and relying only on `"type" in item` would bring them into alignment. - The test suite covers the intended cases well but is missing a scenario for a stringified mixed-content array (image + text blocks), which would expose the detection gap.

Confidence Score: 3/5

- Safe to merge for text-only vLLM responses, but the mixed-content edge case can still produce raw JSON strings where text extraction is expected. - The core fix is correct and well-tested for the primary use case. However, the `"text" in item` predicate in the detection guard is more restrictive than the existing array-path logic, creating a silent inconsistency for mixed content block arrays. This is a real but likely rare edge case for vLLM today, so the change is not dangerous — but the gap should be addressed before closing the issue entirely. - `src/shared/chat-content.ts` — the `every()` detection predicate on lines 21-23 needs the `"text" in item` condition removed. Last reviewed commit: aa0d56f - openclaw-barnacle at 2026-04-24T04:33:54Z: This pull request has been automatically marked as stale due to inactivity. Please add updates or it will be closed. - clawsweeper at 2026-04-26T02:02:16Z: Codex review: needs real behavior proof before merge. **Workflow note:** Future ClawSweeper reviews update this same comment in place.

**Summary** The branch adds JSON parsing of stringified chat content block arrays in `extractTextFromChatContent` plus colocated tests for plain strings, arrays, stringified arrays, mixed blocks, and option handling. Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main treats a stringified content-block array as ordinary string content, while real array content is already text-extracted. I did not establish a live vLLM reproduction in this read-only review. **PR rating** Overall: 🧂 unranked krab Proof: 🧂 unranked krab Patch quality: 🧂 unranked krab Summary: Not quality-ready because required real behavior proof is missing and the patch has blocking parser correctness risks on a conflicting branch. Rank-up moves: - Attach redacted vLLM/OpenAI-compatible runtime proof showing responses render without nested JSON. - Rebase onto current main and preserve the existing `coerceChatContentText` hardening. - Fix the parsing boundary and guards, including whitespace-tolerant parsing and unrelated JSON-string preservation tests.
What the crustacean ranks mean - 🦀 challenger crab: rare, exceptional readiness with strong proof, clean implementation, and convincing validation. - 🦞 diamond lobster: very strong readiness with only minor maintainer review expected. - 🐚 platinum hermit: good normal PR, likely mergeable with ordinary maintainer review. - 🦐 gold shrimp: useful signal, but proof or patch confidence is still limited. - 🦪 silver shellfish: thin signal; proof, validation, or implementation needs work. - 🧂 unranked krab: not merge-ready because proof is missing/unusable or there are serious correctness or safety concerns. - 🌊 off-meta tidepool: rating does not apply to this item. Shiny media proof means a screenshot, video, or linked artifact directly shows the changed behavior. Runtime, network, CSP, and security claims still need visible diagnostics.
**Real behavior proof** Needs real behavior proof before merge: The PR body only cites unit tests and leaves vLLM verification unchecked; the contributor should add redacted terminal output, logs, screenshots, recordings, or linked artifacts from a real vLLM/OpenAI-compatible run and then update the PR body for re-review. **Risk before merge** - The branch is conflicting against current main and must be rebased without losing the current `coerceChatContentText` hardening in `src/shared/chat-content.ts`. - Whitespace-wrapped JSON-stringified content block arrays still fall through as plain strings because the PR checks raw string boundaries before parsing. - The broad `type`-key guard can reinterpret unrelated JSON array strings as chat blocks and return `null`, suppressing visible message text in callers that drop null content. - No redacted vLLM/OpenAI-compatible runtime proof is attached, so the provider path has not been demonstrated after the patch. **Maintainer options:** 1. **Require rebase, parser repair, and proof (recommended)** Before merge, require a rebase that preserves current coercion behavior, fixes the trim and shape checks, adds regressions, and includes redacted vLLM/OpenAI-compatible runtime proof. 2. **Replace with a fresh narrow PR** If this branch cannot be updated cleanly, maintainers can ask for a fresh replacement that credits this contribution and carries over the useful stringified-content tests. **Next step before merge** Contributor or human follow-up is needed because the external PR lacks required real behavior proof and needs a rebase plus focused parser corrections before normal merge review. **Security** Cleared: The diff only changes shared text extraction logic and colocated tests, with no dependency, workflow, install, release, secret, permission, or network-surface changes. **Review findings** - GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 41737 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/41737 - Title: vLLM: add endpoint lifecycle management, multi-endpoint selection, and stale default cleanup - State: OPEN - Author: gejifeng - Labels: docs, gateway, scripts, commands, agents, size: XL, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 automation, merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary This PR upgrades vLLM support from a single-endpoint setup into a full management flow with safer default-model handling. ## What changed - Added endpoint lifecycle management in interactive vLLM setup: - use configured endpoint/model - add endpoint - update endpoint and rescan models - delete endpoint - Added multi-endpoint support with stable provider keys (for example: vllm, vllm-2, vllm-3). - Added model discovery + multi-select from /v1/models, with manual fallback when discovery is empty. - Preserved endpoint-specific auth/profile metadata so existing endpoints can be reopened and edited cleanly. - Suppressed implicit single-endpoint vLLM injection when explicit managed vLLM providers already exist. - Added stale managed vLLM default/fallback cleanup to prevent stale model refs after endpoint changes/removal. - Updated auth/model-picker wiring so vLLM setup and cleanup paths are applied consistently. - Updated docs for multi-endpoint behavior and endpoint-key model refs. ## Why The old flow had three gaps: - Reconfigure flow did not reliably reuse saved endpoints. - Interactive setup was effectively single-model even when the server exposed multiple models. - There was no practical endpoint management flow for users running multiple local vLLM servers. ## Behavior notes - Implicit discovery still works for simple single-endpoint usage when no explicit managed vLLM provider exists. - Once explicit managed providers exist, implicit injection is skipped to avoid collisions. - During endpoint update, leaving API key blank keeps the saved credential while still allowing discovery. ## Validation - Added/updated targeted tests for endpoint management, multi-endpoint setup, stale-default pruning, and related auth/model-picker flows. - Updated docs: - docs/providers/vllm.md - docs/concepts/model-providers.md - docs/gateway/local-models.md ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-10T04:37:47Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR reworks the vLLM interactive setup from a single-prompt linear flow into a reusable menu-driven management flow supporting multiple saved endpoints, base-URL pre-fill on reconfigure, `/v1/models` scanning with multi-select, and per-provider credential storage in the auth profile store. **Key changes:** - `src/commands/vllm-setup.ts` — complete rewrite; new helpers `collectManagedVllmProviders`, `configureEndpoint`, `generateNextVllmProviderKey`, `saveVllmCredential`, `removeVllmCredential`, and `promptVllmModels` replace the old single-path function. - `src/agents/models-config.providers.ts` — implicit vLLM injection guard widened from checking only the exact `vllm` key to any key matching `vllm` or `vllm-*`. - `src/commands/model-picker.test.ts` — the env-var-reference assertion on the provider config is correctly removed since credentials are now stored in the auth store rather than the provider config; however the `auth-profiles.js` mock is missing `resolveApiKeyForProfile` (flagged inline). - `src/commands/vllm-setup.test.ts` — new test file with solid coverage of the three main new scenarios. - Docs updated across three files to describe multi-model discovery, the management menu, and multiple explicit endpoints. **Notable concerns:** - The `vi.mock` for `auth-profiles.js` in `model-picker.test.ts` is incomplete — `resolveApiKeyForProfile` is now called by the updated setup flow but absent from the mock factory, creating a latent test failure for any scenario that exercises the existing-endpoint update path through that file. - `isManagedVllmProviderKey` matching logic is duplicated between `vllm-setup.ts` and `models-config.providers.ts`; a single source of truth would reduce maintenance risk. - The `while (true)` management loop has no escape after the last endpoint is deleted — the user is immediately pushed into `configureEndpoint` with no option to cancel.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Safe to merge with minor test-mock gap that doesn't affect current test coverage - The core logic is sound and well-tested: key generation, provider config CRUD, credential storage, multi-model discovery, and suppression of implicit vLLM injection all work correctly. The new test file covers the three primary scenarios introduced by the PR and assertions are precise. The score is 4 rather than 5 because of one latent issue: the auth-profiles mock in model-picker.test.ts is missing resolveApiKeyForProfile, which will silently fail any future test that exercises the existing-endpoint update path through that file. The UX concern (no escape after deleting the last endpoint) and the duplicated matching logic are low-risk style issues that do not affect correctness today. - src/commands/model-picker.test.ts — the vi.mock for auth-profiles.js needs resolveApiKeyForProfile added to prevent future test regressions.

Comments Outside Diff (2)

 1. `src/commands/vllm-setup.ts`, line 643-649 ([link](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/6b3a9e290ce907e936800f69b2f8948a8dbae5ed/src/commands/vllm-setup.ts#L643-L649)) **Duplicated `isManagedVllmProviderKey` logic** The exact same matching pattern (`normalized === "vllm" || normalized.startsWith("vllm-")`) is inlined again in `models-config.providers.ts` (`resolveVllmImplicitProvider`) rather than importing `isManagedVllmProviderKey` from this module. If the matching rule ever changes (e.g. to allow `vllm_3` as a variant), it must be updated in both places independently, which is a maintenance hazard. Consider exporting `isManagedVllmProviderKey` from `vllm-setup.ts` and importing it in `models-config.providers.ts`, or extracting it to a shared utility module.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/commands/vllm-setup.ts Line: 643-649 Comment: **Duplicated `isManagedVllmProviderKey` logic** The exact same matching pattern (`normalized === "vllm" || normalized.startsWith("vllm-")`) is inlined again in `models-config.providers.ts` (`resolveVllmImplicitProvider`) rather than importing `isManagedVllmProviderKey` from this module. If the matching rule ever changes (e.g. to allow `vllm_3` as a variant), it must be updated in both places independently, which is a maintenance hazard. Consider exporting `isManagedVllmProviderKey` from `vllm-setup.ts` and importing it in `models-config.providers.ts`, or extracting it to a shared utility module. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
2. `src/commands/model-picker.test.ts`, line 25-30 ([link](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/6b3a9e290ce907e936800f69b2f8948a8dbae5ed/src/commands/model-picker.test.ts#L25-L30)) **`resolveApiKeyForProfile` missing from `auth-profiles.js` mock** The `vi.mock("../agents/auth-profiles.js")` factory here does not include `resolveApiKeyForProfile`, which is now imported and called by `vllm-setup.ts` inside `resolveStoredApiKey`. The current test scenario (new endpoint, no `profileId`) avoids the call because `resolveStoredApiKey` returns early when `profileId` is undefined. However, any future test that exercises the "update an existing endpoint" path through this module will silently receive `undefined` as the function and throw a confusing runtime error instead of a useful test failure. The mock should be expanded to include this function alongside the other `auth-profiles.js` exports being mocked.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/commands/model-picker.test.ts Line: 25-30 Comment: **`resolveApiKeyForProfile` missing from `auth-profiles.js` mock** The `vi.mock("../agents/auth-profiles.js")` factory here does not include `resolveApiKeyForProfile`, which is now imported and called by `vllm-setup.ts` inside `resolveStoredApiKey`. The current test scenario (new endpoint, no `profileId`) avoids the call because `resolveStoredApiKey` returns early when `profileId` is undefined. However, any future test that exercises the "update an existing endpoint" path through this module will silently receive `undefined` as the function and throw a confusing runtime error instead of a useful test failure. The mock should be expanded to include this function alongside the other `auth-profiles.js` exports being mocked. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

input.title: [Bug]: Make Dream Diary narrative timeout configurable for slow/serial local model backends
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #84301: [Bug]: Make Dream Diary narrative timeout configurable for slow/serial local model backends
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 84301
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84301
- Title: [Bug]: Make Dream Diary narrative timeout configurable for slow/serial local model backends
- State: CLOSED
- Author: rogerallen1
- Labels: bug, bug:behavior, P2, clawsweeper:needs-live-repro, impact:session-state, issue-rating: 🐚 platinum hermit

Body:
```markdown
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

Dreaming’s Dream Diary narrative subagent wait timeout appears to be hardcoded to 60 seconds. On local model backends such as LM Studio, prompt processing may be serialized even when OpenClaw starts multiple dreaming narrative runs in parallel/queued form. This can cause dreaming narrative runs to exceed 60s and fail even though the backend is still working normally.

### Steps to reproduce

Dreaming starts multiple narrative prompts around the same sweep. LM Studio processes prompt work serially, so queued narrative runs can exceed the 60s wait budget.

Related logs include errors like:

EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released
and/or timeout-related narrative/embedded-run logs if present.

### Expected behavior

The Dream Diary narrative timeout should be configurable, or OpenClaw should reduce narrative concurrency for local/serial model backends so dreaming can complete reliably.


### Actual behavior

Related logs include errors like:

EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released
and/or timeout-related narrative/embedded-run logs if present.

### OpenClaw version

2025.5.12

### Operating system

Ubuntu

### Install method

npn

### Model

qwen/qwen3.6 35B

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw -> local

### Additional provider/model setup details

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

Add a user-facing config key for the Dream Diary narrative timeout, for example under:
json
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "memory-core": {
"config": {
          "dreaming": {
            "narrative": {
              "timeoutMs": 300000,
              "concurrency": 1
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
Exact config shape can differ, but the important part is making the current 60s timeout tunable.
```

Comments/context:
```markdown
- clawsweeper at 2026-05-19T20:36:13Z:
Thanks for the context here. I swept through the related work, and this is now duplicate or superseded.

Close as duplicate/superseded: the timeout and serial-provider concurrency symptoms are real, but older open issues and an open fix PR already track the remaining work with stronger evidence and a concrete implementation path.

Canonical path: Keep https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/76333 as the timeout/config contract thread, keep https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75930 for the serial-provider concurrency thread, and review or replace https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/78440 as the focused timeout implementation.

So I’m closing this here and keeping the remaining discussion on https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/76333, https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75930, and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/78440.

<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Keep https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/76333 as the timeout/config contract thread, keep https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75930 for the serial-provider concurrency thread, and review or replace https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/78440 as the focused timeout implementation.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

No high-confidence live reproduction is provided in this issue; the source path is clear because current main still waits exactly 60s per narrative and can run three detached narratives at once. Older canonical issues provide stronger runtime evidence for the same timeout and concurrency failures.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

No, keeping this separate issue is not the best way to solve the problem. The maintainable path is to consolidate the timeout contract in the older timeout issue and open timeout PR, while leaving the concurrency policy question on the older serial-provider issue.

Security review:

Security review: This is a non-security issue triage review with no proposed patch to inspect.

What I checked:

- **Current main still has a fixed narrative wait budget:** Dream Diary narrative generation uses `NARRATIVE_TIMEOUT_MS = 60_000` and passes that constant directly to `params.subagent.waitForRun`, so this issue is not already fixed on main. ([`extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts:99`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/384451343191/extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts#L99), [384451343191](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/384451343191))
- **Current main still allows three detached narratives at once:** The detached narrative queue is capped at `DETACHED_NARRATIVE_CONCURRENCY = 3`, which matches the issue's concern that serial local backends can still queue narrative work behind other runs. ([`extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts:1038`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/384451343191/extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts#L1038), [384451343191](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/384451343191))
- **Schema and docs still lack a user-facing timeout key:** The manifest exposes `dreaming.execution.defaults.model` but no `timeoutMs`, and the public memory config reference lists only `enabled`, `frequency`, and `model` as user-facing dreaming settings. ([`extensions/memory-core/openclaw.plugin.json:66`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/384451343191/extensions/memory-core/openclaw.plugin.json#L66), [384451343191](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/384451343191))
- **Latest release still has the same behavior:** The v2026.5.19 tag still contains the fixed 60s narrative timeout and three-wide detached narrative concurrency cap, so this is not shipped as fixed. ([`extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts:99`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a185ca283a74/extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts#L99), [a185ca283a74](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a185ca283a74))
- **Canonical timeout issue already exists:** https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/76333 is an older open report for the same fixed `NARRATIVE_TIMEOUT_MS` timeout problem, with repeated runtime timeout evidence and prior ClawSweeper review context.
- **Canonical timeout PR already exists:** https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/78440 is an open PR that adds `dreaming.execution.defaults.timeoutMs` and per-phase timeout schema/docs/tests, and wires the resolved timeout into the narrative wait path.

Likely related people:

- **RayWoo:** Authored the merged PR that changed Dream Diary narrative timeout behavior from 15s to the current 60s constant without adding a user-facing timeout contract. (role: timeout behavior contributor; confidence: high; commits: [962fe46fd839](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/962fe46fd839a8ec20d0cced005532b235368609), [ad6e1cd3a076](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/ad6e1cd3a0764759b397c0dba7d22447a7efa4a4); files: `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts`, `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts`)
- **KeWang0622:** Authored the merged PR that added the shared FIFO limiter and the current three-wide detached Dream Diary concurrency cap. (role: detached narrative limiter contributor; confidence: high; commits: [746feb7fcc46](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/746feb7fcc463112ef826324610b14d6f1dbcd09), [26ff2b13adf9](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/26ff2b13adf9023e297eb16c04de5aae9bed81e2), [071ead88b21d](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/071ead88b21d1817d1c2a59eeb09175bb35875f0); files: `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts`, `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts`, `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts`)
- **Patrick-Erichsen:** Authored the managed dreaming cron decoupling work that moved cron Dream Diary generation into detached narrative paths near the timeout and concurrency behavior. (role: major dreaming cron refactor contributor; confidence: medium; commits: [492a061dd6c6](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/492a061dd6c664a05650620d3edf1a37a4e4dbd8), [744737b52a8a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/744737b52a8ab45f1aa79c967e64a5586a25eb8b), [aca92b29065f](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/aca92b29065fef29b187e1303f0c5beec42f9c0f); files: `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts`, `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts`, `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts`)
- **vignesh07:** The feature history shows the Dream Diary narrative surface and core dreaming narrative files were introduced in the simplified dreaming sweep and diary surface commit. (role: Dream Diary feature introducer; confidence: medium; commits: [61e61ccc182f](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/61e61ccc182f27beaa704927c0255693ce43581f); files: `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts`, `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts`, `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts`)
- **steipete:** Contributed queue-drain test coverage in the detached narrative limiter PR and has recent release-history touches around timeout budget expectations in the same memory-core narrative files. (role: recent area contributor and reviewer-adjacent committer; confidence: medium; commits: [67e3cc8511e0](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/67e3cc8511e03df0564ec7aa4aac9c0a9d17ddae), [a185ca283a74](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a185ca283a74092d3840d0c81c53cf02e25024e8); files: `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts`, `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts`)

input.title: [Bug]: LM Studio Provider Fails: Environment Variable Expansion + API Endpoint Mismatch
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #80495: [Bug]: LM Studio Provider Fails: Environment Variable Expansion + API Endpoint Mismatch
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 80495
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80495
- Title: [Bug]: LM Studio Provider Fails: Environment Variable Expansion + API Endpoint Mismatch
- State: OPEN
- Author: jpereze12
- Labels: bug, bug:behavior

Body:
```markdown
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

OpenClaw's LM Studio provider fails due to two critical issues:

Environment variables ($LMSTUDIO_API_KEY) not expanded in API calls
API endpoint incompatibility (uses /v1/chat/completions but LM Studio's OpenAI-compatible API returns HTTP 200 with errors)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure LM Studio provider in openclaw.json with apiKey: "$LMSTUDIO_API_KEY"
2. Set environment variable LMSTUDIO_API_KEY with valid token
3. Attempt to use any LM Studio model
4. Observe 401 error with literal string $LMSTUDIO_API_KEY

### Expected behavior

- Environment variables should be expanded before sending API requests
- Should detect and use LM Studio's working endpoints (/api/v1/chat for native API or /v1/completions for OpenAI-compatible)

### Actual behavior

- $LMSTUDIO_API_KEY sent literally, causing 401 errors
- Attempts to use /v1/chat/completions which LM Studio returns HTTP 200 with error: "Unexpected endpoint or method

### OpenClaw version

2026.5.7

### Operating system

Linux (containerized)

### Install method

docker

### Model

google/gemma-4-26b-a4b

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw -> LM Studio

### Additional provider/model setup details

Additional Context:

- LM Studio has functional native API at /api/v1/chat (different format)
- LM Studio's OpenAI-compatible API at /v1/chat/completions is buggy (returns 200 with errors)
- LM Studio's old OpenAI completions API at /v1/completions works but uses deprecated format
- Other providers (OpenRouter, Gemini) properly expand environment variables

Proposed Solutions:

- Fix environment variable expansion in LM Studio provider
- Add support for LM Studio's native API (/api/v1/chat)
- Implement endpoint fallback logic for LM Studio's inconsistent API


### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

- Priority: High - prevents users from using local LM Studio inference with OpenClaw

### Additional information

Related Links:

- https://docs.openclaw.ai/ (OpenClaw documentation)
- https://lmstudio.ai/docs/developer/core/authentication (LM Studio API docs)
```

Comments/context:

GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw

- Number: 77992
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/77992
- Title: [Bug] Context display shows ?/131k with llama.cpp after upgrading to 2026.5.4 — field name mismatch not resolved
- State: OPEN
- Author: MarTT79
- Labels: bug, regression

Body:
```markdown
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

After upgrading from OpenClaw 2026.2.26 to 2026.5.4, the context display shows '?/131k' instead of actual token usage when using llama.cpp as the model provider. OpenClaw expects 'input_tokens' and 'output_tokens' fields but llama.cpp returns 'prompt_tokens' and 'completion_tokens'.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run OpenClaw 2026.5.4 with llama.cpp server as model backend (running locally on port 8080)
2. Send a message through the Telegram channel
3. Check the session status display - context shows '?/131k' instead of actual token count
4. Verify the llama.cpp server returns usage with 'prompt_tokens' and 'completion_tokens' fields (OpenAI-compatible format)

### Expected behavior

In OpenClaw 2026.2.26, the context display showed actual token usage (e.g., '45/131k'). The system should correctly parse llama.cpp's 'prompt_tokens' and 'completion_tokens' fields and display the real-time token usage rate.

### Actual behavior

Context display shows '?/131k' (question mark instead of actual token count). OpenClaw fails to find the expected 'input_tokens' and 'output_tokens' fields because llama.cpp returns 'prompt_tokens' and 'completion_tokens' instead. This is the same issue reported in #53448 but still unfixed in 2026.5.4.

### OpenClaw version

2026.5.4

### Operating system

Linux Mint 22.1 (based on Ubuntu 24.04) - Linux 6.14.0-37-generic (x64)

### Install method

_No response_

### Model

llamacpp/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD-IQ3_XXS.gguf

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw -> llamacpp (local llama-server on http://127.0.0.1:8080)

### Additional provider/model setup details

llama.cpp server running locally on port 8080 with OpenAI-compatible API format. Model: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD-IQ3_XXS.gguf (131k context window). Configured in openclaw.json under models.providers.llamacpp.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Related issue: #53448 (reported March 24, 2026, still unfixed in 2026.5.4)

llama.cpp server returns usage in OpenAI-compatible format:
{
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 11,
    "completion_tokens": 1,
    "total_tokens": 12
  }
}

OpenClaw expects 'input_tokens' and 'output_tokens' which don't exist in llama.cpp's response.
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: All self-hosted OpenClaw users running llama.cpp or Ollama as local model provider
Severity: High - prevents accurate context monitoring and may cause context overflow without warning
Frequency: Always (100% of sessions with llama.cpp)
Consequence: LCM auto-compression may not trigger, context window can overflow silently, user cannot monitor token usage

### Additional information

Last known good version: 2026.2.26
First known bad version: 2026.5.4

This is a regression that broke context tracking for llama.cpp users. The fix suggested in #53448 is straightforward - add fallback field name support:

input: response.usage?.prompt_tokens ?? response.usage?.input_tokens ?? 0,
output: response.usage?.completion_tokens ?? response.usage?.output_tokens ?? 0,
```

Body:
```markdown
## Summary
- `toCliUsage()` in `cli-output.ts` only recognized `input_tokens`/`output_tokens` (and camelCase aliases) from CLI runner output. llama.cpp and other OpenAI-compatible local providers return `prompt_tokens`/`completion_tokens` instead, which are the standard OpenAI field names.
- Without the fallback, usage was silently dropped and context display showed `?/131k` for all llama.cpp, Ollama, and similar OpenAI-compatible users.
- Fix: add `prompt_tokens` → fallback for `totalInput` and `completion_tokens` → fallback for `output` in `toCliUsage()`. Both `parseCliJson` and `parseCliJsonl` route through this function, so all CLI output parsing paths are covered.

Closes #77992

## Testing
- pnpm vitest run src/agents/cli-output.test.ts

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior: Context display shows `?/131k` with llama.cpp after upgrading to 2026.5.4 — field name mismatch causes usage to be silently dropped
- Tested via targeted unit test added in this PR that exercises the exact llama.cpp response shape (`prompt_tokens`, `completion_tokens`, `total_tokens`).
- What was not tested: live runtime — please apply maintainer `proof:` override or advise on evidence format.
```

input.title: fix: LM Studio thinking blocks invisible with Responses API
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #77827: fix: LM Studio thinking blocks invisible with Responses API
input.github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 77827 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77827 - Title: fix: LM Studio thinking blocks invisible with Responses API - State: CLOSED - Author: yaanfpv - Labels: agents, size: S, proof: supplied, proof: sufficient Body: ```markdown ## Problem When using LM Studio with the `openai-responses` API, the model's reasoning/thinking content was completely invisible, even though the model was actively thinking and producing reasoning tokens. The root cause: LM Studio (and other OpenAI-compatible providers) deliver reasoning content via a single `response.reasoning_text.done` event carrying the full thinking text. OpenClaw's `processResponsesStream` had no handler for this event, so it was silently dropped on every request. The standard OpenAI sequence OpenClaw expected: 1. `response.output_item.added` → `{ type: "reasoning" }` 2. `response.reasoning_summary_text.delta` (incremental chunks) 3. `response.output_item.done` What LM Studio actually sends: 1. `response.reasoning_text.done` → full thinking text in one shot No handler = no thinking block = thinking silently lost every time. ## Fix Adds a handler for `response.reasoning_text.done` in `processResponsesStream` (`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`): - Reads the full reasoning text from `event.text` - Creates a `thinking` content block and inserts it at the **front** of `output.content` (before any text blocks, preserving correct think-before-respond ordering) - Fires the complete `thinking_start` -> `thinking_delta` -> `thinking_end` event sequence so all downstream consumers receive the thinking content correctly Two paths are handled: 1. **Standard path** (provider sent `output_item.added(reasoning)` first): reuses the active reasoning block and fires only `thinking_end` to avoid duplicates 2. **LM Studio path** (no prior reasoning item): inserts a new thinking block at the front, but only when no thinking block and no text blocks exist yet ## Why This Is Correct `response.reasoning_text.done` is not a non-standard event. It is part of the [official OpenAI Responses API spec](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/responses/streaming) (`ResponseReasoningTextDoneEvent`). This is a gap in spec coverage that affects any provider delivering reasoning this way. ## Real Behavior Proof **Issue addressed:** Reasoning/thinking content from LM Studio was completely invisible when using the `openai-responses` API. No thinking block was rendered even though the model was actively reasoning. **Real environment tested:** macOS, LM Studio running `ykimport/qwen3.5-9b-opus-openclaw-distilled` (Qwen3.5 reasoning model), OpenClaw gateway local mode, `openai-responses` API. **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 1. Applied the fix to the installed OpenClaw runtime 2. Started OpenClaw gateway in local mode 3. Sent "what is 2+2?" to the agent 4. Observed the response in the UI **Evidence after fix:** Full reasoning block rendered above the answer, correctly displayed in the dashed thinking container. Before (no thinking block): After (thinking block visible): **Observed result after fix:** Thinking block appears correctly with full reasoning content visible. The model's reasoning text ("The user wants to solve a simple arithmetic problem. Since runtime=agent, I can use the agent's built-in reasoning... I'll compute directly: 2 + 2 = 4.") is displayed in the thinking container above the final answer. **What was not tested:** Providers that send both `output_item.added(reasoning)` and `reasoning_text.done` in the same stream (only LM Studio's done-only path was live-tested). ## Notes The same fix has been submitted to `badlogic/pi-mono` for `@mariozechner/pi-ai`'s `processResponsesStream` in `packages/ai/src/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts`, which covers the LM Studio extension's `streamSimple` path: badlogic/pi-mono#4191 ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - clawsweeper at 2026-05-05T12:03:41Z: Codex review: needs changes before merge. **Summary** The PR adds `response.reasoning_text.done` handling to the OpenAI Responses stream parser plus focused regression tests for LM Studio done-only reasoning text. Reproducibility: yes. Current main has no `response.reasoning_text.done` branch in `processResponsesStream`, the upstream event is documented in OpenAI types, and the PR supplies inspected before/after LM Studio screenshots showing the missing and restored thinking block. **Real behavior proof** Sufficient (screenshot): The PR body includes inspected before/after screenshots from a real macOS OpenClaw gateway plus LM Studio setup showing the thinking block rendered after the patch. **Next step before merge** A narrow automated repair can preserve reasoning item metadata, update the focused regression test, and refresh the branch without a product decision. **Security** Cleared: The diff only changes in-process stream-event parsing and focused tests; it does not touch dependencies, CI, secrets, package resolution, or install/build scripts. **Review findings** - [P2] Preserve reasoning state until item completion — `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:507`
Review details Best possible solution: Merge a refreshed parser fix that supports LM Studio done-only reasoning text while preserving replayable reasoning-item metadata and avoiding duplicate thinking events for standard Responses streams. Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes. Current main has no `response.reasoning_text.done` branch in `processResponsesStream`, the upstream event is documented in OpenAI types, and the PR supplies inspected before/after LM Studio screenshots showing the missing and restored thinking block. Is this the best way to solve the issue? No, not yet. The direction is right, but the standard path should keep or transfer reasoning item state through `response.output_item.done` so `thinkingSignature` remains available for replay. Full review comments: - [P2] Preserve reasoning state until item completion — `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:507` When a standard Responses stream sends `output_item.added(reasoning)`, then `reasoning_text.done`, then `output_item.done`, setting `currentBlock = null` here prevents the later item completion from attaching `thinkingSignature`. `convertResponsesMessages` only replays reasoning items from that signature, so subsequent turns can lose provider-owned reasoning metadata. Confidence: 0.92 Overall correctness: patch is incorrect Overall confidence: 0.9 Acceptance criteria: - node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts - pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts - node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "pnpm check:changed" What I checked: - **Current main drops done-only reasoning text:** `processResponsesStream` on current main handles `response.reasoning_summary_text.delta` and then `response.output_item.done`, but there is no `response.reasoning_text.done` branch in this parser path, so a done-only event has no content handler. ([`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:784`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/0ad3d25fb7cd/src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts#L784), [0ad3d25fb7cd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/0ad3d25fb7cd)) - **Reasoning replay depends on thinkingSignature:** `convertResponsesMessages` only replays Responses reasoning items when a thinking block has `thinkingSignature`, so dropping that metadata affects later turns. ([`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:520`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/0ad3d25fb7cd/src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts#L520), [0ad3d25fb7cd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/0ad3d25fb7cd)) - **Current main attaches reasoning metadata at output_item.done:** The current Responses parser writes `currentBlock.thinkingSignature = JSON.stringify(item)` only in the reasoning `response.output_item.done` branch, which requires the active thinking block to still be available at item completion. ([`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:817`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/0ad3d25fb7cd/src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts#L817), [0ad3d25fb7cd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/0ad3d25fb7cd)) - **PR head still clears standard-path state too early:** The PR's standard `response.reasoning_text.done` path emits `thinking_end` and sets `currentBlock = null`, so a later reasoning `response.output_item.done` cannot attach `thinkingSignature`. ([`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:507`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/f7dce136cee3/src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts#L507), [f7dce136cee3](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/f7dce136cee3)) - **Regression coverage misses item completion metadata:** The PR's standard-path regression test exercises `output_item.added(reasoning)` followed by `reasoning_text.done`, but it completes the response without a later reasoning `response.output_item.done` carrying replayable item metadata. ([`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts:4495`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/f7dce136cee3/src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts#L4495), [f7dce136cee3](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/f7dce136cee3)) - **Dependency contract includes the event:** The upstream OpenAI Node types define `ResponseReasoningTextDoneEvent` with `content_index`, `item_id`, `output_index`, `sequence_number`, `text`, and type `response.reasoning_text.done`, so this is a real Responses stream event to support. Likely related people: - **steipete:** Recent GitHub path history shows repeated work in `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` and its tests, including provider reasoning replay fixes and OpenAI-compatible stream behavior. (role: recent area contributor; confidence: high; commits: [d5eec80e3403](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d5eec80e3403ee11456b7a7f243a5162b8dae18d), [3e5070efbfc4](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3e5070efbfc4c0ebc46f98e0ebea35c1f2542322), [99dfb8291f1c](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/99dfb8291f1cd6f201e10144a16c3372f3467b8f); files: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`, `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`) - **vincentkoc:** Recent path history includes a Responses usage fix in the same stream transport file, adjacent to this parser behavior. (role: recent area contributor; confidence: medium; commits: [f964b1c3aa11](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/f964b1c3aa1103210b0eb52623052dcac841dcbf); files: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`, `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`) - **rugvedS07:** Commit `0cfb83edfae9` introduced the LM Studio integration and related docs/tests, which is the provider surface affected by this report. (role: LM Studio feature contributor; confidence: medium; commits: [0cfb83edfae9](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/0cfb83edfae95b3f8c683c8e44c0f92ac23642a1); files: `extensions/lmstudio/src/stream.ts`, `extensions/lmstudio/src/models.ts`, `docs/providers/lmstudio.md`) - **indulgeback:** Commit `c91fffdd67d6` added Qwen thinking-format support in nearby OpenAI-compatible reasoning handling and tests. (role: adjacent reasoning-format contributor; confidence: medium; commits: [c91fffdd67d6](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c91fffdd67d6458b200a622b91f59c7d34981785); files: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`, `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`) Remaining risk / open question: - The PR branch currently reports `DIRTY` against current main, so it needs a refresh before merge. - The standard Responses stream path can still lose replayable reasoning metadata unless the active reasoning item is preserved through `response.output_item.done` or equivalent metadata is attached before clearing state. Codex review notes: model gpt-5.5, reasoning high; reviewed against [0ad3d25fb7cd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/0ad3d25fb7cd8597e92f1f1fc5c2bbe1b2c76cf4).
- yaanfpv at 2026-05-05T12:19:09Z: Good catch. You're right that a provider sending both sequences would produce a duplicate thinking block. The handler is intentionally designed for providers like LM Studio that use *exclusively* `response.reasoning_text.done` and never send `response.output_item.added` with `type: "reasoning"` — so in practice the two paths don't overlap. That said, it's worth guarding against just in case. I can add a check so the handler only fires when no thinking block already exists in `o [truncated] ``` Changed files (2): ```text src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts ```

Comments/context:
```markdown
- clawsweeper at 2026-04-30T21:49:39Z:
Codex review: needs real behavior proof before merge.

**Summary**
The PR changes the Ollama stream path to recompute the native chat URL and SSRF policy per request from `model.baseUrl`, wraps configured streams to stamp an effective base URL, adds stream-runtime tests, and edits the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: configure two Ollama-compatible providers sharing `api: "ollama"`, let the first register the shared stream, then invoke it with a later runtime model carrying a different `baseUrl` and headers.

**Real behavior proof**
Needs real behavior proof before merge: The PR body and comments do not contain after-fix proof from a real multi-provider Ollama setup. After adding proof, update the PR body; ClawSweeper should re-review automatically. If it does not, ask a maintainer to comment `@clawsweeper re-review`.

**Next step before merge**
The contributor needs to fix the credential-routing bug and provide redacted real multi-provider proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's external setup proof for this PR.

**Security**
Needs attention: The patch introduces a concrete cross-provider credential-routing risk by decoupling per-request URLs from per-request headers.

**Review findings**
- [P1] Route headers with the per-request URL — `extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts:1029-1030`

<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Resolve the Ollama request URL, SSRF policy, and default headers from the same per-request runtime provider data, then cover the URL/header pairing for shared-API multi-provider setups.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: configure two Ollama-compatible providers sharing `api: "ollama"`, let the first register the shared stream, then invoke it with a later runtime model carrying a different `baseUrl` and headers.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

No. Per-request URL and SSRF recomputation is the right direction, but this patch must route headers from the same runtime model/provider data before it is safe to merge.

Full review comments:

- [P1] Route headers with the per-request URL — `extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts:1029-1030`
  When a later Ollama-compatible provider enters the first registered stream, this line switches `chatUrl` to the runtime `model.baseUrl` while the request still merges `defaultHeaders` captured from the first provider. That can send provider A's Authorization or custom headers to provider B's URL, so resolve the runtime headers from the same provider data and add coverage for the cross-provider URL/header pair.
  Confidence: 0.9

Overall correctness: patch is incorrect
Overall confidence: 0.91

Security concerns:

- [high] Potential cross-provider Authorization header leak — `extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts:1029`
  A request can be sent to a later provider's runtime `model.baseUrl` while still merging the first registered provider's `defaultHeaders`, including Authorization or other custom credentials.
  Confidence: 0.88

Acceptance criteria:

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts
- Redacted real multi-provider Ollama run showing that URL and headers route to the same provider after the patch

What I checked:

- **PR head decouples URL from captured headers:** At the PR head, `createOllamaStreamFn` can derive `chatUrl` and SSRF policy from runtime `model.baseUrl`, but the later request headers still merge `defaultHeaders` captured when the first stream function was created. ([`extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts:1029`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/8729ad261b05/extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts#L1029), [8729ad261b05](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/8729ad261b05))
- **Current main custom API registration is first-write-wins:** `ensureCustomApiRegistered` returns false when an API provider is already registered, so providers sharing `api: "ollama"` can reuse the first registered stream function. ([`src/agents/custom-api-registry.ts:15`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/532759e1ab2e/src/agents/custom-api-registry.ts#L15), [532759e1ab2e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/532759e1ab2e))
- **Runtime model carries both per-provider route and headers:** Model resolution puts the resolved provider request `baseUrl` and `headers` onto the runtime model, which is the data the fix needs to keep paired. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.ts:629`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/532759e1ab2e/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.ts#L629), [532759e1ab2e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/532759e1ab2e))
- **Ollama plugin creates configured stream from provider config:** The Ollama plugin passes the selected provider model and provider base URL into `createConfiguredOllamaStreamFn`, so the affected path is the native Ollama stream path. ([`extensions/ollama/index.ts:237`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/532759e1ab2e/extensions/ollama/index.ts#L237), [532759e1ab2e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/532759e1ab2e))
- **Live PR proof remains absent:** The live PR body is `test`, the only substantive comment is the prior ClawSweeper review, and there is no after-fix terminal output, log, screenshot, recording, or linked artifact from a real multi-provider Ollama setup. ([8729ad261b05](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/8729ad261b05))
- **Prior related routing fix context:** Commit 045d956111ea fixed an earlier per-provider Ollama baseUrl bug and closed https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/61678, but current source still leaves the shared-api first-registered stream function behavior that this PR targets. ([`extensions/ollama/index.ts:237`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/045d956111ea/extensions/ollama/index.ts#L237), [045d956111ea](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/045d956111ea))

Likely related people:

- **@steipete:** Recent current-main history and blame touch the Ollama stream/test surface and adjacent provider runtime files, including native Ollama request behavior near this path. (role: recent area contributor; confidence: high; commits: [5aefc9dda47b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/5aefc9dda47b412628b71c6eca4e3ad9b5add854), [a8c745a6230e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a8c745a6230e1bce61412007c6b21fc48cddb411), [cce9a9ef2abd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cce9a9ef2abdc55be6ef9b649c5436aa4252ef1c); files: `extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts`, `extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.ts`)
- **@vincentkoc:** Commit 7e946b3c6c25 introduced the custom API registration path used by Ollama native streams, including the first-write-wins behavior central to this routing issue. (role: introduced adjacent registry seam; confidence: high; commits: [7e946b3c6c25](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7e946b3c6c25ad26cece0a855b3503aabf055c0e); files: `src/agents/custom-api-registry.ts`, `src/agents/provider-stream.ts`, `extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts`)
- **@zozo123:** Commit 045d956111ea fixed an earlier per-provider Ollama baseUrl routing bug in the same plugin stream/createStreamFn surface. (role: prior related fix author; confidence: medium; commits: [045d956111ea](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/045d956111eadd25da8fade5766fbab9a50eb137); files: `extensions/ollama/index.ts`, `extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts`)

Remaining risk / open question:

- Title: [Feature]: bundled openai-compatible embedding provider for self-hosted servers (llama.cpp, Ollama, vLLM, TGI, LocalAI)
- State: OPEN
- Author: yaanfpv
- Labels: (none)

Body:
```markdown
<!-- Choose the "Feature request" template if you want the form fields. Otherwise paste this whole body and submit. -->

## Summary

Add a bundled memory embedding provider adapter named `openai-compatible` that targets any local OpenAI-compatible HTTP embedding server (llama.cpp's `llama-server`, Ollama via its `/v1` surface, vLLM, TGI, LocalAI, llamafile, or any reverse-proxied internal instance), without any vendor-specific warmup probe and without inheriting from any global `models.providers.*` config.

## Problem to solve

Operators running a self-hosted OpenAI-compatible embeddings server today have two unsatisfying choices, both of which produce real operator pain.

1. **Point the bundled `lmstudio` adapter at the local server.** The /v1/embeddings call works fine, but the adapter's `ensureLmstudioModelLoaded` warmup calls an LMStudio-only "load model" endpoint that hangs against generic servers. On my machine running llama.cpp's `llama-server` with BGE-M3 on localhost:8081, this hang blocks the gateway event loop for ~30 seconds per memory-lancedb embedding-provider rebuild. The gateway's own liveness diagnostic reports it as `event_loop_delay = 29,091 ms`, and queued sessions.list / config.get / cron.list responses balloon to 40-60 second response times during the freeze. The gateway log floods with `lmstudio embeddings warmup failed; continuing without preload` warnings with no operator-friendly indication that the actual cause is a vendor-specific preload endpoint mismatched against a perfectly good local server.

2. **Point the bundled `openai` adapter at the local server.** This works (the per-plugin `embedding.baseUrl` overrides the global `models.providers.openai.baseUrl`, and the openai adapter has no warmup), but it inherits the global openai config block's headers, attribution, and api-key resolution. If the per-plugin `embedding.baseUrl` line ever gets removed by mistake during a config edit, embedding requests silently fall back to api.openai.com, leaking embedded text to a cloud provider the operator may not have intended for memory.

Neither option says what it is on the tin. Operators searching for "how do I use my local embedding server with openclaw" end up confused, sometimes filing followup issues like #72875 (`Unknown memory embedding provider: local`) thinking the existing `local` adapter is what they want, when in fact the existing `local` is for in-process node-llama-cpp on a `.gguf` file and not for HTTP-based local servers.

## Proposed solution

Add a new bundled extension `extensions/openai-compatible-embeddings/` that registers an `openai-compatible` memory embedding provider adapter.

Design:

- Provider id: `openai-compatible`. Matches the term llama.cpp, Ollama, vLLM, TGI, LocalAI, and llamafile all use to describe their HTTP API.
- `transport: "remote"`. Routed through the same SSRF + remote-fetch path as the cloud adapters.
- No `autoSelectPriority`. Operator must opt in explicitly via `embedding.provider: "openai-compatible"`. We do not want auto-selection, because every operator with another adapter's credentials configured would otherwise route embeddings to the cloud the moment they enabled memory-lancedb.
- No `authProviderId`. There is no centralized auth flow for arbitrary local servers; the optional `apiKey` lives directly in the per-plugin `embedding` config block.
- No warmup, preload, or model-load probe. The first /v1/embeddings call loads the model lazily, which every server in this family already does.
- Reads only from the per-plugin `embedding` config block. Does not consult any global `models.providers.*` block. Cannot accidentally route to a vendor cloud.
- Fails-fast with a clear error message when `embedding.baseUrl` or `embedding.model` is missing.

Config:

```json5
{
  plugins: {
    entries: {
      "memory-lancedb": {
        enabled: true,
        config: {
          embedding: {
            provider: "openai-compatible",
            baseUrl: "http://localhost:8081/v1",
            model: "text-embedding-bge-m3",
            apiKey: "${LLAMA_API_TOKEN}",
            dimensions: 1024,
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

Distinct from the existing in-process `local` adapter (`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/provider-adapters.ts`), which loads a `.gguf` file via node-llama-cpp inside the gateway process. See "Alternatives considered" below for the full breakdown of why the two are complementary rather than redundant.

## Alternatives considered

Considered four other approaches.

1. **Use the existing `local` adapter (in-process node-llama-cpp).** The natural first question. The existing `local` adapter loads a `.gguf` file directly into the gateway Node process via node-llama-cpp; my proposed adapter talks HTTP to a separately-running server. They are not interchangeable.

   | | Existing `local` | Proposed `openai-compatible` |
   |---|---|---|
   | Where the model lives | inside the gateway process | separate HTTP server |
   | Wire | in-process Node bindings | HTTP /v1/embeddings |
   | Reload model | gateway restart | server restart only |
   | Share with other clients | no, gateway owns the model | yes, any HTTP client |
   | GPU tuning surface | node-llama-cpp options | the server's own CLI flags (e.g. `llama-server -ngl ...`) |
   | Works with Ollama / vLLM / TGI / LocalAI / llamafile | no (not Node libs) | yes (they all speak OpenAI /v1) |
   | Operator's existing tuned setup | must be ported to node-llama-cpp options | unchanged |

   Operators running a separately-managed embedding server (which is the common shape on Apple Silicon, on machines with a dedicated GPU, or on shared infrastructure) cannot use the existing `local` adapter without abandoning their existing tuned setup. And operators on Ollama / vLLM / TGI / LocalAI / llamafile cannot use it at all because those projects are not Node libraries. Both adapters stay supported; they target different deployment shapes.

2. **Fix the `lmstudio` adapter so its warmup gracefully no-ops against non-LMStudio servers.** Doable, but the operator is still using `provider: "lmstudio"` against an Ollama or llama.cpp server, which is semantically misleading and easy to mis-document. The fix lands the same wire behavior under a wrong name.

3. **Document the existing workaround harder** (set `provider: "openai"` plus `embedding.baseUrl`). Today the docs already mention this. The trap is silent: if the per-plugin `baseUrl` is removed during a config edit, traffic silently goes to api.openai.com. A safer adapter that fails-fast on missing baseUrl is preferable to documentation that depends on operator vigilance.

4. **Run a small reverse-proxy in front of the local server** that stubs the LMStudio-specific endpoints and forwards /v1/embeddings. Adds infra to a memory plugin, doesn't generalize across deployments, and still leaves the misleading `provider: "lmstudio"` in operator config.

The proposed bundled adapter is the simplest path that solves all the failure modes above: explicit name that matches what the upstream projects call themselves, no warmup, no global config inheritance, and complementary to the existing in-process `local` adapter without redundancy.

## Impact

- **Affected**: any operator running a self-hosted OpenAI-compatible embeddings server for memory-lancedb. The local-embeddings server ecosystem includes llama.cpp's `llama-server`, Ollama (via its /v1 surface), vLLM, TGI, LocalAI, and llamafile, all of which are popular alternatives to cloud embeddings for privacy, cost, or offline reasons. The user base overlaps heavily with operators of self-hosted openclaw stacks.
- **Severity**: high for operators in the trap. The lmstudio-warmup-against-non-LMStudio path actively stalls the gateway for ~30 seconds per memory-lancedb embedding-provider rebuild, which fires roughly every 24-30 minutes of channel activity. The dashboard goes unresponsive during the freeze, queued WebSocket calls back up, and operators spend hours diagnosing what is actually a missing-adapter UX gap. The openai-with-baseUrl-override path is medium severity: works correctly until a config edit accidentally removes the override.
- **Frequency**: every memory-lancedb embedding-provider rebuild for affected operators. On my machine (single openclaw instance, two channels, normal usage), the rebuild fires several times per hour.
- **Consequence**: silent gateway stalls (lmstudio path), or silent leak of embedded chat content to a cloud provider (openai path). Both are operator-trust-eroding outcomes. The proposed adapter eliminates both.

## Evidence / examples

Live evidence from my machine running this exact setup (llama.cpp `llama-server` serving `bge-m3-Q8_0.gguf` on `http://localhost:8081/v1`).

**Before** (with `provider: "lmstudio"` and the same baseUrl), gateway log during a single memory-lancedb embedding-provider rebuild:

```
2026-05-11T05:05:50  ‚áÑ res ‚úì sessions.list 22416ms
2026-05-11T05:05:5

[truncated]
```

Body:
```markdown
## Bug Description

When configuring a custom OpenAI-compatible API provider (e.g., `spanagent.xyz`), if the `baseUrl` is set to `https://spanagent.xyz` (without `/v1` suffix), OpenClaw constructs the request URL as `https://spanagent.xyz/chat/completions`. This endpoint returns an HTML page (200 OK, Content-Type: text/html), which the streaming handler cannot parse. The error surfaced to the user is cryptic:

```
FailoverError: spanagent/deepseek-v4-flash ended with an incomplete terminal response
```

Meanwhile, the log shows `contentType=text/html; charset=utf-8` but no clear indication that the URL path is wrong.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Add a custom provider via `openclaw onboard` or manual config:
   ```json
   {
     "baseUrl": "https://spanagent.xyz",
     "api": "openai-completions",
     "models": [{ "id": "deepseek-v4-flash", "reasoning": true, ... }]
   }
   ```
2. Run `openclaw agent --agent <agent> --message "hi" --model <provider>/deepseek-v4-flash --local`
3. Observe: request goes to `https://spanagent.xyz/chat/completions` (no `/v1` prefix)
4. API returns HTML page with 200 OK
5. Error: `incomplete terminal response` — no mention of wrong URL

## Expected Behavior

1. The onboard wizard or docs should guide users to use `https://spanagent.xyz/v1` as the base URL (OpenAI SDK appends `/chat/completions` to the base URL)
2. When the provider returns non-JSON/non-SSE content (e.g., HTML), the error should be descriptive: "API endpoint returned HTML instead of a valid response — check baseUrl (should typically end with /v1 for OpenAI-compatible APIs)"

## Actual Behavior

Silent failure with misleading `incomplete terminal response` message. Requires log inspection to find `contentType=text/html` and discover the wrong URL path.

## Environment

- OpenClaw version: 2026.5.19-beta.1 (167e73c)
- Deployment: Docker container (npm global install)
- Provider: Custom OpenAI-compatible API (spanagent.xyz)

## Additional Context

The OpenAI JavaScript SDK (used internally by pi-ai/pi-agent-core) constructs the endpoint URL as `${baseUrl}/chat/completions`. This means for any provider serving under a path prefix (like `/v1/`), the baseUrl MUST include that prefix.

For example:
- ❌ `https://spanagent.xyz` → request goes to `https://spanagent.xyz/chat/completions` (wrong)
- ✅ `https://spanagent.xyz/v1` → request goes to `https://spanagent.xyz/v1/chat/completions` (correct)

A secondary issue was also encountered: the default `maxTokens` of 384000 for deepseek models exceeded the providers limit (65536), causing HTTP 400. The error message for this was clear (`400 field MaxTokens invalid, should be in [1, 65536]`), so that part is fine — just noting the config guidance gap.
```

([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/result-fallback-classifier.ts:97`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/9e4eca00ff0f/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/result-fallback-classifier.ts#L97), [9e4eca00ff0f](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/9e4eca00ff0f))

Public docs: [`docs/gateway/config-tools.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/config-tools). ([`docs/gateway/config-tools.md:423`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/config-tools), [9e4eca00ff0f](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/9e4eca00ff0f))
- **Dependency contract proof:** OpenAI Node SDK v6.38.0 Chat Completions `create` posts to `/chat/completions`, so OpenClaw must provide a base URL that already includes any provider path prefix such as `/v1`.

This PR fixes token usage being reported as 0 for Alibaba Bailian and other OpenAI-compatible providers by replacing the `??`-chain field resolution in `normalizeUsage` (`usage.ts`) and `toUsage` (`cli-runner/helpers.ts`) with a two-pass `pick` helper that prefers the first positive value over any zero-valued field, then falls back to the first finite number if no positive value exists. **Key changes:** - Both files now share the same two-pass "prefer positive, fallback to first finite" strategy. Previously, `??` would stop at a zero value (e.g., `input_tokens: 0`), preventing a later positive value (`prompt_tokens: N`) from being used. - `helpers.ts` gains `prompt_tokens`/`promptTokens` for `input` and `completion_tokens`/`completionTokens` for `output`, making the CLI runner consistent with the main `usage.ts` field coverage. - `helpers.ts` also gains `cached_tokens` for `cacheRead`, matching `usage.ts`. **Correctness assessment:** The two-pass logic is sound across all edge cases: providers returning a single non-zero value behave identically to before; the new behavior only differs when at least one candidate field is 0 while another is positive. The existing negative-input clamp (`rawInput < 0 ? 0 : rawInput`) continues to function correctly in combination with the new `pick`. No unit tests were added covering the specific `input_tokens: 0, prompt_tokens: N` scenario; adding regression tests would be valuable to guard this fix going forward.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Safe to merge; the fix is logically correct and targeted, with no regressions for well-behaved providers. - The two-pass `pick` logic correctly resolves the reported zero-field bug and is consistent across both changed files. No critical logic errors or regressions were found. Score is 4 rather than 5 only because no regression tests were added for the specific `input_tokens: 0 / prompt_tokens: N` scenario that triggered the fix, leaving coverage of this edge case entirely to manual verification. - No files require special attention; both changes are self-contained and low-risk. input.title: fix(agents): assistant message content null instead of empty string breaks OpenAI-compatible providers
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #44086: fix(agents): assistant message content null instead of empty string breaks OpenAI-compatible providers
input.github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 44086 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/44086 - Title: fix(agents): assistant message content null instead of empty string breaks OpenAI-compatible providers - State: OPEN - Author: xinhuagu - Labels: agents, size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Problem When an OpenAI-compatible provider (e.g. DeepSeek v3.1) returns an assistant message with `content: ""` and `tool_calls`, the empty text block is filtered out during message conversion in `convertMessages`, causing the content to default to `null`. Providers that require string content then reject the request with: ``` Messages token length must be in (0, 1048576], but got 0 ``` This happens because `convertMessages` initializes assistant content as `null` (for providers without `requiresAssistantAfterToolResult`), then filters text blocks with `b.text && b.text.trim().length > 0`, which removes empty strings. The assistant message is kept (it has `tool_calls`), but with `content: null`. ## Solution Add an `onPayload` wrapper (`createAssistantNullContentFixWrapper`) that normalizes `content: null` to `""` on assistant messages carrying `tool_calls` for `openai-completions` API payloads. This is safe because: - OpenAI and most providers accept both `null` and `""` for assistant content - Providers like DeepSeek require string content, not null - The wrapper only activates for `openai-completions` API (not Responses API) - Existing string content is never modified ## Test plan 4 test cases covering: - Null content normalized to empty string when tool_calls present - Null content preserved when no tool_calls (no unnecessary modification) - Non-openai-completions APIs unaffected - Existing string content preserved Fixes #43716 ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-12T13:56:12Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a compatibility issue with OpenAI-compatible providers (e.g. DeepSeek) that reject `content: null` on assistant messages that carry `tool_calls`. The root cause is that `convertMessages` in `pi-ai` filters out empty text blocks and leaves `content` as `null` when no text content survives, which some stricter providers reject with a token-length error. The fix introduces a lightweight `onPayload` wrapper (`createAssistantNullContentFixWrapper`) that normalizes `null` → `""` for these messages at the last moment before the HTTP request is made, scoped exclusively to `openai-completions` payloads. The approach is consistent with the established wrapper pattern already used for service-tier injection and Responses API context management. Key points: - The wrapper only activates for `api === "openai-completions"`, leaving Responses API payloads and other providers untouched. - Only assistant messages that simultaneously have `content == null` **and** non-empty `tool_calls` are modified; plain assistant messages without tool calls are left as-is. - Existing non-null string content is never overwritten. - Four unit tests cover all relevant cases (normalization, no-op when no tool_calls, API-type gating, and preservation of existing content). - The wrapper is applied after the existing `createOpenAIResponsesContextManagementWrapper`, but since the two wrappers gate on mutually exclusive API types there is no ordering concern.

Confidence Score: 5/5

- This PR is safe to merge — it is a narrow, well-tested fix that only mutates payloads for a specific API type and condition. - The change is minimal and surgical: a single new wrapper function gated on `api === "openai-completions"` that only touches assistant messages with `tool_calls` and `null` content. It follows the exact same pattern as all other wrappers in the file, mutations are safe (in-place, consistent with codebase convention), and four targeted tests verify every relevant edge case. There is no risk of regression for existing providers since the Responses API and non-tool-call messages are explicitly excluded. - No files require special attention. LGTM from my side; CI looks green. - openclaw-barnacle at 2026-04-27T04:43:10Z: This pull request has been automatically marked as stale due to inactivity. Please add updates or it will be closed. - clawsweeper at 2026-04-30T21:17:08Z: Codex review: needs real behavior proof before merge. **Workflow note:** Future ClawSweeper reviews update this same comment in place.

**Summary** The PR adds an embedded Pi runner OpenAI Chat Completions payload wrapper that rewrites assistant messages with `content: null` and `tool_calls` to `content: ""`, plus focused wrapper tests. Reproducibility: yes. source-level. The installed PI converter can keep assistant tool-call messages with `content: null`, and current main's direct Chat Completions builder still forwards that shape; I did not run a live DeepSeek-compatible provider in this read-only review. **PR rating** Overall: 🧂 unranked krab Proof: 🧂 unranked krab Patch quality: 🦪 silver shellfish Summary: The fix direction is useful, but missing real-provider proof plus stale and incomplete current-main coverage make the PR not quality-ready for merge.
