input.title: [Feature]: GPT4Free (g4f) OpenAI-Compatible Backend + Fallback & Proxy Support
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #84587: [Feature]: GPT4Free (g4f) OpenAI-Compatible Backend + Fallback & Proxy Support
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 84587
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84587
- Title: [Feature]: GPT4Free (g4f) OpenAI-Compatible Backend + Fallback & Proxy Support
- State: CLOSED
- Author: opensource-elearning
- Labels: enhancement, P3, impact:auth-provider

Body:
```markdown
### Summary

Add optional GPT4Free (g4f) OpenAI-compatible backend support with built-in fallback and per-provider proxy routing for improved reliability in agent execution. https://github.com/xtekky/gpt4free

### Problem to solve

OpenClaw agents depend on external LLM backends for reasoning and task execution. In practice, this creates a single point of failure:

If a provider is rate-limited, blocked, or unstable, agent execution fails mid-workflow
Users in restricted regions cannot reliably access major APIs
There is no built-in fallback mechanism to continue execution using alternative models
Proxy routing is not supported per provider, limiting access in blocked environments

This leads to brittle agent behavior where long-running tasks fail even though alternative inference sources (like local OpenAI-compatible servers such as g4f) are available.

### Proposed solution

Introduce optional OpenAI-compatible backend support with a g4f preset, plus lightweight resilience features.

1. g4f backend preset (OpenAI-compatible)
{
  "provider": "openai_compatible",
  "api_base": "http://127.0.0.1:1337/v1",
  "model": "gpt-4o-mini",
  "api_key": "dummy"
}
2. Optional g4f mode (explicit preset)
{
  "provider": "g4f",
  "api_base": "http://127.0.0.1:1337/v1",
  "model_priority": [
    "gpt-4o-mini",
    "claude-3-haiku",
    "deepseek-chat"
  ]
}
3. Proxy support (critical for reliability)
{
  "proxy": "socks5://127.0.0.1:1080"
}
4. Lightweight fallback behavior

If a request fails:

retry once on same model
switch to next model in priority list
optionally fallback to local model

No orchestration layer required—just deterministic failover.

### Alternatives considered

Manual switching between providers in config → works but not reliable for long-running agents
Full multi-provider orchestration system → too heavy for OpenClaw’s minimal design
External proxy tools only → does not solve model-level fallback or agent continuity

### Impact

Affected users/systems:

OpenClaw agent runtime (core execution loop)
users running long autonomous workflows
users in restricted or high-latency regions
developers using local-first or low-cost setups

Severity:

Medium → High (can fully break agent runs mid-task)

Frequency:

Intermittent but common in real-world usage (rate limits, network blocks, provider outages)

Consequences:

failed or partially completed agent workflows
manual restart and re-prompting of tasks
inability to run long autonomous chains reliably
reduced usability in non-US / restricted environments

### Evidence/examples

OpenAI-compatible servers like g4f already expose /v1/chat/completions interfaces
Many OpenClaw deployments already rely on switching models manually when failures occur
Real-world agent workflows are frequently interrupted due to provider rate limits or blocking
Proxy-based access is commonly required in restricted networks

(Industry pattern: OpenAI-compatible abstraction is already standard across tools like Ollama / vLLM / LM Studio ecosystems.)

### Additional information

This proposal is intentionally minimal and aligns with OpenClaw’s philosophy:

No new orchestration framework
No dependency on external router services
Only a thin compatibility layer for OpenAI-style endpoints
Optional feature flags only

Goal is to make OpenClaw more resilient in real deployments, especially for autonomous multi-step agents where provider failure currently breaks execution entirely, want everything free
```

So I’m closing this as a scope-fit item for the plugin/community path. Please upload or publish it through [ClawHub.com](https://clawhub.ai/) so it can live as an installable community skill instead of a bundled OpenClaw core change.

<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Keep core on the existing generic provider, fallback, proxy, and plugin contracts; publish any g4f preset or installer through ClawHub, with concrete runtime defects tracked as narrower bugs.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Not applicable; this is a feature/provider-packaging request, not a bug report. The review verified current provider, fallback, proxy, and plugin surfaces instead of establishing a failing current-main reproduction path.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

No; a g4f-branded backend preset in core is not the best solution. The maintainable path is to keep generic OpenAI-compatible provider/proxy/fallback contracts in core and publish g4f-specific packaging through ClawHub.

Security review:

Security review: No patch is under review; any future g4f plugin should receive normal plugin provenance, dependency, proxy credential, and security review before promotion.

What I checked:

- **Live issue context:** Live GitHub state matches the supplied context: this is an open external enhancement request with no protected security, release-blocker, beta-blocker, or maintainer label, and the existing ClawSweeper comment already recommended ClawHub/plugin scope.
- **Core/plugin scope anchor:** VISION.md says core stays lean, optional capabilities should usually ship as plugins, bundle-style plugins are preferred when sufficient, and ClawHub owns plugin discovery, publisher status, provenance, and security review. input.title: [Bug]: Gemini 2.5 Flash via vertex-ai (OpenAI-compatible) streaming times out — thinking tokens not handled
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #84384: [Bug]: Gemini 2.5 Flash via vertex-ai (OpenAI-compatible) streaming times out — thinking tokens not handled
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 84384
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84384
- Title: [Bug]: Gemini 2.5 Flash via vertex-ai (OpenAI-compatible) streaming times out — thinking tokens not handled
- State: OPEN
- Author: teknolojay
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:needs-live-repro, impact:message-loss, issue-rating: 🐚 platinum hermit

Body:
```markdown
### Bug type

Regression / Missing coverage

### Summary

Gemini 2.5 Flash via the `vertex-ai` provider (OpenAI-compatible endpoint through a sidecar proxy) always hits the LLM idle timeout (~28s), even though the sidecar returns HTTP 200 in ~2-3 seconds. Direct non-streaming curl to the same sidecar works perfectly.

**Root cause:** Gemini 2.5 Flash always produces `reasoning_tokens` in its response, even when thinking is not explicitly requested. The OpenAI-compatible streaming SSE parser in OpenClaw cannot handle these thinking/reasoning tokens — it consumes SSE chunks containing only reasoning activity without yielding stream events, so the idle watchdog never resets and kills the connection.

**PR #76080 (merged May 2) fixed this for the native `google` transport** by ensuring `thoughtSignature`-only SSE parts refresh the idle watchdog. However, the `vertex-ai` (OpenAI-compatible) transport does not have the equivalent fix.

### Reproduction

**Setup:**
- OpenClaw 2026.5.6 (c97b9f7)
- Vertex AI sidecar proxy on `127.0.0.1:8787` (auto-refreshes SA tokens via google-auth-library)
- Service account auth (`type: service_account`), project `lkkff-ai`, region `us-central1`
- Model: `vertex-ai/google/gemini-2.5-flash`

**Non-streaming works (2s):**
```bash
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1beta1/projects/lkkff-ai/locations/us-central1/endpoints/openapi/chat/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model":"google/gemini-2.5-flash","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Say hi"}],"max_tokens":50,"stream":false}' \
  --max-time 15
# Returns: 200, "Hi there!", reasoning_tokens: 23, time: 1.8s
```

**Streaming fails (28s timeout):**
Any message through OpenClaw agent → sidecar returns 200 in ~3s → OpenClaw streaming parser hangs → `AbortError: This operation was aborted` → `LLM request timed out` at 28s.

**Gateway log:**
```
[agent/embedded] embedded run agent end: isError=true model=google/gemini-2.5-flash provider=vertex-ai error=LLM request timed out. rawError=terminated
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed reason=timeout next=none detail=terminated
```

**Sidecar log (healthy):**
```
{"msg":"request","method":"POST","path":"/v1beta1/.../chat/completions","status":200,"ms":2845}
{"msg":"stream error","error":"This operation was aborted"}  // OpenClaw killed the connection
```

### What we tried (none worked)

| Config change | Result |
|---|---|
| `thinkingDefault: "off"` in agents.defaults | /status shows Think: off, but model still returns reasoning_tokens |
| `reasoning: false` on model catalog entry | No effect — sidecar response still contains reasoning_tokens |
| `timeoutSeconds: 60` on provider | No effect — idle timeout fires, not request timeout |
| Switching to `gemini-2.0-flash` | 404 on Vertex AI OpenAI-compatible endpoint |
| `OPENCLAW_THINKING` env var | Not recognized |

### Why native google-vertex transport is unreachable

PR #76080 fixes this for the native `google` transport. However, our credentials are `type: service_account`. OpenClaw's ADC check (`hasGoogleVertexAuthorizedUserAdcSync`) only activates the native transport for `authorized_user` credentials. Service account users are forced through the OpenAI-compatible `vertex-ai` path, which lacks the fix.

### Expected behavior

The `vertex-ai` OpenAI-compatible streaming parser should handle reasoning/thinking tokens in the SSE stream the same way the native `google` transport does after PR #76080 — either by:
1. Yielding a keepalive event to refresh the idle watchdog when reasoning tokens are present
2. Or stripping/ignoring reasoning-only chunks without blocking the stream

### Environment

- **OpenClaw:** 2026.5.6 (c97b9f7)
- **OS:** Ubuntu 24.04 (NVIDIA DGX Spark)
- **Node:** 22.22.2
- **Provider:** vertex-ai via sidecar proxy (OpenAI-compatible endpoint)
- **Model:** google/gemini-2.5-flash
- **Auth:** Service account JSON (not ADC authorized_user)
- **Channel:** Telegram

### Related

- #76071 — Same symptom for native Google transport (closed, fixed by #76080)
- #76080 — Fix for native transport idle watchdog + thoughtSignature handling
- #79595 — google-vertex auth profile detection issue
```

**Workflow note:** Future ClawSweeper reviews update this same comment in place.

- Maintainers can comment `@clawsweeper explain` to ask for more context, or `@clawsweeper stop` to stop active automation.

</details>

**Summary**
Keep open: current main has the native Google thoughtSignature keepalive, but the shared OpenAI-compatible completions parser still appears to skip reasoning/usage-only chunks unless they produce content-bearing deltas, so this Vertex sidecar path is not proven fixed.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was run. Source inspection gives a medium-confidence repro path: OpenAI-compatible chunks that carry reasoning/usage activity without content do not push assistant stream events, so the idle wrapper can still time out.

**Ways to help us reproduce this**
- Add a screenshot or short recording showing the behavior.
- Add expected vs actual behavior.
- Include redacted logs or terminal output.

**Next step**
This is a narrow, source-traceable streaming parser bug with clear likely files and a focused regression-test path, but live sidecar proof is still missing.


<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Add a focused OpenAI-compatible completions streaming fix that treats Gemini/Vertex reasoning-only activity as stream activity without leaking bogus visible text, with regression coverage beside the existing reasoning stream tests.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

No high-confidence live reproduction was run. Source inspection gives a medium-confidence repro path: OpenAI-compatible chunks that carry reasoning/usage activity without content do not push assistant stream events, so the idle wrapper can still time out.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

Yes for a fix PR: the maintainable path is to handle model activity in the shared OpenAI-compatible streaming parser, not to ask users to raise timeouts or switch transports.

Label changes:

- add `P2`: This is a normal-priority provider streaming bug with a concrete but limited Vertex sidecar/Gemini blast radius.
- add `impact:message-loss`: The reported failure causes agent responses to be aborted before a Telegram-visible assistant message is delivered.
- add `issue-rating: 🐚 platinum hermit`: Current issue advisory state selects this label.
- add `clawsweeper:needs-live-repro`: Current issue advisory state selects this label.

Label justifications:

- `P2`: This is a normal-priority provider streaming bug with a concrete but limited Vertex sidecar/Gemini blast radius.
- `impact:message-loss`: The reported failure causes agent responses to be aborted before a Telegram-visible assistant message is delivered.

Acceptance criteria:

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts
- git diff --check

What I checked:

- **Reporter evidence:** The issue gives a concrete OpenClaw 2026.5.6 setup with a 127.0.0.1 Vertex sidecar, non-streaming success in about two seconds, streaming abort at the LLM idle timeout, and related native-Google fix context.
- **Native Google path handles empty thinking activity:** The native Google transport treats thoughtSignature-only parts as thinking activity and pushes a zero-length thinking_delta, which matches the prior fix pattern the issue asks to extend. ([`extensions/google/transport-stream.ts:1249`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d7896ed4c981/extensions/google/transport-stream.ts#L1249), [d7896ed4c981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d7896ed4c981))
- **Native regression coverage exists:** The Google transport test explicitly covers thoughtSignature-only parts keeping the stream active and expects an empty thinking_delta event. ([`extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts:1610`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d7896ed4c981/extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts#L1610), [d7896ed4c981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d7896ed4c981))
- **OpenAI-compatible parser still skips non-event chunks:** The shared completions stream loop records usage, but choice-less chunks and chunks without a delta continue without pushing an outer stream event. ([`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:2259`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d7896ed4c981/src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts#L2259), [d7896ed4c981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d7896ed4c981))
- **OpenAI-compatible reasoning support is text-bearing:** Current reasoning handling emits thinking only from reasoning_details text or string reasoning_content/reasoning/reasoning_text fields, not from reasoning token count activity alone. ([`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:2454`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d7896ed4c981/src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts#L2454), [d7896ed4c981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d7896ed4c981))
- **Idle watchdog depends on yielded stream events:** streamWithIdleTimeout races the next outer assistant-stream event against the idle timer, so provider bytes consumed internally do not reset the watchdog unless the transport pushes an event. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.ts:287`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d7896ed4c981/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.ts#L287), [d7896ed4c981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/d7896ed4c981))

Likely related people:

- **zhangguiping-xydt:** The provided related PR context credits this contributor with the native Google thoughtSignature-only stream fix that is the closest existing implementation pattern. (role: native Google fix contributor; confidence: medium; commits: [a55b2af7a5d3](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a55b2af7a5d332f754f7cab129a22a238856be47); files: `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`, `extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`)

(role: native Google fix commit author; confidence: medium; commits: [a55b2af7a5d3](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a55b2af7a5d332f754f7cab129a22a238856be47); files: `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`, `extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`)

(role: recent idle-timeout contributor; confidence: medium; commits: [9e9feb52f43c](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/9e9feb52f43c5b20975fc64263e1a7eeabb726b0); files: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts`, `src/config/schema.help.ts`)

input.title: fix: emit assistant update for tool-call-only messages from OpenAI-compatible providers [AI-assisted]
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #51462: fix: emit assistant update for tool-call-only messages from OpenAI-compatible providers [AI-assisted]
input.github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 51462 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/51462 - Title: fix: emit assistant update for tool-call-only messages from OpenAI-compatible providers [AI-assisted] - State: OPEN - Author: ccgibson - Labels: agents, size: XS, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary When an OpenAI-compatible provider (e.g. vLLM) returns a tool-call-only response with no text content, \`handleMessageEnd\` silently skips emitting the assistant update event. This prevents tool execution from completing — the session goes idle without executing the requested tools. ## Root Cause In \`pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts\`, \`handleMessageEnd()\` line 324 gates the assistant update emission on \`cleanedText || hasMedia\`. When vLLM returns a response where \`content\` is \`null\` (valid per the [OpenAI spec](https://github.com/openai/openai-python/blob/main/src/openai/types/chat/chat_completion_message.py#L18) — \`content: Optional[str] = None\`) and only \`tool_calls\` are present, no text or media is extracted, so the event is never emitted. **Before:** \`\`\`typescript if (!ctx.state.emittedAssistantUpdate && (cleanedText || hasMedia)) { \`\`\` **After:** \`\`\`typescript const hasToolCalls = Array.isArray(assistantMessage.content) && assistantMessage.content.some( (b: unknown) => b != null && typeof b === "object" && ("type" in b) && (b.type === "toolCall" || b.type === "toolUse" || b.type === "functionCall"), ); if (!ctx.state.emittedAssistantUpdate && (cleanedText || hasMedia || hasToolCalls)) { \`\`\` ## Reproduction Any vLLM-hosted model with tool calling enabled through the \`openai-completions\` provider: 1. Configure a vLLM model with \`supportsTools: true\` in \`openclaw.json\` 2. Ask the model to use a tool (e.g. "read the file /etc/hosts") 3. The model returns a tool call response with \`content: null\` and valid \`tool_calls\` 4. **Before fix:** Session silently drops to idle — tool never executes 5. **After fix:** Tool executes and result is returned to the model Tested across 4 providers to isolate the root cause: | Provider | \`content\` value on tool calls | Tool calling before fix | After fix | |---|---|---|---| | **Neuralwatt vLLM** (Kimi K2.5) | \`null\` | Silently drops | Works | | **Nebius vLLM** (Kimi K2.5) | \`null\` | Silently drops | Works | | **DeepInfra** (Kimi K2.5) | \`" "\` (whitespace) | Already worked | N/A | | **Neuralwatt vLLM** (GLM-5) | \`""\` (empty string) | Already worked | N/A | DeepInfra and GLM-5 worked because their non-null \`content\` values produced text blocks that passed the \`cleanedText\` gate. vLLM's \`content: null\` (which is spec-compliant per OpenAI's \`Optional[str] = None\`) produced no text blocks, hitting the bug. ## Context This is a targeted fix for one aspect of #13603. The broader issue describes multiple vLLM compatibility problems; this PR addresses the specific case where tool-call-only responses are silently dropped due to the missing tool call check in \`handleMessageEnd\`. The \`pi-ai\` streaming parser and \`pi-agent-core\` agent loop both correctly handle tool-call-only responses — the bug is only in the \`handleMessageEnd\` event emission gate in the subscription handler layer. ## Code Understanding The \`handleMessageEnd\` function in \`pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts\` is called by OpenClaw's event subscription system when the agent loop finishes streaming an assistant message. Its job is to extract text/media from the message and emit an \`"assistant"\` stream event so the TUI/webchat/Slack can display the response. The \`emittedAssistantUpdate\` flag prevents duplicate emissions (since streaming deltas may have already emitted updates). The bug: when the assistant message contains only tool call blocks (no text, no media), the function skips the emission entirely. While tool execution in \`pi-agent-core\`'s agent loop proceeds correctly regardless, the skipped emission leaves session state inconsistent, causing the TUI to show "idle" instead of the tool execution flow. ## Automated Review Feedback Addressed **Greptile + Codex reviews on initial commit identified:** 1. **P2 - Incomplete tool-call type coverage** — Fixed: now checks all three variants (\`toolCall\`, \`toolUse\`, \`functionCall\`) matching \`isOpenAIToolCallType()\` in \`pi-embedded-helpers/openai.ts\` and \`TOOL_CALL_TYPES\` in \`tool-call-id.ts\`. 2. **P2 - Null content blocks** — Fixed: added \`b != null && typeof b === "object"\` guards before accessing \`b.type\`, consistent with defensive patterns in \`extractAssistantText()\` and \`promoteThinkingTagsToBlocks()\`. 3. **P1 - Empty text dropped by downstream consumers** — Investigated: \`buildAssistantStreamData()\` produces \`text: ""\` which some HTTP consumers (\`openai-http.ts:527\`) skip via \`if (!content) return\`. However, this only affects the display layer — tool execution is handled independently by \`pi-agent-core\`'s agent loop. The fix works because the \`emittedAssistantUpdate = true\` flag prevents the subscription handler from entering an inconsistent state. Verified end-to-end: tool calls execute and results display correctly in the TUI. ## AI-Assisted PR This PR was developed with AI assistance (Claude Code). - **Testing:** Fully tested on a local OpenClaw v2026.3.13 instance with real vLLM inference traffic across multiple providers (Neuralwatt, Nebius, DeepInfra) - **Validation method:** Patched all 6 compiled dist bundles containing \`handleMessageEnd\`, restarted OpenClaw, and confirmed tool calls execute end-to-end with Kimi K2.5 on vLLM - **Code understanding:** Traced the full code path from \`pi-ai\` streaming parser → \`pi-agent-core\` agent loop → OpenClaw subscription handlers → TUI event display to isolate the bug to the \`handleMessageEnd\` emission gate - **Codex review:** Ran via GitHub integration on initial commit; all findings addressed in updated commit - **Session context:** The fix was discovered while debugging vLLM CUDA OOM crashes on a GLM-5 inference server. Comparing API response formats across providers (Neuralwatt vLLM, Nebius vLLM, DeepInfra) revealed the \`content: null\` vs \`content: ""\` discrepancy that triggers this bug. ## Test Plan - [x] Verified tool-call-only responses from vLLM Kimi K2.5 now execute tools end-to-end - [x] Verified text-only responses still work correctly - [x] Verified GLM-5 (\`content: ""\`) still works (no regression) - [x] Verified DeepInfra Kimi K2.5 (\`content: " "\`) still works (no regression) - [x] \`pnpm build\` — passes - [x] \`pnpm check\` — pre-existing failures only (identical to main: import boundary issues in Slack/Telegram/Discord/Matrix plugins) - [x] \`pnpm test\` — 971 passed, 12 failed, 3 skipped — **identical to main**, zero new failures ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-21T06:28:56Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a silent tool-execution failure affecting OpenAI-compatible providers (e.g. vLLM) that return `content: null` on tool-call-only responses. The single-line gate change in `handleMessageEnd` correctly gates the `emittedAssistantUpdate` emission on `cleanedText || hasMedia || hasToolCalls`, and the new `hasToolCalls` detection is defensive and consistent with the existing `TOOL_CALL_TYPES` set in `tool-call-id.ts` and `isOpenAIToolCallType()` in `pi-embedded-helpers/openai.ts`. **Key observations:** - The root cause is accurately diagnosed: `content: null` (valid per the OpenAI spec) produces no text blocks, so the old guard never triggered, leaving session state inconsistent. - All three block-type variants (`toolCall`, `toolUse`, `functionCall`) are covered, matching the canonical set already used elsewhere in the codebase. - Null/type guards (`b != null && typeof b === "object" && "type" in b`) are consistent with defensive patterns in `extractAssistantText()` and `promoteThinkingTagsToBlocks()`. - The emitted event will carry `text: ""` for pure tool-call turns; the PR description correctly notes that downstream display consumers may silently drop empty content, but this is acceptable because tool execution is driven independently by `pi-agent-core` — the important thing is that `emittedAssistantUpdate` is set to prevent session-state inconsistency. - Minor: the three type-string literals are a third copy of a set already centralized in `tool-call-id.ts`; using `extractToolCallsFromAssistant()` would eliminate the duplication, but this is non-blocking. - No unit tests were added for the new `hasToolCalls` branch; a test asserting that a tool-call-only `assistantMessage` triggers event emission would strengthen confidence in the fix long-term.

Confidence Score: 5/5

- Safe to merge — targeted one-line fix with a well-understood, fully-tested root cause and no regressions across 971 tests. - The change is minimal (one new local variable + one OR clause), the logic is correct and defensive, it is consistent with the canonical type constants already in the codebase, and the author tested it end-to-end across four providers. The only remaining item is a non-blocking style suggestion to reuse `extractToolCallsFromAssistant` instead of inlining the type strings. - No files require special attention.
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts Line: 328-336 Comment: **Consider reusing the shared `TOOL_CALL_TYPES` constant** The three string literals `"toolCall"`, `"toolUse"`, and `"functionCall"` are already centralized in `tool-call-id.ts` as `TOOL_CALL_TYPES` (and duplicated in `isOpenAIToolCallType()` in `pi-embedded-helpers/openai.ts`). Inlining them here creates a third site that must be kept in sync if a new tool-call block type is ever added. A lightweight option would be to import and reuse `extractToolCallsFromAssistant` from `tool-call-id.ts`, which already does this check with the same defensive guards: ```suggestion const hasToolCalls = extractToolCallsFromAssistant(assistantMessage).length > 0; ``` This would remove the duplicated type-string literals and the manual array-iteration logic entirely. (Minor — not a blocker.) How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
Last reviewed commit: ["fix: emit assistant ..."](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/0a38944540614b550be7ed9ec45642930528554e) - ccgibson at 2026-03-21T06:38:13Z: ## Addressing review feedback **Greptile (P2 - reuse shared constant):** Addressed in 60ef952 — replaced inline type checks with `extractToolCallsFromAssistant()` from `tool-call-id.ts`, which already handles null guards, all three tool call type variants (`toolCall`/`toolUse`/`functionCall`), and ID validation. No duplicated type-string literals. **Codex (P2 - empty text dropped by downstream relays):** Investigated and confirmed this is correct but not a blocker for this fix. The downstream HTTP relays (`openai-http.ts:527`, `openresponses-http.ts:676`) do drop empty-text assistant events. However, the fix works because: 1. Tool execution is handled independently by `pi-agent-core`'s agent loop (`agent-loop.js:111`) — it extracts tool calls from `message.content` regardless of text 2. Setting `emittedAssistantUpdate = true` prevents the subscription handler from entering an inconsistent state on subsequent turns 3. Verified end-to-end: tool calls execute and results display correctly in the TUI with vLLM Kimi K2.5 A follow-up improvement could emit tool-call metadata in the assistant event for downstream consumers, but that's a broader change beyond this targeted fix. - ccgibson at 2026-03-21T06:42:18Z: **Re: Codex P2 — carrying tool-call info in tool-only assistant updates** Confirmed: all three downstream HTTP relays (`openai-http.ts:527`, `openresponses-http.ts:678`, `acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts:294`) drop the assistant event when text/delta is empty. For remote clients connecting via these HTTP endpoints, tool-call-only turns remain invisible. However, this PR's scope is the TUI/webchat silent-idle bug described in #13603 — and that path doesn't go through the HTTP relays. The TUI receives tool execution events through the subscription handler's separate tool event path, and the `emittedAssistantUpdate` flag is what prevents the handler from entering an inconsistent state. Making the HTTP relays aware of tool-call-only assistant turns (e.g., emitting tool-call metadata or a synthetic marker) would be a worthwhile follow-up, but it touches `openai-http.ts`, `openresponses-http.ts`, and `acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts` — a broader change than this targeted fix. Happy to open a follow-up issue for that if maintainers agree it's worth tracking separately. - ccgibson at 2026-03-21T07:06:57Z: **Re: `build-smoke` CI failure** This is not caused by our change — `build-smoke` is currently failing on every main branch run (10+ consecutive failures as of today): ``` failure 2026-03-21T06:30:48Z (main) failure 2026-03-21T06:10:32Z (main) failure 2026-03-21T05:45:13Z (main) failure 2026-03-21T05:36:14Z (main) failure 2026-03-21T05:27:55Z (main) failure 2026-03-21T05:00:12Z (main) failure 2026-03-21T04:47:51Z (main) failure 2026-03-21T04:40:42Z (main) failure 2026-03-21T04:28:51Z (main) ... ``` The failure is `ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:18789` — the gateway doesn't bind before the smoke test connects. Pre-existing infrastructure issue on main, unrelated to this PR. The `check` job (formatting + lint + type checking) passes cleanly on this branch. - ccgibson at 2026-03-21T17:37:07Z: **Re: Codex P2 — tool-call blocks without IDs** Valid point. Replaced `extractToolCallsFromAssistant()` with a local `hasToolCallBlocks()` that only checks the block type without requiring a valid `id`. This handles providers that emit `{ type: "toolCall", name: "read", arguments: {} }` without an ID (pi-ai sets `id: ""` when the provider omits it). Updated in 05f7bff. - clawsweeper at 2026-04-28T12:04:09Z: Codex review: needs real behavior proof before merge. **Workflow note:** Future ClawSweeper reviews update this same comment in place.

input.title: [Bug]: openai-completions fallback candidate is selected, but fallback does not complete successfully through an OpenAI-compatible proxy
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #66125: [Bug]: openai-completions fallback candidate is selected, but fallback does not complete successfully through an OpenAI-compatible proxy
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 66125
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66125
- Title: [Bug]: openai-completions fallback candidate is selected, but fallback does not complete successfully through an OpenAI-compatible proxy
- State: OPEN
- Author: Cybertr0n313
- Labels: bug, regression, P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-info, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦪 silver shellfish

Body:
```markdown
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

OpenClaw selects a local `openai-completions` proxy as a fallback candidate after the base URL is corrected to `/v1`, but the agent turn still does not complete through that provider even though direct authenticated `POST /v1/chat/completions` requests succeed against the same endpoint and model.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure a local OpenAI-compatible provider with base URL `http://127.0.0.1:8317/v1` and API mode `openai-completions`.
2. Put that provider near the front of the fallback list.
3. Intentionally poison the primary model identifier to force fallback.
4. Trigger a minimal agent turn.
5. Observe that OpenClaw selects the proxy-backed model as a fallback candidate but does not complete through it and falls through to the next provider.
6. Separately verify the same proxy accepts `GET /v1/models` and an authenticated minimal `POST /v1/chat/completions` request against model `z-ai-glm-5`.

### Expected behavior

If a provider in `openai-completions` mode can list models, accept an authenticated minimal `POST /v1/chat/completions`, and is selected as the next fallback candidate, OpenClaw should either complete successfully through it or log the specific request-shape incompatibility clearly enough to diagnose.

### Actual behavior

After the base URL fix, OpenClaw recognizes the provider and selects it as a fallback candidate during real agent execution, but the completion path still does not succeed through that provider and OpenClaw falls through to the next fallback without enough detail to identify which request feature is incompatible.

### OpenClaw version

2026.4.11

### Operating system

Ubuntu Linux

### Install method

npm global

### Model

Primary intentionally invalid to force fallback; fallback model verified directly: `z-ai-glm-5`

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw -> local OpenAI-compatible proxy (`http://127.0.0.1:8317/v1`) -> upstream model/provider

### Additional provider/model setup details

Provider API mode is `openai-completions`. Before the `/v1` path correction, the provider could be skipped due to path failure. After correcting the base URL, OpenClaw now considers/selects it during fallback. Direct verified proxy behavior: `GET /v1/models` succeeds; authenticated minimal `POST /v1/chat/completions` succeeds with body:

```json
{
  "model": "z-ai-glm-5",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Reply with exactly: pong"
    }
  ],
  "temperature": 0
}
```

Observed mismatch is therefore no longer basic connectivity.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Observed evidence:
- OpenClaw logs show the provider is considered/selected as a fallback candidate after the `/v1` base URL correction.
- Direct authenticated `POST /v1/chat/completions` succeeds against the same endpoint/model.
- During real agent execution, completion still fails through that provider and OpenClaw moves to the next fallback.

Likely incompatibility areas to inspect:
- optional OpenAI request fields beyond a minimal chat payload
- tool/function-related fields
- reasoning-related fields
- response-format expectations
- token/output-control fields
- message role or system/developer message shape
- streaming vs non-streaming assumptions
- strictness around null or empty fields
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: users routing fallback traffic through narrower OpenAI-compatible proxies.
Severity: medium to high; blocks successful completion through the selected fallback provider.
Frequency: reproducible in the observed fallback scenario.
Consequence: unnecessary fallback traversal, failed intended provider usage, and reduced diagnosability.

### Additional information

Request for maintainers:
1. Document the minimal request contract OpenClaw expects for providers in `openai-completions` mode during normal agent execution.
2. Clarify whether a strict minimal compatibility mode exists for proxy-backed text-only chat providers.
3. Allow optional features to be disabled per provider/model so OpenClaw can emit a bare `chat/completions` request when needed.
4. Note any known incompatibilities with proxies that implement only a narrower subset of OpenAI-compatible behavior.

At this point the issue is not basic connectivity. The endpoint is reachable, authenticated, and can complete a minimal chat request. The unresolved problem is compatibility under real framework-generated completion traffic.
```

Comments/context:
```markdown
- steipete at 2026-04-25T02:29:55Z:
Related compatibility hardening landed on `main` in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/49f72b332f5daf0484292e1fc63b2088fa94c8c6.

That commit fixes two request-shape gaps that can break narrower OpenAI-compatible completions proxies:

- non-native `api: "openai-completions"` routes now strip OpenAI-only `store`
- `params.extra_body` / `params.extraBody` can pass provider-specific request fields through to the completions payload

I am leaving this issue open because the original report is broader than the confirmed `store` failure: it covers fallback candidate selection plus insufficient detail when the selected proxy still fails a real agent request. If the same proxy still falls through after this change, the next thing to capture is the exact failing request/response body from the proxy so we can decide whether this needs a strict-minimal completions mode, better fallback logging, or another targeted payload sanitizer.


- steipete at 2026-04-25T02:56:27Z:
Additional related hardening has now landed on `main` after the earlier `store` / `extra_body` fix:

- e5babbb5e7 records OpenAI-compatible usage-only final chunks instead of leaving usage at zero
- 26f06afb90 forwards `prompt_cache_key` only for explicit `compat.supportsPromptCacheKey` opt-in, leaving default proxy payloads unchanged
- 9fbfedf12a skips null / non-object streaming chunks before reading `usage` or `choices`

I am still leaving this issue open because the remaining claim is the broad fallback failure: selected proxy candidate still falls through, but we do not yet have the exact failing real-agent request/response. The next useful artifact is a proxy capture of the request body OpenClaw sends during the failed fallback and the provider response body/status. With that we can decide whether the remaining fix is a strict-minimal completions mode, another targeted sanitizer, or just better fallback logging.

- Maintainers can comment `@clawsweeper explain` to ask for more context, or `@clawsweeper stop` to stop active automation.

</details>

Keep open. Current main contains several OpenAI-compatible proxy hardening fixes and better diagnostics, but the remaining selected-fallback failure still lacks the exact OpenClaw-generated request body and proxy status/body needed to prove a remaining code fix or close as implemented.

**Ways to help us reproduce this**
- Add a screenshot or short recording showing the behavior.
- Add expected vs actual behavior.

**Next step**
Needs reporter or maintainer proxy capture and a maintainer choice on sanitizer versus diagnostics versus strict-minimal behavior before implementation.


<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Keep this open until a redacted proxy capture identifies the incompatible payload or response, then make the narrowest compat, diagnostics, or documentation change with focused regression coverage.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Unclear. The report gives plausible steps and direct proxy curl proof, but I do not have the reporter's local proxy or the exact failing current-main OpenClaw request/response pair.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

Unclear. The landed hardening and current diagnostics are the right direction for confirmed gaps, but choosing another sanitizer, stricter logging, or a strict-minimal mode should wait for the missing proxy capture.

Label justifications:

- `P2`: This is a normal-priority provider fallback/proxy compatibility issue with limited blast radius and missing current-main reproduction proof.
- `impact:auth-provider`: The issue concerns provider routing, fallback selection, and OpenAI-compatible model transport behavior.

What I checked:

- **Live discussion keeps the broad report open:** The issue comments from steipete explicitly say the landed `store`, `extra_body`, usage-chunk, `prompt_cache_key`, and invalid-chunk fixes are related but not enough to close the broader selected-fallback failure without a failing real-agent request body plus proxy response/status.
- **Runtime Chat Completions payload remains broader than the reporter curl:** Current main builds streamed Chat Completions requests with optional `stream_options.include_usage`, token controls, response format, tools/tool_choice, and reasoning fields, so a minimal non-streaming curl success does not prove the OpenClaw runtime payload is accepted by the proxy. ([`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:3026`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3d96111a5afe/src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts#L3026), [3d96111a5afe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3d96111a5afe))
- **Known proxy payload hardening is present:** Current main strips `store` for non-native `openai-completions` routes and supports provider-specific `extra_body` passthrough before store stripping, covering confirmed gaps but not proving this proxy's fallback turn now succeeds. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.ts:597`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3d96111a5afe/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.ts#L597), [3d96111a5afe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3d96111a5afe))
- **Existing compat knobs cover some narrow proxies:** Current wrappers can flatten string-only message content, strip messages to role/content, and remove tools when `compat.supportsTools: false`, but the missing proxy capture is still needed to choose the right mitigation. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/openai-stream-wrappers.ts:183`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3d96111a5afe/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/openai-stream-wrappers.ts#L183), [3d96111a5afe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3d96111a5afe))
- **Docs now describe common OpenAI-compatible failure signatures:** Troubleshooting docs cover cases where tiny `/v1/chat/completions` calls pass but OpenClaw agent turns fail, pointing users to `requiresStringContent`, `strictMessageKeys`, and `supportsTools: false` as current fix options. Public docs: [`docs/gateway/troubleshooting.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/troubleshooting). ([`docs/gateway/troubleshooting.md:226`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/troubleshooting), [3d96111a5afe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3d96111a5afe))

input.title: Interactive runs fail with auth-style 403 when custom OpenAI-compatible provider baseUrl uses Unicode/IDN or punycode hostname, but ASCII hostname/IP works
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #62109: Interactive runs fail with auth-style 403 when custom OpenAI-compatible provider baseUrl uses Unicode/IDN or punycode hostname, but ASCII hostname/IP works
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 62109
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/62109
- Title: Interactive runs fail with auth-style 403 when custom OpenAI-compatible provider baseUrl uses Unicode/IDN or punycode hostname, but ASCII hostname/IP works
- State: OPEN
- Author: nboody
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-info, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦐 gold shrimp

Body:
```markdown
### Summary

OpenClaw interactive runs (Web UI and local TUI / embedded path) fail with:

- `403 Your request was blocked.`
- `failoverReason: "auth"`
- `profileFailureReason: "auth"`

when a custom OpenAI-compatible provider uses either:

- a Unicode/IDN hostname in `baseUrl`
- or the punycode form of that same hostname

The same backend works when accessed via:

- ASCII hostname
- public IP + dedicated `/v1`-only proxy port

This suggests an issue in OpenClaw's handling of non-ASCII-origin hostnames (including punycode forms) in the interactive/embedded run path.

---

### Environment

- OpenClaw version: `2026.4.5`
- Host: Linux
- Surface:
  - Web UI
  - local TUI
- Provider type:
  - custom OpenAI-compatible provider
  - `api: openai-completions`

---

### Affected behavior

With a provider config like this:

```json
{
  "models": {
    "providers": {
      "custom_provider": {
        "baseUrl": "https://<unicode-idn-domain>/v1",
        "api": "openai-completions",
        "apiKey": "<redacted>",
        "models": [
          {
            "id": "gpt-5.4",
            "name": "gpt-5.4",
            "contextWindow": 200000,
            "maxTokens": 8192
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  },
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "model": {
        "primary": "custom_provider/gpt-5.4"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

interactive runs fail before a usable assistant reply is produced.

Relevant logs:

```text
error: 403 Your request was blocked.
failoverReason: auth
profileFailureReason: auth
provider: custom_provider
model: gpt-5.4
```

---

### Key observation

The backend itself is healthy.

Direct manual requests to the same backend succeed:

```bash
curl https://<unicode-idn-domain>/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <redacted>' \
  --data '{"model":"gpt-5.4","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"reply with exactly: ok"}],"max_tokens":8}'
```

This returns HTTP 200 and `ok`.

The same is true for the punycode hostname and for an ASCII hostname.

So the failure is not simply "backend unavailable".

---

### What was tested

#### 1. Different backends
Two different OpenAI-compatible backends were tested:

- a LiteLLM-based backend
- a different OAuth-backed OpenAI-compatible backend

Manual requests succeeded against both backends.

#### 2. Unicode hostname
Using a Unicode/IDN hostname in `baseUrl` caused interactive runs to fail with auth-style 403.

Example shape:

- `https://<unicode-idn-domain>/v1`

#### 3. Punycode hostname
Using the punycode form of the same hostname also failed in interactive runs.

Example shape:

- `https://<punycode-domain>/v1`

Manual `chat/completions` requests still succeeded through this hostname.

#### 4. Public IP + `/v1`-only gateway
A dedicated public proxy port exposing only `/v1/*` was created:

- `http://<public-ip>:<port>/v1`

Using that IP-based URL in the provider made Web UI / TUI interactive runs succeed.

#### 5. ASCII hostname
Using an unrelated ASCII-only hostname also worked:

- `https://<ascii-domain>/v1`

---

### Minimal conclusion

The failure correlates with using a hostname derived from an IDN domain in provider `baseUrl`.

- Unicode hostname: fails in interactive run path
- punycode hostname: also fails
- unrelated ASCII hostname: works
- public IP + port: works

---

### Expected behavior

OpenClaw should handle provider `baseUrl` hostnames consistently regardless of whether the configured hostname is:

- Unicode/IDN form
- punycode form
- plain ASCII hostname
- public IP

If manual requests to the backend succeed, the interactive run path should not fail early with a local auth-style 403 purely because the configured hostname is Unicode or punycode.

---

### Actual behavior

When `baseUrl` contains either a Unicode/IDN hostname or the punycode form of that hostname, interactive runs fail locally with auth-style 403:

```text
403 Your request was blocked.
failoverReason: auth
profileFailureReason: auth
```

even though the same backend is otherwise reachable and returns valid chat completions.

---

### Workaround

Avoid Unicode/IDN-derived hostname forms in provider `baseUrl`.

Use either:

- an unrelated ASCII hostname
- or a public IP + dedicated `/v1`-only proxy

Example working shape:

```json
"baseUrl": "https://<ascii-domain>/v1"
```

or:

```json
"baseUrl": "http://<public-ip>:<port>/v1"
```

instead of:

```json
"baseUrl": "https://<unicode-idn-domain>/v1"
```

or:

```json
"baseUrl": "https://<punycode-domain>/v1"
```

```

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

- Forward `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, and `seed` from inbound `POST /v1/chat/completions` through `streamParams` → runtime stream wrapper → OpenAI transport, so client-supplied sampling parameters reach the upstream provider.
- Extend `AgentStreamParams` with `frequencyPenalty`, `presencePenalty`, and `seed` fields. Forward them through `createStreamFnWithExtraParams` (extra-params layer) and `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` (transport layer) to the provider request payload.
- Add gateway-side validation for the new parameters in `validateOpenAiSamplingParams`: penalty params constrained to `[-2.0, 2.0]`, `seed` must be an integer — matching the existing `temperature`/`top_p` validation contract.
- Resolve aliased param keys with camelCase (runtime/request) priority over snake_case (config) so per-request gateway overrides take precedence over configured model defaults.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.sampling.test.ts` — 8/8 (existing sampling tests, no regressions)
- `pnpm test src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` — 168/168 (no regressions)
- `pnpm test src/gateway/openai-http.usage.test.ts src/gateway/openai-http.image-budget.test.ts` — 7/7 (no regressions)
- `pnpm check:changed` — clean (locally)
- `codex review` — "No discrete correctness issues were identified in the provided diff."
- Fork CI (`Lellansin/openclaw`, run [26092092169](https://github.com/Lellansin/openclaw/actions/runs/26092092169)) — 54/54 jobs passed

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI-compatible Gateway HTTP requests now forward client-supplied `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, and `seed` from `POST /v1/chat/completions` through `streamParams` to the upstream OpenAI-compatible provider. Invalid values are rejected at the gateway boundary with deterministic OpenAI-compatible 400 errors.
- Real environment tested: Local dev gateway on loopback port 18790, built from commit `044a61060a` with `pnpm build`. Runtime Node v24.14.1, CLI/gateway version 2026.5.19. Default agent model `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`. Gateway token and provider key read from local OpenClaw config.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm build`, then stopped the managed gateway service, started a dev gateway on port 18790, and posted 7 authenticated local requests to `http://127.0.0.1:18790/v1/chat/completions`: a happy-path request, three invalid-value probes (out-of-range `frequency_penalty`, out-of-range `presence_penalty`, fractional `seed`), and three boundary probes (`frequency_penalty: -2.0`, `presence_penalty: 2.0`, `seed: -1`).
- Evidence after fix:

  Happy-path request (`frequency_penalty: 0.5`, `presence_penalty: 0.3`, `seed: 42`):
  ```json
  {"id":"chatcmpl_91ca1153-...","object":"chat.completion","model":"openclaw","choices":[{"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"Hi"}}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":16847,"completion_tokens":16,"total_tokens":16863}}
  ```

  Out-of-range `frequency_penalty: 3`:
  ```json
  {"error":{"message":"`frequency_penalty` must be between -2.0 and 2.0.","type":"invalid_request_error"}}
  ```

  Out-of-range `presence_penalty: -3`:
  ```json
  {"error":{"message":"`presence_penalty` must be between -2.0 and 2.0.","type":"invalid_request_error"}}
  ```

  Non-integer `seed: 42.5`:
  ```json
  {"error":{"message":"`seed` must be an integer.","type":"invalid_request_error"}}
  ```

  Boundary `frequency_penalty: -2.0` → 200 OK
  Boundary `presence_penalty: 2.0` → 200 OK
  Negative integer `seed: -1` → 200 OK (rejected upstream by DeepSeek as invalid u64, not a gateway issue)

- Observed result after fix: Valid `frequency_penalty`/`presence_penalty`/`seed` requests completed successfully through the local OpenAI-compatible gateway. Invalid-value probes were intercepted with deterministic OpenAI-compatible 400 errors matching the existing `temperature`/`top_p` validation pattern. Boundary values within spec were accepted.
- What was not tested: `/v1/responses` endpoint (uses a different code path); `top_k` and `repetition_penalty` were intentionally excluded from this PR as they are not standard OpenAI Chat Completions fields and require per-provider gating.
```

**Risk before merge**
Why this matters: - Existing clients that already send invalid `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, or fractional `seed` will now receive gateway 400 responses instead of having those fields ignored.

```markdown
### Summary

Add interactive model parameter prompts (context window, max output tokens, modalities) during OpenAI-compatible provider onboarding.

### Problem to solve

When running `openclaw onboard` and selecting an OpenAI-compatible provider, users can pick a model but cannot configure model-specific parameters. Setting context window, max output tokens, and supported modalities requires manually editing `~/.openclaw/config.yaml`, which is error-prone, discouraged for non-technical users, and easy to break YAML formatting.

### Proposed solution

After the model selection step in the `openclaw onboard` flow for OpenAI-compatible providers, add interactive prompts for the following optional parameters:

- **Context Window** (e.g. `128000`) — maximum input context length
- **Max Output Tokens** (e.g. `4096`) — maximum tokens the model can generate
- **Modalities** (e.g. `text`, `text+image`, `audio`, `video`) — supported input/output modes

All prompts should be optional (allow skipping with sensible defaults).

### Alternatives considered

- **Manual config editing**: Works but is error-prone and not user-friendly.
- **Separate CLI command** (e.g. `openclaw models configure`): Adds an extra step instead of collecting all info during onboarding.

### Impact

- **Affected**: All users configuring OpenAI-compatible providers via CLI onboarding
- **Severity**: Medium — not a blocker, but forces users into unsafe config editing
- **Frequency**: Every time a new OpenAI-compatible model is added
- **Consequence**: Misconfigured context window causes truncated prompts; missing modality config prevents image/audio input from being sent correctly

### Evidence/examples

Current workaround requires manual editing:
```yaml
providers:
  openai-compatible:
    models:
      my-model:
        contextWindow: 128000
        maxOutputTokens: 4096
        modalities: [text, image]
```

### Additional information

Related: bundled providers (Qwen, Fireworks, StepFun, etc.) likely handle this internally. OpenAI-compatible custom providers should have parity.
```

Body:
```markdown
   # Feature Request: Support frequency_penalty and presence_penalty Parameters for OpenAI-Compatible Providers

   ## Summary

   OpenClaw currently only passes `temperature` and `maxTokens` to model providers, ignoring `frequency_penalty` and `presence_penalty` parameters that are critical for
 preventing repetitive output in LLM responses.

   ## Problem Description

   ### Current Behavior

   When configuring model parameters in `openclaw.json`:

   ```json
   "agents": {
     "defaults": {
       "models": {
         "bailian/glm-5": {
           "params": {
             "temperature": 0.7,
             "frequency_penalty": 0.5,
             "presence_penalty": 0.3
           }
         }
       }
     }
   }
 ```

 Only temperature and maxTokens are passed to the API. The frequency_penalty and presence_penalty parameters are silently ignored.

 ### Evidence

 In pi-embedded-B1nFZ7JF.js (and similar files), the createStreamFnWithExtraParams function only processes:

 ```javascript
   if (typeof extraParams.temperature === "number") streamParams.temperature = extraParams.temperature;
   if (typeof extraParams.maxTokens === "number") streamParams.maxTokens = extraParams.maxTokens;
   // frequency_penalty and presence_penalty are NOT processed
 ```

 ### Impact

 Some models (particularly GLM-5) are prone to repetitive output loops when these penalty parameters are not applied. This results in:

 1. Degraded user experience - Users receive messages with repeated content (e.g., same paragraph repeated 8 times)
 2. Wasted tokens - Repetitive output consumes API quota without providing value
 3. Unnecessary model switching - Users are forced to switch away from otherwise capable models

 Proposed Solution

 Extend createStreamFnWithExtraParams to support OpenAI-compatible penalty parameters:

 ```javascript
   function createStreamFnWithExtraParams(ba seStreamFn, extraParams, provider) {
     if (!extraParams || Object.keys(extraParams).length === 0) return;
     const streamParams = {};

     // Existing parameters
     if (typeof extraParams.temperature === "number") streamParams.temperature = extraParams.temperature;
     if (typeof extraParams.maxTokens === "number") streamParams.maxTokens = extraParams.maxTokens;

     // NEW: Support penalty parameters for OpenAI-compatible APIs
     if (typeof extraParams.frequency_penalty === "number") streamParams.frequency_penalty = extraParams.frequency_penalty;
     if (typeof extraParams.presence_penalty === "number") streamParams.presence_penalty = extraParams.presence_penalty;
     if (typeof extraParams.top_p === "number") streamParams.top_p = extraParams.top_p;
     if (typeof extraParams.top_k === "number") streamParams.top_k = extraParams.top_k;
     if (typeof extraParams.repetition_penalty === "number") streamParams.repetition_penalty = extraParams.repetition_penalty;

     // ... rest of function
   }
 ```

 ### API Compatibility

 These parameters are supported by:

 | Provider | frequency_penalty | presence_penalty | repetition_penalty |
 |----------|-------------------| ------------------|------------- ------|
 | OpenAI | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
 | Azure OpenAI | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
 | 智谱AI (GLM) | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
 | 阿里云百炼 | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
 | Anthropic | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
 | Ollama | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

 Use Case

 ### Configuration Example

 ```json
   {
     "agents": {
       "defaults": {
         "models": {
           "bailian/glm-5": {
             "params": {
               "temperature": 0.7,
               "frequency_penalty": 0.5,
               "presence_penalty": 0.3
             }
           },
           "openai/gpt-4": {
             "params": {
               "temperature": 0.8,
               "frequency_penalty": 0.3,
               "presence_penalty": 0.2
             }
           }
         }
       }
     }
   }
 ```

 ### Expected Behavior

 When these parameters are configured, they should be passed through to the underlying API call, allowing users to control:

 - frequency_penalty (0.0 to 2.0): Reduces the likelihood of repeating the same token proportionally to its frequency
 - presence_penalty (0.0 to 2.0): Reduces the likelihood of repeating any token that has appeared at least once
 - repetition_penalty (for Ollama and some local models): Alternative penalty mechanism

 Workaround (Current)

 Users currently have two options:

 1. Switch models - Use models less prone to repetition (e.g., kimi-k2.5, qwen3-max)
 2. Accept degraded output - Continue using affected models with repetitive output

 Neither is a satisfactory solution.

 Additional Context

 - OpenClaw Version: 2026.3.1
 - Related Issue: #32430 (Output Pipeline Race Condition)
 - Affected Models: GLM-5, some smaller models prone to repetition

 References

 - OpenAI API Documentation - Frequency and Presence Penalties (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create#chat-create-frequency_penalty)
 - 智谱AI API Documentation (https://open.bigmodel.cn/dev/api)
 - Ollama API Documentation (https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md)

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 Priority: Medium - Affects user experience but has workaround (model switching)

 Labels: enhancement, api, model-params

 ```
```

<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Review and land or revise https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84094 so standard OpenAI-compatible penalty fields flow through the existing stream-param pipeline, while leaving broader provider-specific knobs to separate scoped work.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes, source-level. Is this the best way to solve the issue?

Yes for the narrow request. A finite mapping for standard OpenAI-compatible penalty fields through the existing stream-param and transport pipeline is more maintainable than broad passthrough of every generation knob.

- `P2`: This is a normal provider-parameter improvement with clear scope, workarounds, and limited blast radius.

What I checked:

- **Current main stream wrapper omits the requested penalty params:** `createStreamFnWithExtraParams` builds stream options for `temperature`, `topP`, token caps, response format, transport, and cache fields, but it has no mapping for `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, `frequencyPenalty`, or `presencePenalty`. - **OpenAI completions transport omits the requested fields:** `BaseStreamOptions` and `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` expose and emit `temperature`, `topP`, `maxTokens`, and `responseFormat`, but not frequency or presence penalties. ([`src/gateway/openai-http.ts:865`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/9cdf8a1e2f9f/src/gateway/openai-http.ts#L865), [9cdf8a1e2f9f](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/9cdf8a1e2f9f))

input.title: fix(acpx): scrub provider credential env from ACP harness spawns
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #84763: fix(acpx): scrub provider credential env from ACP harness spawns
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_pr
- Number: 84763
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84763
- Title: fix(acpx): scrub provider credential env from ACP harness spawns
- State: OPEN
- Author: apoussaint
- Labels: docs, size: M, extensions: acpx, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🦪 silver shellfish, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider, status: 📣 needs proof

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

- **Problem:** `sessions_spawn` with `runtime:"acp"` + `agentId:"claude"` fails immediately with `AcpRuntimeError: Internal error: Invalid API key · code=ACP_TURN_FAILED`.
- **Solution:** Strip provider-credential env vars from the ACP harness launch command, keyed on the agent id, so each harness uses its own auth instead of inheriting the gateway's creds.
- **What changed:** A table-driven per-harness scrub merged into the existing acpx command decoration as an `env -u <NAME>` prefix; new `acp.scrubProviderEnv` config knob (default `true`).
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** The gateway's own auth/model-call path is untouched; the OpenClaw ACP bridge keeps the full env; reuse identity stays the raw command (no session-reuse changes); no blanket env stripping — only named provider-credential vars.

## Motivation

Operators set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in `~/.openclaw/.env` so the gateway can authenticate its own model calls. The value may be a standard key **or** an OAuth access token (`sk-ant-oat01-…`) — Anthropic's `/v1/messages` accepts both, so the gateway works either way. But Claude Code's CLI auth path **rejects** an OAuth token supplied via `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` because it expects its own Keychain OAuth flow. Since an ACP harness inherits the gateway's environment, the spawned `claude` child sees `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, tries to use it, and dies with "Invalid API key" before doing any work.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** ACP spawn of `agentId:"claude"` fails with `Invalid API key`.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS; Claude Code authenticated via Keychain OAuth; `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` present in `~/.openclaw/.env`. The reporter confirmed the diagnosis on this setup (see below). **Full disclosure:** I have not yet re-run a live `sessions_spawn` against a gateway built from this branch — the after-fix evidence below proves the env-strip *mechanism* and the resolved command, via the package's own tests plus a real child process. A live end-to-end run by someone with the failing setup is still recommended before merge.
- **Exact steps/commands run after this patch:**
  - `pnpm` acpx suite: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-acpx.config.ts` → **146 passed**
  - Typecheck: `tsgo:core`, `tsgo:extensions`, `tsgo:extensions:test` → clean
  - Config generators: `config:schema:check`, `config:docs:check` → ok
- **Evidence after fix (real child process):** a POSIX test builds the decorated command and runs it through a shell with the creds set in the parent env, then reads them back in the child:
  ```
  parent env: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-oat01-…, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=…, OPENCLAW_KEEPME=still-here
  command:    env -u ANTHROPIC_API_KEY -u ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN node -e '…print the three vars…'
  child sees: { a: null, t: null, keep: "still-here" }
  ```
  i.e. the two credentials are gone from the child while unrelated vars survive.
- **Observed result after fix:** harness launch command for `claude` becomes `env -u ANTHROPIC_API_KEY -u ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN … node ".../claude-agent-acp-wrapper.mjs" …`; the child no longer inherits the colliding credential.
- **What was not tested:** a live `sessions_spawn runtime:"acp" agentId:"claude"` against a gateway running this exact build (failing session keys exist on the reporter's machine).
- **Before evidence:** reporter's repro — `claude --print 'ping'` fails (inherits `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`); `env -u ANTHROPIC_API_KEY claude --print 'ping'` works. Renaming the var to `OPENCLAW_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in `.env` also fixes ACP spawns, confirming the collision is the cause.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** Auth-path collision. The gateway's `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (possibly an OAuth token) is inherited by the spawned Claude Code harness, whose CLI refuses OAuth tokens supplied through that env var. The real spawn happens in the acpx backend (`extensions/acpx/`), which launches the harness from a command string; the gateway's only lever over the child env is that string.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No isolation of provider credentials between the gateway and the ACP harnesses it spawns; harnesses silently inherited gateway creds.
- **Contributing context:** Each harness (claude, codex, gemini, cursor, copilot, droid, opencode) has its own auth mechanism, so none should inherit OpenClaw's provider creds.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/acpx/src/provider-env-scrub.test.ts`, `extensions/acpx/src/process-lease.test.ts` (incl. real child-process strip), `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.provider-env-scrub.test.ts` (scoped-registry wiring + classification).
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** resolving the `claude` harness command yields an `env -u ANTHROPIC_API_KEY -u ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` prefix; codex/gemini strip their providers; the openclaw bridge is untouched; `acp.scrubProviderEnv:false` disables it; command classification still works through the `env -u` prefix.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** the scrub is a pure, table-driven function plus a deterministic command decoration — unit-coverable without a live agent.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- ACP harnesses (claude/codex/gemini/…) no longer inherit the gateway's provider credentials; each falls back to its own auth.
- New config `acp.scrubProviderEnv` (boolean, **default `true`**). Set `false` to restore the old inherit-everything behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
sessions_spawn(acp, claude) -> spawn "node claude-agent-acp-wrapper.mjs"
  child env INHERITS ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (gateway's, may be OAuth token)
  -> Claude Code reads it -> "Invalid API key" -> ACP_TURN_FAILED

After:
sessions_spawn(acp, claude) -> spawn "env -u ANTHROPIC_API_KEY -u ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN node claude-agent-acp-wrapper.mjs"
  child env has NO ANTHROPIC_* -> Claude Code uses its own Keychain OAuth -> works
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **Yes** — fewer secrets reach subprocesses: provider credentials are removed from the spawned harness environment (a hardening; reduces credential leakage into third-party harnesses).
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **Yes (minor)** — the harness command string gains a leading `env -u <NAME>` prefix. Conservative: only named provider-credential vars are unset; `unwrapEnvCommand` was extended so command classification and the process reaper still match.
- Data access scope changed? **No**
- Risk + mitigation: see Risks below.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw gateway + acpx backend
- Model/provider: Anthropic (Claude Code harness over ACP)
- Integration/channel: ACP `sessions_spawn`
- Relevant config (redacted): `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` set in `~/.openclaw/.env` (value is an OAuth token in the reporter's case)

### Steps

1. With `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` set in `~/.openclaw/.env`, run `sessions_spawn` with `runtime:"acp"`, `agentId:"claude"`.
2. Observe failure (before this patch).
3. With this patch, repeat — the harness launches with provider creds stripped.

### Expected

- The Claude Code harness starts and uses its own Keychain OAuth.

### Actual (before fix)

- `AcpRuntimeError: Internal error: Invalid API key · code=ACP_TURN_FAILED`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (TDD: tests written red, then green; see test files)
- [x] Trace/log snippets (child-process strip output above)
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:** new + existing acpx suite (146 passing) including a real child-process test proving the credential is absent from the child env; scoped-registry produces the scrubbed command for claude/codex/gemini and leaves the openclaw bridge untouched; `acp.scrubProviderEnv:false` disables it; classification tolerates the `env -u` prefix; core+extensions typecheck and lint clean; regenerated config schema/doc baseline checks pass.
- **Edge cases checked:** unknown harness id (strips the union of known provider creds); Windows no-op (`env -u` not portable); de-duped credential names; persistent-session reuse unaffected (reuse identity is the

[truncated]
```

input.title: [Feature]: persist ACPX subprocess stderr to disk for post-mortem debugging
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #66465: [Feature]: persist ACPX subprocess stderr to disk for post-mortem debugging
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 66465
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66465
- Title: [Feature]: persist ACPX subprocess stderr to disk for post-mortem debugging
- State: OPEN
- Author: rtmuller
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:security, impact:crash-loop, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Problem

The ACPX runtime captures child-process stderr into an in-memory `startupStderr` buffer (~8 KB) that is only surfaced when an `AgentStartupError` fires. After successful `initialize`, stderr chunks continue to stream from the child but are effectively dropped — they flow through `captureStartupStderr` (which only keeps startup bytes) and are re-emitted to `process.stderr` only when `options.verbose` is set.

This makes it very hard to debug any post-init failure in the child agent:

- Silent per-turn timeouts (`withTimeout(promptPromise, timeoutMs)` returning `stopReason: "end_turn"`) — the child may have written a useful stack to stderr but it's gone.
- Intermittent OOM / segfault in the spawned `claude-agent-acp` / `codex-acp` / `gemini` process.
- Third-party preload / wrapper diagnostics (NODE_OPTIONS --require, APM agents, proxy bootstraps) that write to stderr on load.

Related existing issues where better stderr capture would have shortened diagnosis time:
- #61895 (orphan claude-agent-acp processes / progressive memory leak)
- #46795 (sessions_spawn streamTo=parent stalls — silent)
- #49081 (acpx plugin exits with code 1 despite healthy binary)
- #64923 (register.runtime JSON parser crashes on non-JSON stderr — this issue also touches the same stdio handling area)

## Proposal

In the ACPX runtime `start()` method, right after the child is spawned and before `child.stderr.on('data', ...)` is attached, open a per-subprocess log file and tee every stderr chunk into it:

\`\`\`js
const stderrLogDir = path.join(os.homedir(), ".openclaw", "acpx-stderr");
fs.mkdirSync(stderrLogDir, { recursive: true });
const stamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, "-");
const cmdBase = path.basename(spawnCommand || "agent").replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_.-]/g, "_");
const stderrLogPath = path.join(stderrLogDir, \`\${stamp}-pid\${child.pid ?? "unknown"}-\${cmdBase}.log\`);
const persistentStderrStream = fs.createWriteStream(stderrLogPath, { flags: "a" });
persistentStderrStream.on("error", () => {});
this.log(\`persisting subprocess stderr to \${stderrLogPath}\`);

const closeStderrLog = () => {
  try { persistentStderrStream.end(); } catch {}
};
child.once("exit", closeStderrLog);
child.once("close", closeStderrLog);

child.stderr.on("data", (chunk) => {
  try { persistentStderrStream.write(chunk); } catch {}
  this.captureStartupStderr(startupStderr, chunk);
  if (!this.options.verbose) return;
  process.stderr.write(chunk);
});
\`\`\`

Characteristics of the proposed change:

- **Additive**: existing behavior (in-memory \`startupStderr\`, verbose \`process.stderr\` mirroring) is unchanged.
- **Cheap**: one open file descriptor per live subprocess.
- **Self-cleaning option**: could add a config-driven retention (e.g. \`plugins.acpx.config.stderrLogRetentionHours\`, default 168 / one week) that prunes old files on runtime start.
- **Uses only already-imported modules** (\`fs\`, \`path\`, \`os\`).
- **Configurable root**: optional \`plugins.acpx.config.stderrLogDir\` to override \`~/.openclaw/acpx-stderr/\`.

## Why this should be upstream rather than a user patch

We have been running this exact patch against \`register.runtime-*.js\` in production since 2026-04-13 on 2026.4.9 and now 2026.4.12. Each upgrade wipes the patch (the bundle filename is hashed) and requires a reapply script. The feature is small, has no runtime cost, and would have been the fastest path to root-cause in multiple of the linked issues above — it seems like a reasonable default to ship.

Happy to open a PR if the maintainers agree on the shape (config keys, retention behavior, log rotation). Feedback on whether this should be opt-in (off by default) or opt-out (on by default) welcome.

## Environment where the patch is in production

- OpenClaw 2026.4.12 (post-upgrade from 2026.4.9)
- Linux, Node 22, gateway under systemd
- Patch bundled into \`dist/register.runtime-DX1TAXAg.js\` at the \`startupStderr\` anchor point
```
