Define durable ACPX stderr/run artifacts at the supported `acpx/runtime` boundary, then expose reviewed OpenClaw config/docs for redaction, file permissions, retention, location, and real ACPX smoke proof.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes. Source inspection on current main shows there is no general ACPX stderr artifact config or runtime option, while the existing implementation only captures redacted Codex wrapper stderr for generic Codex ACP failures.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

No. The raw always-on tee proposed in the issue is useful as a production workaround, but the maintainable upstream shape needs a reviewed artifact contract with redaction, permissions, retention, and ownership at the current `acpx/runtime` boundary.

Label justifications:

- `P2`: This is a normal-priority ACPX diagnostics feature with real operator value but no proven emergency outage in current main.
- `impact:security`: The requested durable stderr logs can contain credentials, prompts, local paths, provider diagnostics, and MCP environment details.
- `impact:crash-loop`: The motivating failures include post-init child crashes, OOMs, segfaults, and silent ACPX stalls that are hard to diagnose today.

Security concerns:

- [medium] Design redaction and retention before persisting stderr
  Always-on raw subprocess stderr logs can copy credentials, prompts, local paths, provider output, proxy/APM diagnostics, and MCP environment values into long-lived files, so the feature needs an explicit redaction, permission, location, and retention policy.
  Confidence: 0.9

Acceptance criteria:

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/acpx/src/runtime.test.ts extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.test.ts extensions/acpx/src/process-lease.test.ts extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts extensions/acpx/src/service.test.ts
- pnpm build if the `acpx/runtime` boundary, bundled plugin wiring, or published package surface changes
- Real ACPX smoke proof showing redacted stderr/run artifacts, permissions/location, retention behavior, and coverage for non-Codex ACP agents

What I checked:

- **Current runtime boundary delegates to acpx:** OpenClaw imports `AcpxRuntime` from `acpx/runtime` and delegates turn execution to the resolved base runtime, so a general subprocess stderr artifact contract belongs at that boundary rather than only in the OpenClaw wrapper. ([`extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts:20`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3d96111a5afe/extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts#L20), [3d96111a5afe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3d96111a5afe))
- **No general stderr artifact options are passed:** `createLazyDefaultRuntime` passes cwd, lease, wrapper root, session store, registry, probe, MCP, permission, and timeout options, but no stderr log path, retention, artifact directory, redaction policy, or enable flag. ([`extensions/acpx/src/service.ts:227`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3d96111a5afe/extensions/acpx/src/service.ts#L227), [3d96111a5afe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3d96111a5afe))
- **No plugin config surface exists:** The ACPX config schema exposes cwd, stateDir, probe, permission, MCP, timeout, queue compatibility, and agent command settings, but not stderr logging, retention, redaction, or artifact toggles. ([`extensions/acpx/src/config-schema.ts:24`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3d96111a5afe/extensions/acpx/src/config-schema.ts#L24), [3d96111a5afe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3d96111a5afe))
- **Codex-only partial diagnostic path:** Current main can generate a bounded redacted stderr log for the generated Codex ACP wrapper, but that prefix is only supplied by `buildCodexAcpWrapperScript` and does not define a general ACPX subprocess artifact surface. ([`extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts:573`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3d96111a5afe/extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts#L573), [3d96111a5afe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3d96111a5afe))
- **Tests cover only the Codex wrapper diagnostic path:** The focused test asserts redacted per-lease Codex wrapper stderr capture, which supports the partial implementation but not the broad request for all ACPX child subprocess stderr after initialization. ([`extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.test.ts:546`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/3d96111a5afe/extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.test.ts#L546), [3d96111a5afe](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3d96111a5afe))
- **Overlapping PR remains open and blocked:** The related terminal-truth artifacts PR is still open, touches wrapper-owned `stdout.log`/`stderr.log` artifacts, and currently carries proof and merge-risk labels rather than a merged implementation. ([bb45eb5acb51](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/bb45eb5acb51))

Likely related people:

- **osolmaz:** Commit `154a7edb7cad` refactored the extension to consume the published `acpx/runtime` library, which is where a general subprocess stderr artifact contract now belongs. (role: current ACPX runtime boundary introducer; confidence: high; commits: [154a7edb7cad](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/154a7edb7cad3d427584eb977e43280f7afb7de7); files: `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts`, `extensions/acpx/src/service.ts`, `extensions/acpx/package.json`)

- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
# Feature Request: Per-agent default model and reasoning_effort in ACPX plugin config

## Problem

When OpenClaw spawns ACP sessions via `sessions_spawn` or `/acp spawn`, there is no way to configure default `model` or `reasoning_effort` per agent harness. Each new session starts with the agent's built-in default model, requiring manual post-spawn configuration via `/acp model` and `/acp set reasoning_effort` every time.

This is particularly painful for teams running multiple harnesses (e.g., Codex + Claude Code + Gemini CLI) where each needs a different model and reasoning configuration.

## Current State

### acpx CLI already supports `--model`

The acpx CLI has a `--model <id>` global option that applies the model via `session/set_config_option` after session creation. The `set` subcommand also supports `reasoning_effort` and other config options at runtime:

```bash
acpx --model gpt-5.4 codex prompt --session <name> --file -
acpx codex set reasoning_effort xhigh --session <name>
```

### OpenClaw ACPX plugin does not pass `--model`

The ACPX plugin's `buildPromptArgs()` constructs the following command:

```
acpx --format json --json-strict --cwd <dir> \
     --approve-reads --non-interactive-permissions <policy> \
     --ttl <ttl> <agent> prompt --session <name> --file -
```

No `--model` flag is included, and no `setConfigOption` call is made after session creation.

### Plugin config schema has no model/reasoning keys

`AcpxPluginConfigSchema` (strictObject) only supports:

- `command`, `expectedVersion`, `cwd`
- `permissionMode`, `nonInteractivePermissions`
- `pluginToolsMcpBridge`, `strictWindowsCmdWrapper`
- `timeoutSeconds`, `queueOwnerTtlSeconds`
- `mcpServers`

### Agent config overrides only support `command`

`readConfiguredAgentOverrides()` reads only the `command` field from agent entries, ignoring any other properties.

### No programmatic `/acp set` from agents

Agents cannot call `/acp model` or `/acp set` programmatically. These are user-facing slash commands only. `sessions_send` to an ACP session sends a new prompt turn, not a control command.

## Proposal

Add an `agentDefaults` key to the ACPX plugin config schema that allows per-agent default `model` and `configOptions`:

```json5
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "acpx": {
        "config": {
          "agentDefaults": {
            "codex": {
              "model": "gpt-5.4",
              "configOptions": {
                "reasoning_effort": "xhigh"
              }
            },
            "claude": {
              "model": "anthropic/claude-opus-4-6",
              "configOptions": {
                "reasoning_effort": "high"
              }
            },
            "gemini": {
              "model": "gemini-3.1-pro"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Implementation options

**Option A — Pass `--model` at prompt time (minimal change):**

In `buildPromptArgs()`, if `agentDefaults[agent].model` is set, append `--model <id>` to the prefix args. This leverages existing acpx CLI `--model` behavior with a one-line change.

**Option B — Auto `setConfigOption` after session creation (more flexible):**

After `ensureSession()` completes (or on first prompt), automatically call `setConfigOption()` for each key in `agentDefaults[agent].configOptions`. This handles arbitrary config options beyond just `model` (e.g., `reasoning_effort`, `mode`, future agent-specific options).

**Recommendation:** Combine both — Option A for `model` (since acpx already supports `--model`), Option B for `configOptions` (generic key-value pairs applied via `session/set_config_option`).

## Current Workaround

After each ACP session spawn, the operator must manually run:

```
/acp model gpt-5.4
/acp set reasoning_effort xhigh
```

This cannot be automated at the config level and must be repeated for every new session.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: 2026.4.1
- ACPX plugin: bundled with OpenClaw
- Agents tested: codex (codex-acp 0.9.5), claude (claude-agent-acp 0.21.0), gemini CLI

```

Body:
```markdown
### Summary

ACPX Codex sandbox (`~/.openclaw/acpx/codex-home/`) does not inherit plugins from the 
user's personal Codex home (`~/.codex/`). This means plugins like Superpowers that are 
already installed in the user's Codex App are invisible to ACP background tasks. Users 
must manually symlink skills and re-add marketplaces inside the sandbox — a workaround 
that breaks on every OpenClaw update.

### Problem to solve

When OpenClaw spawns ACP background tasks via the Codex adapter, it uses a separate 
`CODEX_HOME` at `~/.openclaw/acpx/codex-home/`. This sandbox does NOT inherit plugins 
installed in the user's personal Codex home (`~/.codex/`).
Even when:
- The user has Superpowers installed in their Codex App ✅
- `openclaw.json → skills.load.extraDirs` points to superpowers skills ✅  
- The plugin cache inside `codex-home/plugins/cache/` already contains the plugin files ✅
...ACP tasks only see the 5 built-in system skills: `imagegen, openai-docs, plugin-creator, 
skill-creator, skill-installer`.

### Proposed solution

When OpenClaw spawns ACP background tasks via the Codex adapter, it uses a separate 
`CODEX_HOME` at `~/.openclaw/acpx/codex-home/`. This sandbox does NOT inherit plugins 
installed in the user's personal Codex home (`~/.codex/`).
Even when:
- The user has Superpowers installed in their Codex App ✅
- `openclaw.json → skills.load.extraDirs` points to superpowers skills ✅  
- The plugin cache inside `codex-home/plugins/cache/` already contains the plugin files ✅
...ACP tasks only see the 5 built-in system skills: `imagegen, openai-docs, plugin-creator, 
skill-creator, skill-installer`.

### Alternatives considered

One or more of:
- **Option A:** Add config key `acpx.codex.inheritPlugins: true` that syncs the user's 
  `~/.codex/` plugin list into the ACP sandbox on startup
- **Option B:** Add `acpx.codex.extraMarketplaces: ["obra/superpowers"]` in `openclaw.json` 
  so the wrapper auto-installs specified marketplaces
- **Option C:** Have the Codex ACP wrapper read `skills.load.extraDirs` from `openclaw.json` 
  and pass them to the Codex subprocess
## Environment
- OpenClaw: v2026.5.14-beta.2
- Codex ACP: @zed-industries/codex-acp (via wrapper)
- OS: Ubuntu (WSL2)
- Plugin affected: Superpowers v5.1.0

### Impact

Any user relying on Codex plugins (Superpowers, etc.) for quality coding workflows will 
find that ACP background tasks run without those plugins — producing lower quality output 
with no brainstorming, no TDD, no systematic debugging, and no code review.


### Evidence/examples

_No response_

### Additional information

- OpenClaw: v2026.5.14-beta.2
- Codex ACP: @zed-industries/codex-acp (via wrapper)
- OS: Ubuntu (WSL2)
- Plugin affected: Superpowers v5.1.0
```

This needs a maintainer product/security decision before implementation because it changes ACPX isolation and plugin execution policy; the existing open Codex-home override PR is the current candidate lane to evaluate.

**Security**
Needs attention: The requested behavior changes a Codex ACPX code-execution and credential boundary, so the policy needs maintainer/security review before implementation.

Best possible solution:

Keep ACPX isolated by default, and if accepted, add an explicit allowlisted Codex ACP plugin/home/marketplace path with public docs, focused ACPX tests, and redacted real-runtime proof.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

No high-confidence live reproduction was run. Source, tests, docs, and the pinned adapter contract do confirm current main uses an isolated generated Codex home and does not apply native Codex plugin config to ACP bindings.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

No, automatic inheritance of the operator's personal Codex plugins is not clearly the safest default. A maintainable solution should be explicit and allowlisted, or use a reviewed Codex-home override path such as the related open PR after maintainer/security review.

Security concerns:

- [medium] Avoid implicit plugin trust expansion
  Auto-inheriting personal Codex plugins or marketplace caches into background ACPX tasks could execute plugin-provided tools or hooks in a context that currently stays isolated; any implementation should require explicit operator opt-in and document the boundary.

- **Current ACPX wrapper behavior:** Current main generates the Codex ACP wrapper with `CODEX_HOME` fixed to the generated `./codex-home/` directory before launching the adapter. ([`extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts:580`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/aedcf0f897d4/extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts#L580), [aedcf0f897d4](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/aedcf0f897d4))
- **Generated Codex home contents:** `prepareIsolatedCodexHome` reads source Codex config only to render a generated `config.toml` with trusted project paths; it does not copy plugin caches, marketplaces, or skill extra directories into ACPX state. ([`extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts:612`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/aedcf0f897d4/extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts#L612), [aedcf0f897d4](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/aedcf0f897d4))

Public docs: [`docs/plugins/codex-harness.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/codex-harness). input.title: fix(acpx): strip provider API keys from child harness env
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #81200: fix(acpx): strip provider API keys from child harness env

- State: OPEN

- Labels: docs, scripts, docker, size: L, extensions: acpx, proof: supplied, proof: sufficient, P2, impact:security, impact:auth-provider

Body:
```markdown
## Summary
- add built-in ACP harness metadata for provider API-key env vars that must be stripped before launching provider-owned child harnesses
- route the built-in Claude and Gemini ACP aliases through generated wrappers that delete only those provider API-key env vars from the spawned child process env
- keep the parent process env unchanged and keep Codex behavior as-is except for using the shared wrapper env-copy helper

Fixes #69260

## Notes
- Claude strips `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` before launching `claude-agent-acp`.
- Gemini strips `GEMINI_API_KEY` and `GOOGLE_API_KEY` before launching `gemini --acp`.
- The strip list is applied inside the generated wrapper only, after copying `process.env`; unrelated env vars still pass through.
- Custom non-built-in commands are left alone. A broader user-configurable auth-contract schema and doctor/readiness warnings can be follow-ups if maintainers want that larger surface.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: generated ACPX child wrappers should prevent provider API-key and Gemini selector env fallback for built-in Claude and Gemini harnesses while preserving unrelated env and leaving the parent process env untouched.
- Real environment tested: disposable source checkout on Linux at PR head `21f655c6a7e61cea2a30158e813d7a7e8101d1de`, using generated OpenClaw ACPX wrappers and local child commands that print their received env.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: generated Claude and Gemini wrappers from this branch, then ran a direct Node probe (`node --import tsx probe-acpx-env-strip.ts`) that launched the generated wrappers with dummy provider API-key env vars plus dummy Gemini selector env vars in the child environment, and captured child stdout plus parent env after launch.
- Evidence after fix (console output):

```text
Claude child stdout: {"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY":null,"OPENCLAW_ACPX_ENV_PROBE":"preserved"}
Gemini child stdout: {"argv":["--acp"],"GEMINI_API_KEY":null,"GOOGLE_API_KEY":null,"GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_GCA":null,"GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI":null,"OPENCLAW_ACPX_ENV_PROBE":"preserved"}
Parent env after child launches: {"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY":"parent-dummy-anthropic","GEMINI_API_KEY":"parent-dummy-gemini","GOOGLE_API_KEY":"parent-dummy-google","GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_GCA":"parent-dummy-gca-selector","GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI":"parent-dummy-vertex-selector"}
```

- Observed result after fix: child ACP harness env no longer contains provider API-key variables (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`) or Gemini selector variables (`GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_GCA`, `GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI`); unrelated env still reaches the child; Gemini keeps the default `--acp` launch args; and the parent env remains available/non-mutated after both launches.
- What was not tested: no live provider login/session was exercised; doctor/readiness warnings and explicit API-key opt-in UX are intentionally deferred.

## Validation
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.test.ts extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts extensions/acpx/src/process-reaper.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-acpx.config.ts extensions/acpx`
- `corepack pnpm lint:extensions`

```

input.title: [Bug]: Decouple sidecar startup from ACPX — Discord/heartbeat/cron should start in parallel, not after ACPX ready
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #79625: [Bug]: Decouple sidecar startup from ACPX — Discord/heartbeat/cron should start in parallel, not after ACPX ready
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 79625
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/79625
- Title: [Bug]: Decouple sidecar startup from ACPX — Discord/heartbeat/cron should start in parallel, not after ACPX ready
- State: OPEN
- Author: bzelones
- Labels: bug, bug:behavior

Body:
```markdown
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

Yes

### Summary

Even with OPENCLAW_ACPX_RUNTIME_STARTUP_PROBE=1 enabled, Discord, heartbeat, and cron do not start until after acpx: ready fires. ACPX is the slowest subsystem at startup (~36–110s depending on probe mode), and there is no functional dependency between ACPX readiness and Discord's ability to connect to its gateway, receive messages, or dispatch heartbeats. The current architecture serializes all sidecar startup behind ACPX, making the slowest subsystem a de facto gate for all latency-sensitive ones.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Start gateway cold with OPENCLAW_ACPX_RUNTIME_STARTUP_PROBE=1
2. Observe log sequence:

T+36s  plugins: embedded acpx runtime backend registered
T+36s  browser/server: listening
T+41s  plugins: embedded acpx runtime backend ready
T+41s  gateway/channels/discord: starting provider
T+41s  gateway/heartbeat: started
T+41s  cron: started

3. Observe: Discord, heartbeat, and cron all wait for acpx: ready before starting — despite having no functional dependency on ACPX

### Expected behavior

Sidecar startup should be parallel and dependency-driven, not sequential. Subsystems with no ACPX dependency should start immediately after gateway: ready:

gateway: ready
    ├── Discord        → start immediately (no ACPX dependency)
    ├── browser        → start immediately (no ACPX dependency)
    ├── heartbeat      → start immediately (no ACPX dependency)
    ├── cron           → start immediately (no ACPX dependency)
    └── ACPX           → start in background, emit acpx:ready when done


Subsystems that genuinely require ACPX (e.g. ACP harness spawning) should subscribe to an acpx:ready event rather than having everything blocked by implicit startup ordering.

### Actual behavior

From a cold boot with probe enabled (2026-05-08, Windows 11 / OpenClaw 2026.4.15):
acpx: ready fires at T+41s
Discord, heartbeat, and cron all start at T+41s — zero seconds before or after ACPX
There is no log evidence that any of these subsystems require ACPX to function at startup
Without the probe, this coupling pushes Discord to T+118s — nearly 2 minutes into the boot sequence

### OpenClaw version

2026.4.15

### Operating system

Windows 11 (10.0.26200, x64)

### Install method

npm global

### Model

N/A

### Provider / routing chain

N/A (gateway-level issue)

### Additional provider/model setup details

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Related to #79596 (acpx ready signal semantics) and #79616 (ACPX subphase instrumentation). Those issues address observability and the readiness contract. This issue addresses the underlying coupling that makes ACPX startup latency affect channel availability at all. Fixing this would make Discord startup time independent of ACPX initialization speed regardless of how the readiness contract is resolved.
```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_
```

<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Land a dependency-aware startup fix that pins or loads channel runtime registry state before channel start, runs slow ACPX-dependent work on its own readiness path, and separately decouples heartbeat/cron from ACPX where safe.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes for source-level reproduction: current main still awaits `plugins.runtime-post-bind` before sidecars and gates scheduled-service activation on `startupSidecarsReady`. I did not reproduce the Windows cold-boot timing trace live.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

No, closing now is not the best path. The requested direction is valid, but the linked PR's early channel start happens before the channel registry is pinned, so the maintainable fix needs a dependency-aware startup seam rather than the current reorder.

What I checked:

- **Current main still gates sidecars behind runtime plugin loading:** `startGatewayPostAttachRuntime` awaits `plugins.runtime-post-bind` via `params.loadStartupPlugins()` before constructing the `sidecarsPromise`, so slow startup plugin work remains ahead of the sidecar phase. ([`src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts:726`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/916234977a7c/src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts#L726), [916234977a7c](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/916234977a7c))
- **Channels still start inside the sidecar phase:** `startGatewaySidecars` calls `params.startChannels()` only within `sidecars.channels`; that phase is entered after the runtime plugin load in the post-attach path. ([`src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts:470`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/916234977a7c/src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts#L470), [916234977a7c](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/916234977a7c))
- **Sidecars-ready remains a total sidecars gate:** The code awaits `startGatewaySidecars`, then clears startup-gated methods, calls `onSidecarsReady`, marks `sidecars.ready`, and logs `gateway ready` only after `sidecars.total` resolves. ([`src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts:780`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/916234977a7c/src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts#L780), [916234977a7c](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/916234977a7c))
- **Heartbeat activation still waits for sidecars-ready:** `activateScheduledServicesWhenReady` returns until both `postAttachRuntimeReturned` and `startupSidecarsReady` are true, so heartbeat activation remains coupled to sidecar readiness. ([`src/gateway/server.impl.ts:1430`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/916234977a7c/src/gateway/server.impl.ts#L1430), [916234977a7c](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/916234977a7c))
- **Scheduled service activation starts heartbeat and cron after the gate:** `activateGatewayScheduledServices` starts the heartbeat runner and starts cron unless `startCron` is false; this is called only through the gated activation path in `server.impl.ts`. ([`src/gateway/server-runtime-services.ts:245`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/916234977a7c/src/gateway/server-runtime-services.ts#L245), [916234977a7c](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/916234977a7c))
- **ACPX still participates in startup plugin work:** The ACPX manifest declares `activation.onStartup=true` and `enabledByDefault=true`, so ACPX remains part of the startup-plugin surface involved in the reported ordering. ([`extensions/acpx/openclaw.plugin.json:3`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/916234977a7c/extensions/acpx/openclaw.plugin.json#L3), [916234977a7c](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/916234977a7c))

Related: #79624 flips the acpx startup probe to run by default so gateway `ready` no longer fires before acpx is registered. That addresses the readiness contract (#79596) — clients that act on `ready` as an all-systems-go signal now get a fully registered acpx backend.

This issue is orthogonal: Discord/heartbeat/cron still serialize after `plugins: embedded acpx runtime backend registered` because the plugin lifecycle gates all sidecar startup. Decoupling that would require the plugin startup orchestrator to support parallel startup with per-subsystem dependency declarations — a separate architectural change from the readiness semantics fix in #79624.

I'd like to work on this. Approach: move channel startup before ACPX await using Promise.all(). Channels (Discord/Telegram) have zero dependency on ACPX — they only need their own adapter configs. Subsystems that do need ACPX (model prewarm, ACP sessions) still await it. Will have draft PR in 48h.

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

- Problem: the ACPX config test always expected the source `tsx` MCP server entry, but the resolver intentionally prefers `dist/mcp/*.js` when built entries exist.
- Why it matters: this made `extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts` environment-sensitive and matches the failure tracked in #80431.
- What changed: the ACPX test now builds a temporary OpenClaw layout and verifies both source fallback and dist-present MCP server resolution.
- CI unblock note: current `main` also had a redundant `String(firstContent.text)` in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts` that failed `check-lint`; this PR removes that no-op conversion in a separate commit.
- What did NOT change: no ACPX runtime behavior, manifest schema, config defaults, bundled MCP server code, or Codex app behavior changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #80431
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: ACPX config coverage now matches the resolver contract for both source fallback and built `dist/mcp/*.js` MCP server entries.
- Real environment tested: native Windows PowerShell checkout on branch `dev/vino/acpx-mcp-server-config-test`, Node `v24.13.0`, Corepack `pnpm@11.1.0`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` direct ACPX config resolver probe; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts`; `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8`; `git diff --check`; `codex review --uncommitted`.
- Evidence after fix: copied console output from the direct `node --import tsx --input-type=module` ACPX resolver probe:

```text
source: {"command":"C:\\Program Files\\nodejs\\node.exe","args":["--import","C:\\Users\\vinot\\code\\openclaw\\node_modules\\tsx\\dist\\loader.mjs","C:\\Users\\vinot\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-acpx-proof-source-lSNpLj\\src\\mcp\\plugin-tools-serve.ts"]}
dist: {"command":"C:\\Program Files\\nodejs\\node.exe","args":["C:\\Users\\vinot\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-acpx-proof-dist-zch1c1\\dist\\mcp\\plugin-tools-serve.js"]}
```

Focused ACPX Vitest output reported `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 16 passed (16)`; oxfmt reported `All matched files use the correct format`; `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8` reported `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors` for core, extensions, and scripts; `git diff --check` produced no output; Codex review reported no actionable correctness issue.
- Observed result after fix: the real resolver probe selected the source `tsx` entry without a dist artifact and the built `dist/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.js` entry when that artifact existed; the matching ACPX config test passes with hermetic fixtures for both paths; the CI-equivalent oxlint shard no longer reports the redundant Codex test conversion.
- What was not tested: full `pnpm test extensions/acpx`, live ACPX runtime, packaged build output, and a local Windows pass of the Codex context-engine test file. The Codex line is a lint-only no-op conversion cleanup, and CI Linux checks are the intended proof for that file.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): issue #80431 includes the failing output where the test expected `node --import ... src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.ts` while the resolver returned `node ... dist/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.js`; the PR's first CI run showed `check-lint` failing on `typescript(no-unnecessary-type-conversion)` for `String(firstContent.text)`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the ACPX test asserted the source MCP server entry unconditionally, while `resolvePluginToolsMcpServerConfig` and the matching OpenClaw tools resolver intentionally prefer built dist entries when those files exist.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no test fixture for the dist-present path, so the suite depended on whether the local checkout had build artifacts.
- Contributing context (if known): the failure was reported from a broad local rerun where `dist/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.js` existed.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: ACPX MCP bridge config resolves source entries when dist entries are absent and built entries when dist entries exist.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the resolver through `resolveAcpxPluginConfig` without requiring a real build artifact in the checkout.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): the previous test covered only the source expectation and was environment-sensitive.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows, native PowerShell
- Runtime/container: Node `v24.13.0`, Corepack `pnpm@11.1.0`
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): ACPX plugin config tests
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Install dependencies with `corepack pnpm install` after `pnpm` was not present on PATH.
2. Run a direct `node --import tsx --input-type=module` ACPX resolver probe for source and dist layouts.
3. Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts`.
4. Run `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`.
5. Run `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8`.
6. Run `git diff --check`.
7. Run `codex review --uncommitted`.

### Expected

- The ACPX config resolver and test cover source fallback and built MCP server entries without depending on existing checkout build artifacts.
- The inherited Codex test helper does not contain a no-op type conversion that fails oxlint.

### Actual

- The direct resolver probe printed source and dist outputs for the expected paths, `extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts` passed with 16 tests, formatting passed, and oxlint passed across the same core/extensions/scripts shards used by CI.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: plugin-tools MCP source fallback, plugin-tools MCP built dist selection, OpenClaw tools MCP source fallback, and OpenClaw tools MCP built dist selection via `resolveAcpxPluginConfig`.
- Edge cases checked: temporary layout cleanup runs in `finally`; no real checkout `dist` files are required for the tests.
- What you did **not** verify: full ACPX extension suite, live ACPX process startup, package build, or a local Windows pass of the Codex context-engine test file.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No bot review conversations existed for this PR before creation.

## Compatibility / Migration

Body:
```markdown
## Summary
- add ACPX `codexHome` config plus `OPENCLAW_ACPX_CODEX_HOME` env override for the Codex ACP wrapper
- keep the existing isolated `CODEX_HOME` default when no override is configured
- document the override in the ACP router skill and plugin manifest

## Validation
- `pnpm test extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/acpx/src/config-schema.ts extensions/acpx/src/config.ts extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.test.ts extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts extensions/acpx/openclaw.plugin.json extensions/acpx/skills/acp-router/SKILL.md`
- `pnpm check:test-types`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: ACPX Codex auth now honors an explicit Codex home override instead of always forcing the generated isolated `CODEX_HOME`; this lets operators point the bundled Codex ACP bridge at a known auth/profile directory while keeping the isolated default path unchanged.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Node v22.22.0 and pnpm 10.29.3, repaired PR branch based on current upstream main. Secrets, user identifiers, private paths, and deployment-specific names omitted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Installed dependencies with `pnpm install`.
  2. Ran the ACPX Codex auth/config focused tests.
  3. Ran the full ACPX extension test suite.
  4. Ran changed-file gates and built the repaired branch.
  5. Started the built gateway locally with `node dist/index.js gateway --port <redacted-test-port> --allow-unconfigured` and queried `/health` with `curl`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ pnpm test extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.test.ts extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed
Tests  27 passed

$ pnpm test:extension acpx
Test Files  9 passed
Tests  73 passed

$ pnpm check:changed
completed successfully

$ pnpm build
completed successfully
```

```text
$ node dist/index.js gateway --port <redacted-test-port> --allow-unconfigured
$ curl http://127.0.0.1:<redacted-test-port>/health
{"ok":true,"status":"live"}
```

- Observed result after fix: The repaired ACPX branch preserves the default isolated Codex home behavior and passes the explicit override coverage for `codexHome` / `OPENCLAW_ACPX_CODEX_HOME`; the built gateway also starts and reports live health from the repaired checkout.
- What was not tested: A live authenticated Codex provider session was not exercised; validation was local config/auth-bridge coverage plus built-gateway health smoke.

```

input.title: [Bug]: ACPX Codex worker fails when model/thinking overrides are configured
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #74305: [Bug]: ACPX Codex worker fails when model/thinking overrides are configured
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 74305
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/74305
- Title: [Bug]: ACPX Codex worker fails when model/thinking overrides are configured
- State: OPEN
- Author: SimSef
- Labels: bug, bug:crash

Body:
```markdown
### Bug type

Crash (process/app exits or hangs)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

Codex ACP worker runs succeed without internal model/thinking overrides, but fail with AcpRuntimeError: Internal error when model and model_reasoning_effort overrides are passed to codex-acp.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Start OpenClaw 2026.4.26 with ACPX enabled and Codex configured as an ACP agent.
2. Configure the ACPX Codex command to run codex-acp with -c model=gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort=medium.
3. From an OpenClaw agent, call sessions_spawn with runtime: "acp", agentId: "codex", mode: "run", and streamTo: "parent".
4. Ask the ACP worker to perform a web research task.
5. Observe the spawned ACP task fail with AcpRuntimeError: Internal error.

### Expected behavior

The Codex ACP worker should run successfully with supported model/thinking overrides, or return a specific validation error if those overrides are unsupported.

### Actual behavior

The ACP worker fails with AcpRuntimeError: Internal error; the ACP stream log only records lifecycle start/error events, and no child transcript file is created.

### OpenClaw version

2026.4.26 (be8c246)

### Operating system

Linux 6.17.0-1011-azure x64, Node 22.22.2

### Install method

npm global: /home/azureuser/.npm-global/bin/openclaw

### Model

Override under test: gpt-5.5 with model_reasoning_effort=medium

### Provider / routing chain

OpenClaw agent -> ACPX plugin -> @zed-industries/codex-acp@^0.11.1 -> Codex CLI

### Additional provider/model setup details

ACPX Codex command used shared Codex auth via CODEX_HOME=/home/azureuser/.codex.

ACPX config included permissionMode: "approve-all" and nonInteractivePermissions: "fail".

Direct Codex CLI sanity checks with -c model=gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort=medium succeeded on the same VM/auth setup.

Removing the model/thinking overrides from the ACPX Codex command allowed the ACP worker flow to work again.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Observed OpenClaw log line:

[ws] ⇄ res ✗ agent errorCode=UNAVAILABLE errorMessage=AcpRuntimeError: Internal error runId=f1b71913-b95d-4dea-a691-97596b7af9f6 error=AcpRuntimeError: Internal error: code=ACP_TURN_FAILED

ACP stream log contained only lifecycle start/error events:

phase: start

phase: error

error: Internal error

No child transcript file was created for the failed ACP session.
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: ACPX Codex worker runs where the operator wants to control the child Codex model/thinking level.

Severity: Medium-high, because ACPX works only when the child model/thinking overrides are omitted.

Frequency: Observed on the tested ACPX Codex spawn attempts with model/thinking overrides configured.

Consequence: Operators cannot reliably configure the internal Codex ACP model/thinking level for spawned ACP workers.

### Additional information

The same ACP workflow works after removing -c model=gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort=medium from the ACPX Codex command.
```

Yes for the direction, but the implementation should preserve explicit runtime override precedence. The open PR is the right narrow path to review or replace before this issue closes.

(role: introduced related behavior; confidence: high; commits: [bbfd2c1d1c1c](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/bbfd2c1d1c1c57a3c3ca54d2094ea58a077c5973), [88f0571c8037](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/88f0571c803722a6662c0054c57c9bf97da8e1e5); files: `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts`, `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.test.ts`, `extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts`)

- **SymbolStar:** Opened the focused unmerged PR that targets the duplicate Codex ACP -c override path and references this issue with closing syntax. input.title: fix(acpx): keep oneshot client alive for initial turn
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #81482: fix(acpx): keep oneshot client alive for initial turn
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_pr
- Number: 81482
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/81482
- Title: fix(acpx): keep oneshot client alive for initial turn
- State: OPEN
- Author: moeedahmed
- Labels: channel: matrix, gateway, size: S, extensions: acpx, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof

Body:
```markdown
## Summary
- keep ACPX adapter clients alive across OpenClaw's ensureSession to runTurn boundary for one-shot sessions
- delegate OpenClaw one-shot session creation to ACPX persistent mode while preserving OpenClaw-owned one-shot lifecycle and close behavior
- add regression coverage for the Claude/native ACP handoff shape
- update adjacent guardrail drift exposed by current CI so the branch stays green on latest main

## Why
Native OpenClaw ACP spawns call ensureSession and then runTurn as separate operations. ACPX one-shot mode closes the adapter immediately after session/new, so runTurn has to reconnect/load before any user prompt has been sent. Claude Agent ACP can handle create plus prompt in one process, but the create-close-load-prompt sequence fails with a generic Internal error before producing output.

## Real behavior proof
Before this patch, a native OpenClaw ACP Claude spawn accepted runtime="acp" and created session state, but the first turn failed before assistant output with AcpRuntimeError: Internal error. A direct ACPX baseline still worked from the same machine with /opt/homebrew/bin/acpx --timeout 60 --format json claude exec 'Reply exactly ACP_JSON_OK', proving the adapter/auth path was available and the failure was in the OpenClaw to ACPX handoff.

After this patch, local runtime regression evidence from a real checkout shows the handoff is preserved instead of closing between ensureSession and runTurn:
- node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/acpx/src/runtime.test.ts

GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 44049 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/44049 - Title: [AI-assisted] Fix duplicated acp server args in ACP client - State: OPEN - Author: t0yohei - Labels: size: XS, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, proof: sufficient, P2, rating: 🐚 platinum hermit, status: 👀 ready for maintainer look Body: ```markdown ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: `openclaw acp client` always prefixes the spawned server command with `acp`, so if an upstream caller also passes `serverArgs=["acp", ...]`, the effective command becomes `openclaw acp acp ...` and fails. - Why it matters: this makes ACP client startup brittle and causes session creation to fail for callers that already include the subcommand in forwarded args. - What changed: normalize `serverArgs` in the ACP client by dropping one leading `acp` when present, then prepend the canonical `acp` subcommand exactly once; added a regression test for this case. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): no changes to ACP server behavior, permissions, routing, session semantics, or any non-ACP CLI paths. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [x] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # ## User-visible / Behavior Changes ACP client startup is now tolerant of callers that redundantly include `acp` in forwarded `serverArgs`. Before: `openclaw acp client --server openclaw --server-args acp --server-verbose` could spawn `openclaw acp acp --verbose` and fail. After: the same invocation normalizes to `openclaw acp --verbose` and proceeds to session creation. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: This changes ACP client command construction only by deduping a redundant leading `acp` token in forwarded server args. Risk is low because the change narrows an invalid invocation into the canonical one instead of expanding execution surface. Mitigation: added a regression test covering the duplicated-`acp` case. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: local host runtime - Model/provider: openai-codex/gpt-5.4 - Integration/channel (if any): ACP CLI - Relevant config (redacted): default local OpenClaw CLI setup; no special ACP config required for repro ### Steps 1. Run `openclaw acp client --server openclaw --server-args acp --server-verbose` 2. Observe the spawned server command 3. Attempt session creation ### Expected - The ACP client should spawn the canonical server command once as `openclaw acp --verbose` - Session creation should proceed successfully ### Actual - Before the fix, this could spawn `openclaw acp acp --verbose` - That failed with `error: too many arguments for 'acp'` ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Before: ```text [acp-client] spawning: openclaw acp acp --verbose error: too many arguments for 'acp' ``` After: ```text [acp-client] spawning: openclaw acp --verbose [acp-client] creating session ``` Targeted test: ```text npx vitest run src/acp/client.test.ts ``` ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - Reproduced the failing duplicated-acp invocation locally - Verified the same invocation now spawns openclaw acp --verbose - Verified it reaches session creation successfully - Ran npx vitest run src/acp/client.test.ts - Edge cases checked: - callers that already pass leading acp in serverArgs - existing --server-verbose behavior remains intact after normalization - What you did **not** verify: - full repository green on pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test - broader ACP server behavior outside this argument-normalization path ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (Yes) - Config/env changes? (No) - Migration needed? (No) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR / commit - Files/config to restore: src/acp/client.ts, src/acp/client.test.ts - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: - ACP client spawning malformed commands - regression where valid serverArgs are incorrectly altered ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: overly broad normalization could accidentally rewrite intended server args - Mitigation: normalization only removes a single leading acp; all other args are preserved verbatim - Risk: reviewers may assume the full repository test/build matrix is green - Mitigation: this PR explicitly reports targeted verification only; local pnpm build and pnpm test currently fail due to unrelated existing repository issues outside this change ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-12T12:58:17Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a bug in `buildServerArgs` where a caller already including `"acp"` in forwarded `serverArgs` would cause the spawned command to receive the subcommand twice (e.g. `openclaw acp acp --verbose`). The one-line normalization in `client.ts` is correct and appropriately narrow. The main concern is the regression test: the newly added `"dedupes a leading acp server arg from callers"` test is byte-for-byte identical to the pre-existing `"keeps non-windows invocation unchanged"` test and does **not** cover the fix. Both tests call `resolveAcpClientSpawnInvocation`, which forwards `serverArgs` directly to `materializeWindowsSpawnProgram` without going through `buildServerArgs`. Reverting the fix leaves both tests green, so no real regression guard exists for this change. Key points: - `client.ts` fix is logically sound and properly scoped. - `client.test.ts` regression test is a duplicate of an existing test and provides no coverage of the actual `buildServerArgs` change. - To truly guard against regression, `buildServerArgs` should be exported and unit-tested, or `createAcpClient` should be tested end-to-end with a mocked spawn.

Confidence Score: 3/5

- The production fix is correct and low-risk, but the claimed regression test does not exercise the fix, leaving the behavior unguarded. - The change to `buildServerArgs` is narrowly scoped and logically correct. Score is reduced because the regression test is a no-op duplicate — it calls `resolveAcpClientSpawnInvocation` (which bypasses `buildServerArgs`), so the fix could be silently reverted without any test failing. The PR description explicitly states the full test/build matrix is not green, which adds minor additional uncertainty. - `src/acp/client.test.ts` needs attention — the new test must be rewritten to actually invoke `buildServerArgs` or `createAcpClient` to provide meaningful regression coverage.
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/acp/client.test.ts Line: 155-170 Comment: **Regression test does not exercise the fix** The new test is byte-for-byte identical to the existing `"keeps non-windows invocation unchanged"` test (same input, same expected output, same platform) and does **not** cover the `buildServerArgs` deduplication introduced in `client.ts`. The reason is that `resolveAcpClientSpawnInvocation` (what both tests call) receives `serverArgs` as a plain parameter and forwards it directly to `materializeWindowsSpawnProgram` — it never calls `buildServerArgs`. The deduplication logic lives in `buildServerArgs`, which is only exercised by `createAcpClient`. Concretely, if you revert the two-line change in `buildServerArgs`, this test still passes (as does the original test). The fix is therefore untested. To actually cover the regression, the test should either: 1. Export `buildServerArgs` (or an internal helper) and unit-test it, e.g.: ```ts // src/acp/client.ts — export for testing export function buildServerArgs(opts: AcpClientOptions): string[] { ... } ``` ```ts it("dedupes a leading acp server arg from callers", () => { expect(buildServerArgs({ serverArgs: ["acp", "--verbose"] })) .toEqual(["acp", "--verbose"]); // without leading acp is unchanged expect(buildServerArgs({ serverArgs: ["--verbose"] })) .toEqual(["acp", "--verbose"]); }); ``` 2. Or mock `spawn`/`resolveAcpClientSpawnInvocation` and call `createAcpClient` end-to-end, asserting on the args that reach the spawn call. As-is, the regression can silently re-appear without any test failing. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

input.github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 46949 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/46949 - Title: fix(acp): release dormant oneshot runtimes under pressure - State: OPEN - Author: lanyasheng - Labels: size: L, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, merge-risk: 🚨 availability, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary - release cached oneshot runtime handles after startup identity reconciliation - evict dormant oneshot ACP runtimes before stale-status probing under concurrent session pressure - add regression coverage for pending-identity oneshot sessions and pressure eviction of idle oneshot handles ## Problem OpenClaw could report `ACP max concurrent sessions reached (13/12)` even when ACPX session registry and process state looked clean. In our repro, dormant oneshot sessions could remain counted inside the ACP manager runtime cache, especially after startup identity reconciliation or quick oneshot completion. ## Root cause The ACP session manager only reclaimed obviously stale runtimes under pressure. Oneshoot handles that were no longer actively running could still remain cached and count toward `acp.maxConcurrentSessions`. ## Fix 1. During `reconcilePendingSessionIdentities()`, if the reconciled session is `mode: "oneshot"`, always `runtime.close()` it and clear the cached handle. 2. Before the existing stale-runtime probe path, evict cached oneshot handles whose persisted ACP metadata is no longer `running`. 3. Keep the existing stale probing as a fallback for genuinely stale persistent runtimes. ## Tests - `pnpm exec vitest run src/acp/control-plane/*.test.ts` - 34 passed ## Notes This patch addresses the manager-side leak/counting path. per_label_rationale.label: acp
per_label_rationale.rationale: Central subject is a node-backed ACP runtime: ACP sessions/runs, worker events, protocol plumbing, and delivery/replay semantics.

per_label_rationale.label: gateway
per_label_rationale.rationale: Gateway owns the durable ACP state, gateway↔node command plumbing, startup recovery, and gateway-owned execution lifecycle.

per_label_rationale.label: reliability
per_label_rationale.rationale: Restart/reconnect/cancel/close race hardening, crash recovery, durable replay, and stale-worker/duplicate-delivery prevention are core deliverables.

per_label_rationale.label: sessions
per_label_rationale.rationale: Adds a durable store persisting ACP sessions and runs, leases, terminal state, and checkpoints with restart recovery — session persistence/lifecycle is central.

excluded_label_rationale.label: notifications
excluded_label_rationale.rationale: Outward delivery replay is hardened, but the change is framed as durable recovery (reliability) of ACP delivery rather than a new notify path/gate; boundary candidate.

excluded_label_rationale.label: security
excluded_label_rationale.rationale: Trust-boundary and node-command-policy notes appear and reviewers flag authorization, but the central deliverable is the runtime, not a security control change.

excluded_label_rationale.label: acpx
excluded_label_rationale.rationale: This is ACP node-backed runtime, not ACPX-specific transport/worker surfaces.

excluded_label_rationale.label: docs
excluded_label_rationale.rationale: Docs are included as supporting plan artifacts, not the subject of the PR.

ambiguity.why_not_hard: Core owners (ACP runtime, gateway-owned durable state, reliability/recovery) are explicit and stable; only boundary co-labels (notifications/security/sessions split) are debatable.

GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 48940 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/48940 - Title: ACP: add gateway-owned node-backed runtime - State: OPEN - Author: jorgensandhaug - Labels: docs, channel: googlechat, gateway, channel: feishu, size: XL, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery, merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary - Problem: OpenClaw did not yet have a complete **node-backed ACP runtime** where the gateway owns the durable state and a paired node acts only as the execution worker. - Why it matters: without that split, restart/replay/reconnect behavior is too dependent on the live process, which makes lease fencing, terminal resolution, and outward delivery recovery harder to reason about and prove. - What this PR adds: - a durable ACP gateway store for sessions, runs, accepted worker events, leases, terminal state, replay targets, and projector checkpoints - gateway ingestion of ACP worker events coming back from paired nodes - a real node-host ACP worker bridge and gateway/node command plumbing - a durable ACP reply projection/replay path so restart recovery can resume from gateway state instead of only from the live iterator - startup recovery for incomplete ACP node runs - restart/reconnect/cancel/close race hardening with focused restart/replay test coverage - architecture / protocol / verification docs for the new backend - Landing-only follow-up: after rebasing onto current `main`, this PR also includes a few **test-only** isolation fixes in Feishu / Google / Google Chat extension tests that were needed to make the landing branch green. Those are not ACP runtime behavior changes. ## What changed by area ### 1) Durable ACP gateway state New ACP store/state plumbing under `src/acp/store/**`: - persists ACP sessions and runs - persists accepted worker events - tracks leases / lease epochs - resolves canonical terminal state on the gateway - stores replay targets and projector checkpoints separately from the runtime cursor This is the foundation that lets the gateway stay authoritative even when execution happens on a paired node. ### 2) Gateway ↔ node ACP worker path Main implementation areas: - `src/gateway/**` - `src/infra/node-commands.ts` - `src/node-host/**` This adds the node-backed ACP execution path: - gateway can start/control ACP work on a paired node - node-host can run the ACP worker role - worker events stream back to the gateway over the existing node transport - gateway remains the source of truth for run state and terminal resolution ### 3) Durable reply projection and replay Main implementation areas: - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-replay.ts` This PR moves ACP outward delivery onto a durable replayable path: - reply targets are persisted per run - projector checkpoints are advanced only after successful delivery - restart recovery can replay the unfinished suffix from durable state - reconnect and duplicate accepted events do not re-deliver already completed outward effects ### 4) Startup recovery / reconnect handling Main implementation areas: - `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.ts` - `src/gateway/server-node-events.ts` - `src/acp/runtime/acp-node.ts` This adds the gateway-side recovery flow needed to: - resume incomplete projection after restart - keep reconnect handling deterministic - prevent stale-worker wins - preserve canonical terminal behavior across restart/replay windows ### 5) Test harnesses and regression coverage Main test additions/expansions: - `src/acp/store/store.restart.test.ts` - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.replay.test.ts` - `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.test.ts` - `src/gateway/server-node-events.acp.test.ts` - `src/node-host/invoke-acp.test.ts` - `src/acp/test-harness/restart-harness.ts` These cover the core failure windows this backend needs to survive: - restart after accepted events but before replay checkpoint advancement - restart after terminal persistence but before final outward convergence - reconnect replay of already accepted events - cancel / complete / close race handling - restart recovery with a real memory cut instead of reusing in-process objects ### 6) Docs included in this PR Main docs: - `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime.md` - `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime-protocol.md` - `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime-verification.md` - `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-runtime-program.md` - `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-slice4-summary.md` - `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-slice4-final-judge.md` These are included because this was developed as a staged architecture / verification effort and the final accepted behavior is documented there. ## Explicit scope boundaries / v1 limitations This PR does **not**: - turn nodes into mini-gateways - add automatic cross-node continuation for an in-flight run - claim durable resumed tool-message edit support after restart Current conservative post-restart tool-update behavior remains: - `append_only_after_restart` ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor - [x] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [x] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [x] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [x] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related: ACP node runtime architecture / protocol / verification docs under `docs/experiments/plans/` - Related: landing-only extension test isolation fixes after rebase onto current `main` ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - OpenClaw now has a first-class ACP node-backed runtime path where the gateway owns durable ACP truth and paired nodes execute the worker role. - Gateway restart/recovery can resume incomplete ACP outward projection from durable state. - Duplicate accepted worker-event replay on reconnect is hardened so it does not duplicate outward delivery. - Post-restart tool updates intentionally follow the documented conservative v1 rule: `append_only_after_restart`. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? Yes - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? Yes - Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes - Data access scope changed? Yes ### Security / trust-boundary notes - Paired nodes can now advertise ACP worker capability and receive ACP runtime commands over the existing gateway↔node trust boundary. - Gateway↔node websocket traffic now carries ACP worker control / event flows in addition to existing node commands. - Gateway now persists ACP runtime state needed for durable replay/recovery. - The design mitigates stale-worker / duplicate-delivery risks via lease epochs, canonical terminal resolution, durable replay checkpoints, and reconnect/replay handling on the gateway. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux (landing worktree on vm1) + GitHub Actions CI - Runtime/container: Node / pnpm; Bun build path also checked - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): ACP gateway↔node runtime path - Relevant config (redacted): ACP runtime restart/replay harnesses and paired-node fixtures ### Verified locally - ACP targeted verification gate: - `pnpm test -- src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.replay.test.ts src/acp/store/store.test.ts src/acp/store/store.restart.test.ts src/acp/store/gateway-events.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.acp.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-acp.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/nodes.acp.test.ts` - TypeScript guardrail: - `pnpm tsgo` - Landing-only extension test fixes: - `pnpm test:extensions` ### CI / build notes - `pnpm build` still fails only with the pre-existing Bun wrapper issue: - `Cannot find module './cjs/index.cjs' from ''` - That build failure was already present and is not introduced by the ACP runtime changes in this PR. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What I personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified the ACP-focused local gate listed above. - Verified `pnpm tsgo` passes. - Verified the rebased landing branch’s extension test-isolation fixes with local `pnpm test:extensions`. - Verified the PR now includes the architecture / protocol / verification / final-judge docs for this backend. What I did **not** verify: - I did **not** get a clean `pnpm build` because of the pre-existing Bun wrapper failure noted above. - I did **not** run a fresh manual end-to-end Discord/real-node interactive repro outside the checked-in restart/replay and node-host harnesses. ## Review Guide If you want the shortest path through this PR, review in this order: 1. **Core architecture / intent** - `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime.md` - `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime-protocol.md` 2. **Gateway-owned durable state** - `src/acp/store/**` 3. **Node execution bridge** - `src/node-host [truncated] ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - jorgensandhaug at 2026-03-17T10:54:42Z: /landpr - greptile-apps at 2026-03-17T10:59:50Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR lands the ACP node runtime (slices 1–4), wiring the gateway-side durable store, lease lifecycle, turn delivery, and crash-recovery replay paths to a real node-host worker bridge. The architecture is well thought-out: the gateway store persists every event and checkpoint to disk, the `AcpDurableProjectionRunner` replays from that store on restart, and a layered prepared/pending/settled synthetic-final checkpoint scheme provides at-least-once TTS delivery with transcript-backed idempotency. Key observations from the review: - **Dead code in `src/gateway/server-node-events.ts`** (line 268): The `if (handled) { return; }` guard immediately follows a `if (!handled) { throw ... }` block, making it unreachable. Should simply be `return;`. - **`isRecord` inconsistency in `src/acp/store/gateway-events.ts`** (line 50): The helper does not exclude arrays (`!Array.isArray(value)`), unlike the identical helper in `src/acp/runtime/acp-node.ts`. Downstream validators still reject the payload, but with a misleading field-level error rather than "must decode to an object". - The `recordSettledSyntheticFinalCheckpoint` fallback chain in `dispatch-acp-replay.ts` is intentionally complex and is correctly designed for crash recovery. - Error-swallowing in `dispatch-acp.ts` (swallowing `turnError` when the durable run has a "failed" terminal) is intentional and correct — the durable projection service handles delivery of the error message. - The `await Promise.resolve()` microtask yield in `invoke-acp.ts` before checking for a concurrent turn completion is an acceptable pattern for this use case.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Safe to merge; the two flagged issues are minor style/consistency problems with no runtime impact. - The core logic — durable store, lease lifecycle, event ingestion, turn execution, and crash-recovery replay — is well-structured and covered by a thorough test suite. Two minor issues were found: dead code in `server-node-events.ts` (the `if (handled)` check is always true after the throw guard) and a missing `!Array.isArray` in `gateway-events.ts`'s `isRecord` helper. Neither affects runtime behavior in practice, so the PR is safe to merge with those cleanups addressed. - src/gateway/server-node-events.ts (dead code), src/acp/store/gateway-events.ts (isRecord inconsistency)
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/gateway/server-node-events.ts Line: 268-270 Comment: **Unreachable `if (handled)` guard** The `if (handled) { return; }` block is dead code. The preceding `if (!handled) { throw ... }` means that if execution reaches line 268, `handled` is guaranteed to be `true` — the condition is always met and the `if` check is redundant. ```suggestion return; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/acp/store/gateway-events.ts Line: 50-52 Comment: **`isRecord` is inconsistent with sibling implementation — arrays pass the check** The `isRecord` helper here does not exclude arrays, whereas the identical helper in `src/acp/runtime/acp-node.ts` (line 50) does: ```ts // acp-node.ts return typeof value === "object" && value !== null && !Array.isArray(value); ``` Without the `!Array.isArray(value)` guard, a well-formed JSON array sent by a misbehaving worker node would pass `isRecord` and silently proceed into `parseBaseEnvelope` / `parseInvokePayloadObject`. Downstream field validators (`requireString`, etc.) will still reject the payload, but with a confusing "field must be a non-empty string" error instead of the clearer "must decode to an object" error. Keeping both implementations consistent is worth the one-liner fix. ```suggestion function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record { return typeof value === "object" && value !== null && !Array.isArray(value); } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

per_label_rationale.label: notifications
per_label_rationale.rationale: The change implements the outbound delivery gate: final_only mode now defers delivery to a single turn-level flush instead of prematurely delivering on each done, changing the completion-delivery path.

excluded_label_rationale.rationale: Pre-tool text and pendingToolDeliveries are preserved, but the central concern is text delivery accumulation, not tool-call protocol/schema/rendering.

excluded_label_rationale.label: reliability
excluded_label_rationale.rationale: Text-loss bug motivates it, but the deliverable is delivery-mode semantics, not a generic crash/leak/recovery surface.

excluded_label_rationale.rationale: No ACPX-specific worker/transport/binding; this is generic ACP delivery.
