input.title: fix(acp): preserve pre-tool text in final_only delivery mode
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #84509: fix(acp): preserve pre-tool text in final_only delivery mode
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_pr
- Number: 84509
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84509
- Title: fix(acp): preserve pre-tool text in final_only delivery mode
- State: OPEN
- Author: Jerry-Xin
- Labels: size: S, proof: supplied, proof: sufficient, P2, rating: 🐚 platinum hermit, merge-risk: 🚨 automation, merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery, status: 👀 ready for maintainer look

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

In `final_only` delivery mode (used by Feishu, QQ Bot, and other channels that prefer a single consolidated reply), text output that precedes tool calls within the same turn is lost. Only the post-tool final response reaches the user.

**Root cause**: In `acp-projector.ts`, the `onEvent` handler calls `flush(true)` + `resetTurnState()` on every `done` event, including intermediate ones (e.g. `stopReason=toolUse`). This prematurely delivers accumulated text and then clears the `finalOnlyOutputText` buffer. When the model outputs more text after tool execution, it arrives as a separate "final" delivery — and channels like Feishu that replace the previous card only show the last one.

**Fix**: In `final_only` mode, intermediate `done` events now call `resetTurnState({ preserveAccumulated: true })`, which resets per-completion state (timers, dedup hashes, pipelines) but preserves all turn-scoped accumulated state:
- `finalOnlyOutputText` and `pendingToolDeliveries` — the text/tool content being assembled
- `emittedOutputChars` and `truncationNoticeEmitted` — output limit enforcement across the entire turn
- `pendingHiddenBoundary` and `lastVisibleOutputTail` — hidden-boundary separator state for correct paragraph breaks after hidden tool calls

The turn-level `flush(true)` in `dispatch-acp.ts` delivers everything as one combined message after the entire turn completes.

Fixes #84486.
Also related to #84491.

## Changes

- `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` — Parameterized `resetTurnState()` with `preserveAccumulated` option; `final_only` + `done` now uses partial reset preserving all accumulated turn state
- `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts` — 5 new tests + 1 updated existing test

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts
 ✓ auto-reply  ../../src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts (28 tests) 27ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  28 passed (28)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ auto-reply  ../../src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts (50 tests) 34ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  50 passed (50)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: In `final_only` delivery mode, text output before tool calls within a multi-step agentic turn is lost because `flush(true)` + `resetTurnState()` fires on intermediate `done` events, prematurely delivering and then clearing the `finalOnlyOutputText` buffer. Additionally, `maxOutputChars` enforcement and hidden-boundary separators were reset per-completion instead of being enforced across the entire turn.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `c8e921de4b`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: Applied the new test assertions to unpatched `upstream/main` projector code. Four tests fail, confirming all three bugs:

```
 ❯ supports deliveryMode=final_only by buffering all projected output until done
AssertionError: expected [ { kind: 'tool', …(1) }, …(2) ] to strictly equal []
    → On main, projector delivers text+tools immediately on intermediate "done"
      instead of deferring to the turn-level flush.

 ❯ accumulates text across intermediate done events in final_only mode
AssertionError: expected [ { kind: 'final', …(1) } ] to strictly equal []
    → On main, "Searching..." is delivered as a premature "final" on the first
      "done" event. Subsequent text becomes a second "final", and channels like
      Feishu overwrite the first message.

 ❯ enforces maxOutputChars across intermediate done events
AssertionError: expected 20 to be less than or equal to 20
    → On main, emittedOutputChars resets to 0 on each intermediate "done", so
      each completion gets a full maxOutputChars budget. A turn with N completions
      can produce up to N × maxOutputChars of text.

 ❯ preserves hidden-boundary separator across intermediate done events
AssertionError: expected "Before tool.After tool." to match /Before tool\.\n\nAfter tool\./
    → On main, pendingHiddenBoundary is cleared on intermediate "done", so the
      paragraph separator between pre-tool text and post-tool text is lost.

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
       Tests  4 failed | 24 passed (28)
```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
git checkout fix/final-only-text-before-tools-84486
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ auto-reply  ../../src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts (28 tests) 27ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  28 passed (28)

 ✓ auto-reply  ../../src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts (50 tests) 34ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  50 passed (50)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The projector correctly accumulates `finalOnlyOutputText` across intermediate `done` events without flushing. Output limit (`maxOutputChars`) is enforced across the entire turn. Hidden-boundary separators are correctly inserted between pre-tool and post-tool text segments. Only the explicit turn-level `flush(true)` call delivers the combined text as a single "final" message.

- **What was not tested**: End-to-end verification with a live Feishu or QQ Bot channel was not performed (requires deployed instance with configured channel credentials). The fix is validated at the projector unit-test level, which directly exercises the buggy code paths. The `dispatch-acp.test.ts` regression suite (50 tests) confirms no side effects on other delivery modes.
## Docker real behavior proof (v2) — pre-tool text preserved

### Environment

- **Docker image**: `node:24-bookworm-slim` (Node.js v24.15.0)
- **Container ID**: `a1a700939bfc` running patched OpenClaw gateway at commit `d170b97957`
- **Platform**: Docker on macOS arm64 (OrbStack)
- **LLM model**: `claude-sonnet-4-6` via `mlamp-gateway` (Anthropic Messages API)
- **ACP delivery mode**: `final_only` (default)

### Setup

```bash
docker run -d --name openclaw-test-84486 \
  -v /tmp/openclaw-analysis:/app \
  -v /tmp/openclaw-docker-test/openclaw.json:/root/.openclaw/openclaw.json \
  -p 18799:18799 \
  -e NODE_ENV=production \
  -e OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD=*** \
  node:24-bookworm-slim \
  node /app/openclaw.mjs gateway --bind lan --port 18799
```

Gateway startup:
```
[gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, ...)
[gateway] ready
```

### Test: pre-tool text + tool call + post-tool text

System prompt instructs the model to **always explain before using tools**:

```bash
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer ***" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  http://localhost:18799/v1/chat/completions \
  -d '{
    "model": "openclaw/test-final-only",
    "messages": [
      {"role": "system", "content": "IMPORTANT: You MUST always explain what you are about to do BEFORE calling any tool. First output a brief sentence explaining your plan, then call the tool, then report the result after the tool completes. Never go straight to tool use without explaining first."},
      {"role": "user", "content": "Run the shell command: echo proof_84486_pretool_text"}
    ],
    "stream": true
  }'
```

### Streamed SSE response (complete)

```
data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"role":"assistant"},"finish_reason":null}]}
data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"content":"I"},"finish_reason":null}]}
data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"content":"'ll run that command"},"finish_reason":null}]}
data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"content":" now"},"finish_reason":null}]}
data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"content":"."},"finish_reason":null}]}
data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"content":"The command ran"},"finish_reason":null}]}
data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"content":" successfully and output: `proof_84486_pretool_text`"},"finish_reason":null}]}
data: {"choices":[{"delta":{},"finish_reason":"stop"}]}
data: [DONE]
```

### Session transcript (from container filesystem)

```
[user]       text: "Run the shell command: echo proof_84486_pretool_text"
[assistant]  text: "I'll run that command now."           ← PRE-TOOL TEXT
[toolResult] text: "proof_84486_pretool_text"             ← TOOL EXECUTED
[assistant]  text: "The command ran successfully and output: `proof_84486_pretool_text`"  ← POST-TOOL TEXT
```

### Evidence this proves the fix

1. The model output **pre-tool text** ("I'll run that command now.") BEFORE calling the exec tool
2. The model output **post-tool text** ("The command ran successfully...") AFTER the tool completed
3. In `final_only` mode, **both texts were delivered as a single consolidated SSE stream** — no premature flush, no split messages
4. **On unpatched `upstream/main`**: the intermediate `done` event after the first completion (stopReason=toolUse) would have called `flush(true)` + `resetTurnState()`, delivering "I'll run that command now." as a premature `kind="final"` delivery and clearing the buffer. The post-tool text would arrive as a s

[truncated]
```

input.title: Tasks: mark stale ACP zombie sessions lost during maintenance
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #64616: Tasks: mark stale ACP zombie sessions lost during maintenance
input.github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 64616 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/64616 - Title: Tasks: mark stale ACP zombie sessions lost during maintenance - State: OPEN - Author: shad0wca7 - Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary - detect stale ACP task records whose ACP metadata still reports `running` but whose session transcript file is gone - treat those ACP zombie sessions as missing backing sessions during task-registry maintenance - add regression coverage for both the zombie and still-live ACP cases ## Testing - `npx vitest run src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.issue-60299.test.ts` - `npx vitest run src/commands/status.test.ts` ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-11T03:44:20Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds zombie-session detection for ACP tasks whose metadata still reports `state: "running"` but whose transcript `sessionFile` has gone missing (i.e., sessions killed during maintenance without a clean shutdown). Stale ACP entries older than 7 days with no `sessionFile` are now treated as missing backing sessions and marked `lost`. Two regression tests cover the zombie and live-session paths. The zombie detection logic is correct and the test coverage is solid. Two structural issues worth cleaning up: the `hasZombieAcpBackingSession` call inside `shouldMarkLost` (after the `hasBackingSession` guard) is dead code because `hasBackingSession` already handles the ACP zombie case internally, and `readAcpSessionEntry` is invoked twice per `hasBackingSession` call for ACP tasks.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — zombie detection is correct; two structural redundancies are P2 style issues only. All findings are P2: the dead `hasZombieAcpBackingSession` call in `shouldMarkLost` produces no behavioral difference, and the double `readAcpSessionEntry` read is a performance micro-issue with no correctness impact. The core logic, type usage, and test coverage are sound. src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.ts — redundant zombie check in `shouldMarkLost` and double ACP entry read in `hasBackingSession`.

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.ts`, line 136-149 ([link](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/94531be83bacfc738194bb0ba6ea96e3329aa66f/src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.ts#L136-L149)) **Redundant `readAcpSessionEntry` read inside `hasBackingSession`** `hasBackingSession` reads the ACP entry at line 137, then calls `hasZombieAcpBackingSession` at line 146, which re-reads the same ACP entry via an independent call to `taskRegistryMaintenanceRuntime.readAcpSessionEntry` (line 177). This results in two disk reads for the same session entry on every `hasBackingSession` invocation for ACP tasks. Consider passing the already-fetched `acpEntry` into `hasZombieAcpBackingSession` as a parameter, or inlining the zombie check using the entry already in scope, to avoid the double read.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 63793 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/63793 - Title: fix(acp): fail fast on dead spawns and recover stale disconnects - State: OPEN - Author: eyev0 - Labels: docs, size: L, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🦐 gold shrimp, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 availability, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets: If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(): beta blocker - ` and link the matching `Beta blocker: - ` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation. - Problem: ACP sessions could report a successful `/acp spawn` even when the newly initialized runtime handle was already unusable, and stale gateway disconnects could leave cancel/close/early-turn recovery stuck on a bad cached handle. - Why it matters: Operators can see contradictory UX (`Spawned` followed by immediate `ACP_TURN_FAILED`) and then lose reliable recovery controls for the affected ACP session. - What changed: Probe the freshly initialized runtime status before reporting spawn success; clean up failed post-spawn sessions; treat explicit stale gateway disconnect close codes as recoverable for close, cancel, active-turn cancel, and early-turn retry. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No ACP protocol/schema changes, no new public commands, no auth/provider changes, no gateway restart policy changes, and no broad retry policy for non-stale gateway errors. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # (none) - Related #63656 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: Spawn success was emitted after `initializeSession` without verifying the new runtime handle could answer an immediate status check, and the ACP manager only treated `acpx exited ...` as a stale runtime-handle recovery signal. - Missing detection / guardrail: There was no spawn-time liveliness guard, no regression for gateway-disconnect stale-handle recovery, and no active-turn cancel test for stale disconnect cleanup. - Contributing context (if known): ACP transport disconnects surface as `Gateway disconnected: 1006` or `gateway closed (...)`; some close codes indicate stale transport, while others such as `1008` represent real authorization/pairing failures. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: - `src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts` - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-acp.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: - `/acp spawn` fails and cleans up when the immediate status probe reports `Gateway disconnected: 1006`. - Spawn health checks call direct runtime status and do not depend on capability lookup. - Close, passive cancel, active-turn cancel, and early-turn retry recover known stale gateway disconnect close codes. - Non-stale gateway closures such as `1008 pairing required` are surfaced instead of swallowed. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior is localized to ACP command lifecycle and `AcpSessionManager` runtime-handle recovery, so mocked runtime tests can deterministically exercise every branch without a live ACP backend. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing ACP manager tests covered equivalent `acpx exited ...` recovery and normal active-turn cancel; this PR extends that pattern to gateway disconnect errors and post-spawn health checks. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - `/acp spawn` no longer reports success when the freshly initialized ACP runtime immediately fails status with a gateway disconnect; it returns the ACP error and cleans up the failed session. - `/acp cancel` and `/acp close` can recover stale gateway-disconnected runtime handles for explicit stale/disconnect close codes. - First ACP turns that fail immediately with a stale gateway disconnect get one fresh-handle retry. ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text Before: /acp spawn -> initializeSession OK -> "Spawned" -> first prompt -> ACP_TURN_FAILED -> cancel/close can stay stuck on stale cached handle After: /acp spawn -> initializeSession OK -> direct runtime status probe -> fail? cleanup + ACP error -> pass? bind/report spawn success stale gateway disconnect during close/cancel/early first turn -> clear cached handle -> recover or retry once ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS local checkout - Runtime/container: Node.js via repo `pnpm` lanes - Model/provider: ACP runtime mocked in regression tests - Integration/channel (if any): ACP slash command lifecycle - Relevant config (redacted): ACP enabled with `acpx` backend ### Steps 1. Spawn an ACP session while the freshly initialized runtime status path fails with `Gateway disconnected: 1006`. 2. Attempt cancel/close against an ACP session whose cached runtime handle is stale from a gateway disconnect. 3. Attempt an early first turn that fails before output with `Gateway disconnected: 1006`. ### Expected - Spawn fails immediately with cleanup instead of reporting false success. - Recoverable stale disconnects clear the cached handle and allow recovery/fresh-handle retry. - Non-stale gateway errors remain visible. ### Actual - Before this fix, spawn could report success before immediate turn failure, and stale gateway disconnects were not covered by the existing stale-runtime recovery handling. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Validation: - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config vitest.config.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-acp.test.ts` - `3` files passed, `166` tests passed. - `pnpm check:changed` - Stopped during the final broad e2e shard because the local changed-gate plan selected 293 targets. Completed before stop: core typecheck, core test typecheck, lint, import-cycle checks, gateway shard (`91` files / `990` tests), auto-reply shard (`38` files / `494` tests), agents shard (`41` files / `746` tests), and the smaller ACP/plugin/CLI/commands shards shown in local output. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - Spawn-health failure returns the ACP error, avoids binding, closes the runtime, and deletes the failed session. - Spawn-health success path does not call capability lookup. - Stale gateway disconnect recovery clears cached handles for close, passive cancel, active-turn cancel, and early-turn retry. - Edge cases checked: - Recoverable gateway disconnects are limited to explicit stale/disconnect close codes. - `1008 pairing required` is not treated as recoverable. - What you did **not** verify: - Full live ACP backend replay in this PR cycle. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: A post-spawn status probe can reject a session that is already unstable immediately after initialization. - Mitigation: This is intentional fail-fast behavior to avoid false-success spawns; failed sessions are cleaned up. - Risk: Gateway disconnect recovery could hide real gateway failures if classified too broadly. - Mitigation: Recovery is limited to an explicit stale/disconnect close-code allowlist, with negative tests for `1008 pairing required`. ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-09T15:10:45Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds two complementary safety improvements to the ACP session lifecycle: a post-spawn health check in `/acp spawn` that calls `getSessionStatus` immediately after `initializeSession` and cleans up + fails fast if the session is already dead, and stale `Gateway disconnected` error recovery in `cancelSession`/`closeSession`/early-turn retry paths (mirroring the existing `acpx exited` recoverable-error treatment). A new private `isRecoverableGatewayDisconnectError` helper centralizes the pattern-matching for these errors. Test coverage is added for all four new code paths: post-spawn health failure, close recovery, cancel recovery (passive path), and early-turn retry.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the changes are additive error-recovery paths consistent with existing patterns, with good test coverage for all new scenarios. No P0 or P1 issues found. The isRecoverableGatewayDisconnectError regex correctly matches the observed error format. The post-spawn health check, cancel/close recovery, and early-turn retry are all logically sound and symmetric with the existing acpx exited treatment. Tests cover all four new code paths. No files require special attention.

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. The change adds health-checking and error recovery paths; it does not introduce new network calls, permissions, secrets handling, or command-execution surfaces.

GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 43573 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/43573 - Title: fix(acp): persist initial sessions_spawn task for ACP sessions - State: OPEN - Author: hhhhao28 - Labels: commands, agents, size: XL, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 automation, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–4 bullets: - Problem: `sessions_spawn(..., runtime: "acp")` could create an ACP session successfully, but the initial `task` was not written to transcript/history until a later assistant reply landed. - Why it matters: a freshly spawned ACP session could appear to have empty history immediately after spawn, making the initial task look missing. - What changed: seed the ACP transcript with the initial spawn task as soon as the session is created, pass `inputProvenance` through the first ACP dispatch, and dedupe the later persisted prompt so the same task is not stored twice. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): non-ACP spawn flows and normal post-turn transcript persistence semantics are unchanged. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [x] Memory / storage - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #43518 - Related # ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - ACP sessions spawned via `sessions_spawn` now show the initial task in transcript/history immediately, before the first assistant reply arrives. - The later ACP turn no longer duplicates that same seeded prompt in transcript/history. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Windows - Runtime/container: local pnpm workspace - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): ACP runtime via `sessions_spawn` - Relevant config (redacted): `runtime: "acp"` ### Steps 1. Spawn a session with `sessions_spawn` using `runtime: "acp"` and an initial `task`. 2. Inspect the spawned session transcript/history immediately after spawn, before any assistant reply lands. 3. Let the first ACP turn complete and inspect the transcript again. ### Expected - The initial task is visible in transcript/history immediately after spawn. - The later ACP turn does not append a duplicate copy of the same prompt. ### Actual - Before this fix, the spawned ACP session could appear to have empty history until the first assistant reply arrived. - The initial task only showed up later, which made the spawn task look missing. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - ACP spawn writes the initial `task` into transcript/history immediately. - The first ACP turn does not append a duplicate copy of the seeded prompt. - The same prompt text with different provenance still persists as separate entries. - Edge cases checked: - Session-manager init no longer wipes intentionally pre-seeded prompt-only transcripts. - What you did **not** verify: - I did not rely on the repo's `pnpm build` wrapper on this Windows machine because the local `bash` shim is broken; CI should cover the standard entrypoint. Local verification run: - `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts src/acp/session-transcript.test.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts` - `pnpm check` ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR/commit. - Files/config to restore: ACP transcript persistence behavior only; no config or migration rollback is required. - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: duplicated initial prompts in ACP transcripts, or spawned ACP sessions still showing empty history immediately after spawn. ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: seeding the spawn prompt could cause the later ACP turn to persist a duplicate message. - Mitigation: transcript persistence now dedupes by normalized text plus provenance, with regression coverage. - Risk: prompt-only transcripts seeded at spawn time could be wiped during session-manager initialization. - Mitigation: session-manager init now preserves intentionally seeded ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-12T01:45:29Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes a bug where freshly spawned ACP sessions via `sessions_spawn` appeared to have empty history until the first assistant reply arrived. The fix seeds the session transcript with the initial spawn task immediately at spawn time, passes `inputProvenance` through to the first ACP dispatch, and deduplicates the later persisted prompt so it isn't stored twice. Key changes: - New `src/acp/session-transcript.ts` module centralises ACP transcript persistence, extracting the previous inline implementation from `commands/agent.ts` and adding `persistAcpPromptTranscript` for seeding at spawn time. - `session-manager-init.ts` now preserves prompt-only transcripts during init (guards the file-reset on `!hasMessages` instead of only `!hasAssistant`), preventing the seeded entry from being wiped. - `acp-spawn.ts` calls `persistAcpPromptTranscript` after a successful session creation and forwards `inputProvenance` to the agent dispatch. - Two concerns in `forcePersistPromptOnlyTranscript`: it accesses `isPersisted` and `_rewriteFile` from `SessionManager` via an unsafe cast with optional chaining, so if the library's private API changes the function silently no-ops without any warning; additionally `_rewriteFile` is invoked without `await`, which could cause a race condition if the underlying implementation is async.

Confidence Score: 3/5

- Safe to merge with awareness of the private-API coupling in forcePersistPromptOnlyTranscript, which silently no-ops if the library internals change. - The overall approach is sound — the dedup logic, provenance forwarding, and session-manager-init guard are all correct. However, the core persistence mechanism for prompt-only transcripts (forcePersistPromptOnlyTranscript) relies on private/undocumented internal methods of @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent via unsafe casting with optional chaining. If those methods are absent or renamed, the function silently does nothing and the fix regresses without any error signal. Additionally, the _rewriteFile call is not awaited despite likely being async file I/O. These issues don't break existing functionality but could cause the new feature to silently stop working after a library update. - src/acp/session-transcript.ts — specifically the forcePersistPromptOnlyTranscript function (lines 191–204).
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/acp/session-transcript.ts Line: 191-204 Comment: **Silent failure when library private API is absent** `forcePersistPromptOnlyTranscript` reaches into the `SessionManager` instance via an `as unknown as { isPersisted?: () => boolean; _rewriteFile?: () => void }` cast and relies on optional chaining to call `_rewriteFile`. This means if the `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` library ever renames, removes, or makes these private members non-enumerable, the entire persistence path silently no-ops — the function returns without writing anything, and the initial spawn task disappears from transcript again without any error or warning. The guard `if (!manager.isPersisted?.())` compounds the risk: when `isPersisted` is not present on the object, `manager.isPersisted?.()` evaluates to `undefined` (falsy), so `!undefined` is `true` and the function exits immediately, skipping the `_rewriteFile` call entirely. This is precisely the failure mode the fix is meant to prevent. If `_rewriteFile` must be called, the absence of the method should at least be logged as a warning so that silent regressions are detectable: ```typescript function forcePersistPromptOnlyTranscript( sessionManager: ReturnType, ): void { const manager = sessionManager as unknown as { isPersisted?: () => boolean; _rewriteFile?: () => void; }; if (!manager.isPersisted?.()) { return; } if (typeof manager._rewriteFile !== "function") { // Library API has changed — log so the regression is detectable. log.warn("forcePersistPromptOnlyTranscript: _rewriteFile not available on SessionManager"); return; } manager._rewriteFile(); } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/acp/session-transcript.ts Line: 191-204 Comment: **`_rewriteFile` may be async but is called without `await`** The type cast declares `_rewriteFile` as `() => void`, but if the underlying library implementation is `async _rewriteFile(): Promise` (which is common for file I/O operations), the returned `Promise` will be silently discarded. The file write would happen asynchronously after `forcePersistPromptOnlyTranscript` returns, creating a race condition where `emitSessionTranscriptUpdate(sessionFile)` fires before the file has actually been written, and readers polling the file immediately after spawn could still see empty content. Since `persistAcpTranscriptMessages` is already `async`, the fix is straightforward — make `forcePersistPromptOnlyTranscript` async and `await` the call: ```typescript async function forcePersistPromptOnlyTranscript( sessionManager: ReturnType, ): Promise { const manager = sessionManager as unknown as { isPersisted?: () => boolean; _rewriteFile?: () => Promise | void; }; if (!manager.isPersisted?.()) { return; } await manager._rewriteFile?.(); } ``` And at the call site (line 130): ```typescript if (changed && promptText && !replyText) { await forcePersistPromptOnlyTranscript(sessionManager); } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Only two P2 style issues were found. No files require special attention.

Path: src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts Line: 113 Comment: **Unnecessary type cast weakens the `acp` check** `SessionEntry` already declares `acp?: SessionAcpMeta`, so the cast to `{ acp?: unknown }` is redundant and silently drops the stronger type. A plain optional-chain is safer and lets TypeScript catch future renames of the field. ```suggestion const hasAcpSession = Boolean(params.sessionEntry?.acp); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/agents/subagent-announce.ts Line: 184 Comment: **Contradictory `noReplyGuard` wording may confuse the model** The guard opens with "when ALL of these hold" (implying AND), but then lists three conditions joined by "or (c)" (implying OR). A model parsing this strictly might treat (c) alone as sufficient to suppress a reply, or might demand all three simultaneously — neither is the intended behavior. Restate so the logical connective is unambiguous: ```suggestion const noReplyGuard = `Reply ONLY with ${SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN} when at least one of these holds: (a) this completion is a duplicate of one already processed in this turn, (b) the result is already covered in a prior reply, or (c) it is only incremental confirmation that does not materially change the user-facing conclusion. In every other case you MUST produce a substantive reply.`; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 64322 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/64322 - Title: fix(acp): assign distinct session keys to Discord threads under the same parent channel - State: OPEN - Author: hoh-dev-bot - Labels: channel: bluebubbles, channel: discord, channel: imessage, channel: line, channel: matrix, channel: feishu, size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary - **Root cause**: `matchDiscordAcpConversation` returned the parent channel ID as `conversationId` on a parent-match, and `resolveMaterializedConfiguredBinding` materialised using `resolved.match` (which carried that wrong ID). All threads under the same parent folded into one ACP session. - **Fix**: parent-match now returns `{ matchPriority: 1 }` only; the registry materialises from the live `conversation` ref (threadId + parentConversationId) instead of the match object. - **Type debt (Finding 3)**: narrowed `ChannelConfiguredBindingMatch` from `ChannelConfiguredBindingConversationRef & { matchPriority?: number }` to `{ matchPriority?: number }` — only `matchPriority` is consumed downstream; the identity fields were dead weight that obscured the original bug. Updated all 7 `matchInboundConversation` implementations and test mocks. - **Known limitation (Finding 2)**: `configured-binding-session-lookup` uses `rule.target` (compile-time parent channel ID) for reverse lookup, so per-thread session keys can never be matched. Documented with a comment and a test that asserts `null`. - **Pre-existing lint/type fixes** surfaced by staging the touched files (not introduced by this PR): `no-redundant-type-constituents` in `discord/channel.ts` and `feishu/channel.ts`; `TS2493` tuple-length mismatch in `msteams/attachments.test.ts`. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related: #50955 (complementary fix — expects `conversationId = threadId` which this PR ensures) - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause - Root cause: `matchDiscordAcpConversation` (discord/channel.ts) returned `{ conversationId: parentConversationId, matchPriority: 1 }` on parent match. `resolveMaterializedConfiguredBinding` (configured-binding-registry.ts) then called `materializeConfiguredBindingRecord({ conversation: resolved.match })` which used that wrong `conversationId` for session key hashing. All threads under the same parent produced the identical session key. - Missing detection: no test exercised two threads under the same parent and compared their session keys. - Contributing context: `ChannelConfiguredBindingMatch` extended `ChannelConfiguredBindingConversationRef`, making the identity fields look meaningful in a match context when they were only used for priority selection. ## Regression Test Plan - Coverage level: unit tests - Target: `src/channels/plugins/acp-bindings.test.ts` - New test: `"assigns distinct session keys to different threads under the same parent channel"` — creates two binding resolutions for thread IDs `1111111111111111111` and `2222222222222222222` under the same parent `1479098716916023408`, asserts `thread1.targetSessionKey !== thread2.targetSessionKey`. - Also: `"returns null for per-thread session keys via reverse lookup"` — documents the known limitation that reverse lookup can't reconstruct thread-derived session keys. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Each Discord thread now gets its own independent ACP session rather than sharing the session of the parent channel. ## Diagram N/A. ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? No - Command/tool execution surface changed? No - Data access scope changed? No ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm - Integration/channel: Discord / ACP ### Steps 1. Configure an ACP binding pointing at a Discord parent channel. 2. Send messages from two different threads under that parent. 3. Observe that each thread now routes to its own ACP session. ### Expected Each thread has a distinct `targetSessionKey`. ### Actual (before fix) All threads under the same parent shared the parent's session key. ## Evidence - [x] Unit test passes: 8/8 in `acp-bindings.test.ts` including the new regression test - [x] 928 discord extension tests pass - [x] tsgo clean (only pre-existing `msteams` error fixed as part of this PR) ## Human Verification - Verified: `pnpm vitest run src/channels/plugins/acp-bindings.test.ts` (8/8), `pnpm vitest run extensions/discord/src/` (928/928), `pnpm tsgo` clean. - Edge cases checked: wildcard account binding, exact account binding, registry version invalidation. - Not verified: live Discord end-to-end (requires deployed environment). ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes — sessions that previously shared a key will now split. Existing sessions under the parent channel ID continue to work; thread sessions start fresh. - Config/env changes? No - Migration needed? No ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: existing threads that were silently sharing the parent session will now get a new session on next message. GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 62552 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/62552 - Title: fix(acp): stabilize bridge session keys - State: OPEN - Author: kong373 - Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown # PR: fix(acp): stabilize bridge session keys ## Summary - Use `acp-bridge:` for ACP bridge fallback sessions created without metadata. - Match pending ACP prompts across raw and Gateway-canonical session keys, e.g. `acp-bridge:` and `agent:main:acp-bridge:`. - Treat terminal child sessions as missing backing sessions during task registry maintenance for `cron`, `cli`, and `subagent` tasks. ## Why `acpx ... openclaw exec ...` could hang or fail because ACP bridge fallback keys used `acp:`. Gateway canonicalization turned those into `agent:main:acp:`, which collided with internal OpenClaw ACP runtime session semantics and expected `/acp spawn` metadata. After switching to `acp-bridge:`, final Gateway chat events still arrive under the canonical key, so pending prompt lookup also needs raw/canonical key equivalence. The task maintenance change prevents stale tasks from staying live solely because a child session record still exists after it has already reached a terminal state. ## Validation - `pnpm install` - `pnpm tsgo` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm vitest run src/acp/translator.stop-reason.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t 'matches canonical gateway session keys for ACP bridge sessions'` - `pnpm vitest run src/acp/translator.session-rate-limit.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t 'uses ACP bridge session keys for new CLI sessions without metadata'` - `pnpm vitest run src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.issue-60299.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t 'marks active cron tasks lost when their child session is terminal'` ## Note Running the full `src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.issue-60299.test.ts` file hung on the pre-existing `marks chat-backed cli tasks lost after the owning run context disappears` case, apparently around plugin bootstrap/session chat-type derivation. The new cron terminal-child regression test passes when run directly. ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-07T15:05:52Z:

GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 45739 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/45739 - Title: ACP: recover parent relay output from gateway state - State: OPEN - Author: stevenayl - Labels: agents, size: M, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary - Problem: ACP child runs can finish successfully while the parent `streamTo: "parent"` relay only sees synthetic `start`/`stall` notices because it depends on local `onAgentEvent(...)` delivery. - Why it matters: orchestration from the parent session loses child progress and terminal completion, so `sessions_spawn` looks hung even when the ACP child actually replied or finished work. - What changed: the parent relay now backfills child assistant output from gateway `chat.history` and terminal state from `agent.wait`, while still preferring local assistant/lifecycle events when they arrive. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): ACP spawn semantics, child execution, and the normal local event path are unchanged; this only adds a best-effort fallback when relay events do not cross the process boundary. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #45205 - Related #45205 ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - ACP parent relays now recover missing child assistant output and completion from gateway state when local relay events do not arrive, so parent sessions stop getting stuck on `start`/`stall`-only updates. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): Yes - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: - The relay now makes best-effort gateway calls to `chat.history` and `agent.wait` for the spawned child session/run. - Mitigation: calls are scoped to the known child session key / run ID, use short timeouts, and only supplement the existing local event path. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS host for source tests; live repro previously validated against Debian 13 install from issue pattern - Runtime/container: Node v25.6.0, pnpm 10.23.0 - Model/provider: ACP child via Codex during manual smoke; issue originally reported with Claude/OpenRouter - Integration/channel (if any): ACP `sessions_spawn` with `streamTo: "parent"` - Relevant config (redacted): ACP enabled with backend `acpx`, parent streaming enabled ### Steps 1. Start an ACP-capable parent session and spawn a child with `sessions_spawn`, `runtime: "acp"`, and `streamTo: "parent"`. 2. Use a task that produces visible assistant output quickly. 3. Observe the parent relay output and child transcript / ACP stream log. ### Expected - Parent relay receives child assistant progress and terminal completion. ### Actual - Before the fix, affected runs could emit only synthetic `start`/`stall` notices even though the child transcript already contained assistant output and the child had completed. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.test.ts src/commands/agent.acp.test.ts` - New relay regression cases cover: - missing local assistant + terminal events recovered via `chat.history` + `agent.wait` - missing local terminal event with assistant deltas still present - missing local assistant output recovered before a local lifecycle `end` - Manual live smoke on the installed 2026.3.12 runtime: 5 repeated real ACP spawns with unique markers all produced assistant output plus terminal completion in the generated `.acp-stream.jsonl` relay logs. - Edge cases checked: - start notice behavior unchanged - no-output stall / resume behavior unchanged - max relay lifetime timeout unchanged - local whitespace-preserving delta aggregation unchanged - What you did **not** verify: - I did not rely on `src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts` for this fix because that suite fully mocks `acp-spawn-parent-stream.js` and, in this tarball snapshot, currently fails during unrelated mock-hoisting/module-load setup before exercising the relay path. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No - If yes, exact upgrade steps: ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: - Revert this commit or temporarily remove the fallback poll / gateway backfill from `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts`. - Files/config to restore: - `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts` - `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.test.ts` - `CHANGELOG.md` - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: - duplicated child progress relays - parent relay completion firing before child output is flushed - relay logs missing `assistant_history` / terminal backfill in the missing-event path ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: fallback history polling could duplicate output when local assistant events already arrived. - Mitigation: the fallback path bails once local assistant events have been seen and tracks whether history output was already relayed. - Risk: extra gateway lookups could introduce relay noise or hangs. - Mitigation: calls are short-timeout, best-effort, and capped to the relay lifetime / poll interval already used by the stall watcher. ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-14T04:33:15Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a best-effort gateway fallback to the ACP parent stream relay so that child assistant output and terminal completion are recovered from `chat.history` and `agent.wait` when local `onAgentEvent` deliveries don't cross the process boundary. The approach is sound and the new tests cover the three key recovery scenarios. **Issues found:** - **Race condition in `primeHistoryBaseline`** (see inline comment): after the `async` gateway call resolves, the function does not re-check `historyBaselineReady`. A concurrent `maybeRelayHistoryAssistant` call from the first fallback poll can prime the baseline with the correct reply (setting `lastHistoryAssistant = "actual reply"`), then `primeHistoryBaseline` overwrites it with an earlier/empty result. This leaves the deduplication guard stale, causing the already-relayed output to be emitted a second time on the next poll cycle. A one-line `historyBaselineReady` guard after the `await` is the minimal fix.

Confidence Score: 3/5

- Merge with caution — the fallback logic is largely correct but a missing post-await guard in primeHistoryBaseline can produce a duplicate output relay in a narrow race window. - The overall design is correct and the tests validate the main scenarios. The deduplication flags (historyOutputRelayed, sawLocalAssistantEvent, sawLocalTerminalEvent) provide layers of protection. However, primeHistoryBaseline does not re-check historyBaselineReady after its await, allowing a concurrent maybeRelayHistoryAssistant call to set valid state that primeHistoryBaseline then overwrites. The resulting stale lastHistoryAssistant baseline can cause a false "change" detection and a duplicate assistant output relay on the next poll tick. The impact is a redundant progress message (not a hung relay or data loss), but it's a real correctness issue in the new state machine. - src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts — specifically the primeHistoryBaseline function (lines 300-310)

Path: src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts Line: 300-310 Comment: **`primeHistoryBaseline` doesn't re-check `historyBaselineReady` after its `await`** `primeHistoryBaseline()` is fired with `void` at startup while the fallback poll timer starts at the same `fallbackPollIntervalMs`. Both paths can call `readLatestAssistantFromChildHistory()` concurrently (the priming call and the poll's `maybeRelayHistoryAssistant` call are not mutually guarded — `fallbackPollInFlight` only blocks concurrent poll runs, not the one-time `primeHistoryBaseline`). Race scenario: 1. `primeHistoryBaseline` starts: `historyBaselineReady = false`, gateway call in-flight. 2. The first fallback poll fires, `maybeRelayHistoryAssistant(false)` runs concurrently, also calls the gateway. Because `historyBaselineReady` is still `false`, it sets `historyBaselineReady = true`, `lastHistoryAssistant = "actual reply"`, and relays the output (`historyOutputRelayed = true`). 3. `primeHistoryBaseline` resolves: the `disposed` guard passes, but there is no check on `historyBaselineReady`. It unconditionally overwrites `lastHistoryAssistant` with whatever its own (potentially earlier/empty) gateway response returned — e.g. `lastHistoryAssistant = ""`. 4. On the next poll cycle: `reply = "actual reply"`, `lastHistoryAssistant = ""`, so `changed = true` and `historyOutputRelayed && !changed` evaluates to `false` — the output is emitted again. The fix is to add a `historyBaselineReady` guard after the `await`, mirroring the existing `disposed` check: ```suggestion const primeHistoryBaseline = async () => { if (historyBaselineReady || disposed) { return; } const reply = await readLatestAssistantFromChildHistory(); if (disposed || historyBaselineReady) { return; } historyBaselineReady = true; lastHistoryAssistant = reply ?? ""; }; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #69260: Harden Gemini ACP integration against ambient API-key fallback and add generic auth-contract hooks for ACP agents
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 69260
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/69260
- Title: Harden Gemini ACP integration against ambient API-key fallback and add generic auth-contract hooks for ACP agents
- State: OPEN
- Author: anyech
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, clawsweeper:source-repro, clawsweeper:linked-pr-open, impact:security, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
### Summary
This is an integration-hardening / defense-in-depth issue, not a claim that OpenClaw is the sole root cause of Gemini auth drift.

We just finished a local incident/debugging cycle where a risky Gemini ACP path could drift durable Gemini auth away from `oauth-personal` and into the API-key lane when `GEMINI_API_KEY` was ambient.

The deepest bug seam appears to belong upstream in Gemini CLI, because ACP auth handling seems able to persist a fallback auth method back into durable Gemini settings.

However, OpenClaw also contributed to the exposure: the ACP launch/runtime path allowed provider auth env to be inherited broadly. That is a defensible default in some environments, but in a mixed-credential host it makes the wrong auth lane eligible unless the integration hardens against it explicitly.

### What local stage validation showed
We ran an isolated-HOME comparison without touching live user state.

#### Old risky path
- no explicit outer auth selector for the Gemini ACP lane
- bare Gemini ACP launch path
- ambient `GEMINI_API_KEY`

Result:
- the staged auth state drifted to the API-key lane

#### Hardened local path
- Gemini durable settings pinned to `oauth-personal`
- explicit outer selection guard for the Gemini ACP lane
- Gemini launch path strips Gemini / GenAI auth env before exec
- gateway/runtime no longer carries broad Gemini auth env by default

Result:
- the same staged pressure did not drift the auth state back

### Why file this in OpenClaw
Even if the deepest fix lands upstream in Gemini CLI, OpenClaw still has useful product-level hardening work here.

The lesson is broader than Gemini:

> ACP-backed agent integrations that depend on a specific auth mode should be able to declare that auth contract explicitly, scrub incompatible ambient auth env, and fail closed when the contract is not met.

### Suggested product direction
#### 1. Auth-contract metadata on ACP agent definitions
Allow an ACP-backed agent or route to declare:
- expected auth mode
- whether fallback auth methods are allowed
- whether ambient provider auth env should be stripped

#### 2. Integration-level env scrubbing hooks
Provide a first-class way to remove provider auth env from ACP launches when a route is supposed to use another auth surface.

For example, Gemini ACP routes should be able to scrub things like:
- `GEMINI_API_KEY`
- `GOOGLE_API_KEY`
- `GOOGLE_GENAI_API_KEY`
- `GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI`
- `GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_GCA`

#### 3. Fail-closed preflight hooks for fragile ACP routes
Allow an ACP route to run auth/readiness preflight before spawn and stop early if the declared auth contract is already violated.

#### 4. Better docs for mixed-credential hosts
Document the risk pattern where a host intentionally keeps both:
- a durable OAuth login for an ACP-backed CLI tool, and
- API keys for unrelated scripts/tools

Without auth pinning and env hygiene, those mixed credentials can create silent fallback/drift paths.

### Scope boundary
I am **not** asking OpenClaw to patch Gemini CLI internals here.

I am asking OpenClaw to make this class of unsafe ACP integration easier to prevent:
- minimize ambient auth exposure
- make auth policy explicit
- fail closed instead of silently improvising a different auth method

### Why this is worth doing even if upstream fixes Gemini CLI
The local A/B already showed meaningful value at the integration layer:
- old path drifts
- hardened path holds

So even with an upstream fix, OpenClaw should still defend itself better at the ACP boundary.

### Expected next step
I think this should start as an issue/discussion, not as an immediate PR.

If maintainers agree with the direction, any later implementation should be validated in a disposable/stage environment only, not by patching a live install.
```

([`extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts:767`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/167e73cd5f84/extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts#L767), [167e73cd5f84](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/167e73cd5f84))

GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 70306 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/70306 - Title: fix(acp+gateway): clean final emit, fallback visibility, legacy unit resolve - State: OPEN - Author: nrubioru - Labels: gateway, cli, agents, size: M, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider, merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Problem Three related rough edges in ACP/Codex orchestration and gateway ops: 1. Parent sessions could fail to surface a coherent final answer after `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")`. Mid-flight snippet flushes compacted whitespace and truncated aggressively, so short multi-line Codex key/value output could collapse into a single line before any clean final answer reached the parent. 2. Harness fallback visibility was weak. Operators could not easily answer "did this actually run in Codex or fall back?" without grepping logs. 3. Legacy hosts running `clawdbot-gateway.service` exposed `CLAWDBOT_SYSTEMD_UNIT`, but the relevant resolver paths only honored `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT`, so status/restart tooling could target the canonical `openclaw-gateway.service` instead of the actual running unit. ## Solution - `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts` - Accumulate non-commentary child output into a dedicated final buffer. - On `phase === "end"`, emit a single normalized ` final:` system event with preserved newlines, line-aware truncation, and whitespace cleanup. - On `phase === "error"`, surface the partial transcript before the error text. - Keep the existing mid-flight snippet behavior for progress chatter. - `src/agents/harness/selection.ts` - Record a 16-slot in-memory ring of harness selection diagnostics with requested runtime, selected harness, fallback usage, and reason. - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-acp/diagnostics.ts` - Extend `/acp doctor` to show recent harness selections, recent harness fallbacks, and the last 5 diagnostic entries. - `src/daemon/systemd.ts` and `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts` - Fall back to `CLAWDBOT_SYSTEMD_UNIT` when `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT` is unset so legacy hosts resolve the actual running unit correctly. ## Files changed - `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts` - `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.final-output.test.ts` (new) - `src/agents/harness/selection.ts` - `src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts` - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-acp/diagnostics.ts` - `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts` - `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.test.ts` - `src/daemon/systemd.ts` ## Tests run - `pnpm test src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.test.ts` - `pnpm test src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.final-output.test.ts` - `pnpm test src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts` - `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/commands-acp.test.ts` - `pnpm test src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.test.ts` - `pnpm test src/daemon/inspect.test.ts` - `pnpm tsgo` - `pnpm format:check` on touched files - repo pre-commit hook (`oxlint` / `oxfmt`) clean ## Limitations - Parent follow-through is mitigated, not fully fixed. - `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")` still returns an accepted envelope immediately; the clean child final reaches the parent via the relay side-channel rather than the tool result itself. - A true attach-and-wait or resume-on-child-completion primitive would be a larger behavior change and should land separately. - The new smoke coverage is unit-test based, not a live Codex roundtrip. - The harness diagnostic ring is process-local and resets on gateway restart. - Docs / changelog are not updated here. ## Explicit note on parent follow-through This change substantially improves the normal-path symptom where the parent appeared to "move on" without surfacing a clean final answer. The relay now emits a coherent final system event on completion, and the compact-whitespace collapse bug is covered by tests. However, the underlying architecture is unchanged: if the parent stops generating before the child-completion event arrives, the parent can still appear to move on. A true fix requires a larger orchestration change. ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-22T18:54:01Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes three independent rough edges in ACP/Codex orchestration and gateway ops: it replaces per-snippet flushes with a normalized full-transcript final emit on child completion; adds a 16-slot in-memory diagnostic ring to make harness fallback visible via `/acp doctor`; and wires up `CLAWDBOT_SYSTEMD_UNIT` as a legacy fallback in both the daemon resolver and the restart-helper so status/restart tooling targets the unit that is actually running on legacy hosts. The implementation is well-structured and the test coverage addresses the main regression (multi-line k/v output collapsing). Two P2 observations worth considering before a follow-up: (1) the `accumulatedFinalText` buffer caps at 12 k chars by dropping *newer* deltas, so for long outputs the parent receives the earliest portion rather than the actual final answer; (2) the harness diagnostic ring records `selected=pi` even on the `fallback=none` throw paths, which can look like a successful PI fallback in `/acp doctor` when the request actually failed outright.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; all open findings are P2 quality suggestions that do not block correct operation for the targeted use cases. The primary bug fix (multi-line k/v output collapsing) is well-covered by tests and the implementation is correct for the targeted short-output case. The legacy unit fallback is symmetric across both resolver paths and is similarly tested. Both P2 findings are design tradeoffs or edge-case improvements rather than present defects in the normal path. src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts — the accumulation cap drops newest deltas for long outputs; src/agents/harness/selection.ts — diagnostic entries on throw paths record a misleading selected harness.
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts Line: 396-398 Comment: **Accumulation cap drops newest content for long outputs** Once `accumulatedFinalText` reaches `FINAL_ANSWER_MAX_CHARS * 2` (12 000 chars), every subsequent delta is silently discarded. `normalizeFinalAnswerText` then crops the kept portion to 6 000 chars, so the parent sees the *first* 6 000 chars of output rather than the *last* 6 000. For agents whose conclusive answer arrives at the end of a long transcript (the common case), the final emit will be stale preamble, not the actual answer. The existing "huge output" test stops at 8 000 chars so it never exercises this path. A circular/tail-based approach keeps the most-recent content instead: ```ts if (accumulatedFinalText.length >= FINAL_ANSWER_MAX_CHARS * 2) { // Keep the tail so the final emit reflects the most recent output. accumulatedFinalText = accumulatedFinalText.slice(-FINAL_ANSWER_MAX_CHARS); } accumulatedFinalText += delta; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/agents/harness/selection.ts Line: 118-122 Comment: **Diagnostic records "pi selected" on paths that throw, not select** Both `fallback === "none"` error paths call `recordSelection("pi", "…;fallback-disabled")` immediately before throwing. The diagnostic ring therefore shows `selected=pi fallback=true` for runs that never actually executed on PI — they threw instead. An operator reading `/acp doctor` could incorrectly conclude the request fell back and ran on PI, when in fact it failed outright. Consider recording a distinct status (e.g. `selectedHarnessId: "(none — threw)"`) or skipping the diagnostic entry on the throw path. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Greptile Summary

This PR addresses two distinct problems: (1) the ACP verification gate was incorrectly failing persistent-session turns and applying a hard failure banner to oneshot runs that produced real progress output but no tracked file diff — both are now handled more gracefully; and (2) `session.message` payloads arriving during an active chat run are now appended directly to the transcript (with chat-side deduplication and optimistic-echo replacement) instead of discarding the update until the run ends. The `verifyAcpWorktreeDiff` helper and its test coverage look solid; the session-message append path is well-tested for the normal case.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; all remaining findings are P2 style/robustness observations with no current defect on the changed path. The verification-gate logic is correct and well-tested (persistent bypass, oneshot-with-progress → blocked, oneshot-without-progress → failed). The session.message append path handles the active-run guard, deferred reload, and optimistic-echo replacement. The only open items are a potential deduplication gap (only triggers if the server sends messageId without embedding it in the message object, corrected on the next loadChatHistory anyway) and a documentation note about untracked files in the git-diff check — both P2. No files require special attention.
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts Line: 139-145 Comment: **Deduplication relies on embedded identity in stored messages** `resolveTranscriptMessageIdentity(entry)` is called on stored messages _without_ the `options` fallback, so if a server sends `session.message` with a top-level `messageId`/`messageSeq` that is **not** embedded in the message object itself (no `id`, `__openclaw.id`, or `__openclaw.seq` field), the stored entry resolves to `null` identity. A second delivery of the same event (e.g. on reconnect) would then bypass this guard and append a duplicate. The existing test always provides a message with `__openclaw: { id: "msg-2", seq: 2 }`, so it passes, but the dedup contract is only upheld when the server co-embeds the identity inside the message payload. If the server ever sends `messageId` as event-level metadata only (without embedding in the message), the guard is ineffective. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/agents/acp-verification-gate.ts Line: 19-22 Comment: **`git diff --stat HEAD` misses untracked files** `git diff HEAD` detects all uncommitted tracked changes (staged + unstaged relative to HEAD), but new files that have not yet been `git add`'d are invisible to it. An agent that writes files without staging them would appear to produce zero tracked changes and trigger the verification gate. The user-facing message ("No tracked file changes landed") correctly scopes to tracked changes, but it may be worth noting this in the function's JSDoc so future callers understand the boundary — especially since some ACP backends may not auto-stage written files. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 57824 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/57824 - Title: Fix ACP image forwarding and Synology invalid-token throttling - State: OPEN - Author: luoyanglang - Labels: size: S, channel: synology-chat, proof: supplied, proof: sufficient, P2, rating: 🐚 platinum hermit, status: 👀 ready for maintainer look Body: ```markdown ## Summary This PR bundles two small, low-risk fixes: 1. Fix ACP attachment collection so URL-only image attachments are forwarded into ACP turns. 2. Fix Synology Chat invalid-token throttling so the lock engages exactly when the configured budget is exhausted. ## What changed ### ACP image forwarding Fix ACP attachment collection so image attachments that only provide `MediaUrl` are still forwarded into ACP turns. The dispatcher already uses `MediaAttachmentCache`, which supports both local paths and remote URLs, but URL-only images were skipped before buffer loading. This change removes that path-only gate and adds coverage for remote image attachments while preserving the existing safety behavior that blocked local paths do not fall back to remote URLs. ### Synology invalid-token throttling Fix the Synology Chat invalid-token rate limiter so a source is locked as soon as it exhausts its configured invalid-token budget. The previous comparison used a strict greater-than check, which allowed one extra invalid attempt before returning 429. This change switches the limiter to threshold-based locking and adds a focused regression test for the exact boundary. ## Validation - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.test.ts` Result on 2026-05-12 after rebasing onto `origin/main` `5abb4b18c8`: ```text Test Files 1 passed (1) # auto-reply shard Tests 41 passed (41) Test Files 1 passed (1) # extension-messaging shard Tests 23 passed (23) ``` ## Real behavior proof **Behavior or issue addressed:** URL-only ACP image attachments should reach ACP turns, and Synology invalid-token lockout should engage exactly at the configured invalid-token budget without blocking valid zero-budget requests pre-auth. **Real environment tested:** Local Linux checkout of OpenClaw on branch head `ff7aee2b89`, Node/tsx runtime from the repository dependency set, after rebasing onto `origin/main` `5abb4b18c8`. **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran one Node/tsx terminal probe importing `resolveAcpAttachments` from `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-attachments.ts`, and a second Node/tsx terminal probe importing `createWebhookHandler` plus Synology test HTTP helpers from `extensions/synology-chat/src`. **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the ACP attachment probe: ```json { "cacheCalls": [ { "url": "https://example.com/inbound.png", "maxBytes": 10485760, "timeoutMs": 1000 } ], "attachments": [ { "mediaType": "image/png", "data": "cmVtb3RlLWltYWdl" } ] } ``` Terminal output from the Synology webhook probe: ```json { "invalidTokenStatuses": [ 401, 401, 401, 401, 429 ], "zeroBudgetValidStatus": 204, "zeroBudgetDeliveries": 1 } ``` **Observed result after fix:** The ACP resolver passed the URL-only image into the attachment cache and emitted an ACP image attachment. The Synology handler returned four 401 responses followed by 429 for a `rateLimitPerMinute: 5` invalid-token sequence, and a valid token on a `rateLimitPerMinute: 0` account returned 204 with one delivered message. **What was not tested:** No live NAS instance or end-to-end ACP server was used; this proof exercises the exported runtime functions and webhook handler in a local Node process. ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-30T18:00:27Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR delivers two targeted bug fixes with corresponding test coverage. The **Synology invalid-token throttling fix** corrects an off-by-one error in `InvalidTokenRateLimiter` where strict `>` comparisons allowed one extra invalid attempt beyond the configured budget before engaging the lock; switching to `>=` makes the limiter engage exactly when the budget is exhausted. The **ACP image forwarding fix** removes a path-only gate in `resolveAcpAttachments` that was incorrectly skipping URL-only image attachments before they could reach `MediaAttachmentCache.getBuffer()`; the safety invariant (local-path attachments never fall back to their remote URL) is preserved by the existing preprocessing step that strips the `url` field whenever a local path is present. - Both changes are mechanically simple (single comparison / single guard removed) with clear, focused regression tests that pin the boundary behavior - The `ACP_ATTACHMENT_TIMEOUT_MS = 1_000` constant now applies to remote fetches where previously only local reads reached that code path — failures are already caught and skipped gracefully, so this is not a blocking concern but worth noting - Test names in the validation commands match the new test descriptions exactly

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — both fixes are minimal, correct, and well-covered by targeted regression tests with no unintended side effects. Both changes are single-line/single-block removals with clear intent. The >= boundary fix matches the documented contract. The ACP path gate removal is safe because the preprocessing step already strips URLs from local-path attachments. New tests pin both boundary cases exactly. No regressions in adjacent paths. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

 | Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.ts | Off-by-one corrected in both isLocked and recordFailure — >= now engages the lock exactly when the budget is exhausted instead of one attempt later. | | extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.test.ts | New regression test pins the exact boundary: 4 attempts return 401, 5th triggers 429 with rateLimitPerMinute=5. | | src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts | Path-only gate removed, allowing URL-only image attachments to reach MediaAttachmentCache.getBuffer(); safety invariant preserved by existing url-stripping preprocessing. | | src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts | Three new tests cover URL-only forwarding, no URL fallback when local path is blocked, and that blocked path+URL still skips the turn. |
Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["synology-chat: lock invalid token budget..."](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/46b842552f470089bfd5bca2b32221e6149c072b) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=26802098) - clawsweeper at 2026-04-28T15:17:41Z: Codex review: needs maintainer review before merge. **Workflow note:** Future ClawSweeper reviews update this same comment in place.

**Summary** The PR removes the ACP image path-only gate, changes Synology invalid-token throttling to threshold lockout with a zero-budget clamp, and adds focused regression tests. Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection against current main shows URL-only ACP image attachments are normalized but skipped before the cache, and Synology invalid-token lockout allows one extra invalid attempt because it uses strict greater-than checks. **PR rating** Overall: 🐚 platinum hermit Proof: 🐚 platinum hermit Patch quality: 🦞 diamond lobster Summary: This is a clean, narrow PR with sufficient terminal proof, focused tests, green current-head checks, and no blocking findings. Rank-up moves: - none

input.title: feat(acp): forward image blocks in tool call results

input.github_context:

Greptile Summary

This PR extends `extractToolCallContent()` in the ACP event mapper to forward `image` content blocks from tool results to ACP clients (e.g., Feishu, Discord), in addition to the previously-supported `text` blocks. The change is minimal and well-tested. **Key changes:** - Added an `else if` branch in `extractToolCallContent()` that handles `type: "image"` blocks, validating that both `data` and `mimeType` are strings before forwarding them as `ImageContent`. - Added comprehensive unit tests covering text-only, image-only, mixed, malformed (missing fields), empty content, and string-fallback cases. **Issue found:** - The image-block validation checks `typeof entry.data === "string"` but doesn't guard against an empty string (`""`), unlike the text-block branch which uses `.trim()`. An image block with `data: ""` or `mimeType: ""` would be forwarded to clients as a valid image, likely causing rendering failures. The test suite also doesn't cover this edge case.

The single P2 finding requires a malformed tool result (empty-string `data` or `mimeType`) to trigger, which does not occur in normal tool output. Tests are thorough for the documented cases. No breaking changes or protocol modifications needed. No files require special attention beyond the minor validation gap in `src/acp/event-mapper.ts`.

For consistency with the text-block handling, you should add emptiness guards: ```suggestion } else if ( entry?.type === "image" && typeof entry.data === "string" && entry.data.length > 0 && typeof entry.mimeType === "string" && entry.mimeType.length > 0 ) { contents.push({ type: "content", content: { type: "image", data: entry.data, mimeType: entry.mimeType, } as ImageContent & { type: "image" }, }); } ``` The corresponding test case `"skips image blocks with missing data or mimeType"` only covers `undefined` values (absent fields), not empty strings — a test for `{ type: "image", data: "", mimeType: "image/png" }` should also be added.
