GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 56176 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56176 - Title: fix: accept MCP protocolVersion 2025-11-25 in ACP server (#56102) - State: OPEN - Author: bugkill3r - Labels: size: XS, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary - ACP server now accepts MCP date-string `protocolVersion` (e.g. `"2025-11-25"`) from VS Code 1.113+ and Cursor - Adds `coerceAcpStream()` transform that intercepts `initialize` requests and coerces non-integer `protocolVersion` values to the ACP integer protocol version - All other messages pass through unmodified ## Test plan - [x] TypeScript compiles cleanly - [x] Formatting and linting pass - [ ] Manual: connect VS Code 1.113 or Cursor with MCP protocolVersion 2025-11-25, verify ACP initializes successfully Closes #56102 ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-28T04:54:04Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a thin `TransformStream` shim (`coerceAcpStream`) around the `ndJsonStream` produced by the ACP SDK. It intercepts JSON-RPC `initialize` messages and replaces any non-numeric `protocolVersion` (e.g. the MCP date-string `"2025-11-25"` sent by VS Code 1.113+ and Cursor) with the ACP SDK's own `PROTOCOL_VERSION` integer, preventing the `-32602 Invalid params` rejection that currently breaks the handshake. All other messages are passed through unmodified. - The core logic is correct and well-scoped: only `initialize.params.protocolVersion` is touched, and only when it is not already a number. - The `as unknown as Stream` double-cast at lines 45–48 silently bypasses TypeScript's structural checks on the returned object — worth revisiting if the SDK's `Stream` type evolves. - The `typeof params.protocolVersion !== "number"` guard is slightly broader than needed; `!Number.isInteger(params.protocolVersion)` would more precisely match the ACP SDK's `uint16` constraint and close an edge case for non-integer floats.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the change is small, well-commented, and correctly isolated to the initialize handshake path. No P0 or P1 issues found. The two comments are both P2 style/precision suggestions that do not affect correctness for the described use case (string date-format protocol versions). The fix is a targeted, low-risk shim with no side effects on the writable path. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

 | Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | src/acp/server.ts | Adds `coerceAcpStream()` wrapper that intercepts `initialize` messages from MCP-native clients and replaces non-integer `protocolVersion` values with the ACP `PROTOCOL_VERSION`. Logic is sound; two minor P2 style observations. |
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/acp/server.ts Line: 34 Comment: **`typeof "number"` check passes through non-integer numbers** The guard `typeof params.protocolVersion !== "number"` treats any JavaScript number (including floats like `3.14`, `NaN`, and `Infinity`) as valid, but the ACP SDK requires a `uint16` integer. If a client somehow sends a non-integer numeric version (e.g. `3.14`), it would bypass coercion and likely trigger a `-32602` rejection from the SDK — the same error this fix is meant to avoid. Using `Number.isInteger` would more precisely match the ACP SDK's constraint: ```suggestion if ("protocolVersion" in params && !Number.isInteger(params.protocolVersion)) { ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/acp/server.ts Line: 45-48 Comment: **`as unknown as Stream` discards type safety** The double-cast bypasses all TypeScript checking on the returned object. If `Stream` from `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` ever gains additional required properties (beyond `readable` and `writable`), this will silently compile but fail at runtime. A safer approach is to validate the shape explicitly: ```ts const { writable } = stream; const readable = (stream.readable as ReadableStream>).pipeThrough(transform); return { readable, writable } satisfies Pick as unknown as Stream; ``` Or, if the SDK exports the type narrowly enough, prefer `as Stream` without the intermediate `as unknown`. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

**src/agents/acp-spawn.ts** - Replaces the hard-coded `requesterIsSubagentSession` gate with `progressForwardEnabled` (`cfg.acp?.progressForward !== false`) in the `implicitStreamToParent` condition. - Keeps the existing heartbeat prerequisites in place: implicit forwarding still requires heartbeat to be enabled for the requester session and a usable session-local heartbeat relay route. - Hardens the thread-bound guards so implicit forwarding is blocked not only for explicit thread context, but also when the parent session is thread-bound via: - an active `targetKind === "session"` binding - a `:thread:` / `:topic:` marker in the session key - `deliveryContext.threadId` for suffixless threaded sessions (for example Discord threads that reuse the base session key) - **src/config/types.acp.ts**: Adds `progressForward?: boolean` to the ACP config type with JSDoc. - **src/config/zod-schema.ts**: Adds `progressForward: z.boolean().optional()` to the ACP config schema. - **src/config/schema.help.ts** / **src/config/schema.labels.ts**: Adds help text and label for `acp.progressForward`. - **docs.acp.md** - Documents the progress forwarding feature, what gets forwarded, how to disable it, and relay tuning options. - Clarifies that implicit forwarding also depends on heartbeat being enabled and a usable session-local heartbeat relay route. - **docs/.generated/config-baseline.json** / **docs/.generated/config-baseline.jsonl**: Regenerated to include the new config field. - **src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts** - Adds regression coverage for `acp.progressForward: false`. - Adds regression coverage for session-key thread markers. - Strengthens the thread-binding tests so they exercise the new guards instead of passing early on the heartbeat gate. - Adds regression coverage for suffixless threaded sessions where thread state is only visible through delivery context. ## Motivation Previously, progress forwarding was only implicitly enabled for subagent sessions with specific heartbeat conditions. This made it difficult to get visibility into long-running ACP runs without explicitly passing `streamTo="parent"` every time. By making it default-on (with a clean opt-out via `acp.progressForward: false`), users get transparent progress updates without additional configuration. The follow-up guard hardening keeps that default useful without leaking unsolicited ACP milestone chatter into user-facing thread conversations. ## Test plan - [x] `vitest run src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.test.ts` - [x] `vitest run src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts` - [x] Verified added coverage for: - `acp.progressForward: false` opt-out - active thread-binding detection without `ctx.agentThreadId` - `:thread:` / `:topic:` session-key marker detection - suffixless threaded-session detection via `deliveryContext.threadId` - [x] TypeScript errors on `main` remain pre-existing / unchanged by this branch (`@mariozechner/pi-tui` missing `getEditorKeybindings` export and `ModelCompatConfig` mismatch) ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-22T19:57:37Z:

This PR makes `acp.progressForward` default to `true`, so ACP sessions spawned from a parent Gateway session automatically forward compact milestone progress updates (start, completion, error, stall, timeout, resumed) to the parent session without requiring explicit `streamTo="parent"`. The change replaces the old `requesterIsSubagentSession` gate with a new `progressForwardEnabled` flag and adds three additional thread-detection guards to prevent progress chatter from leaking into thread-bound sessions. **Key changes:** - `acp-spawn.ts`: `implicitStreamToParent` no longer requires `requesterIsSubagentSession`; adds `progressForwardEnabled`, `requesterHasThreadBinding` (active `targetKind=session` binding), `requesterSessionKeyHasThreadMarker` (`:thread:`/`:topic:` in session key), and `requesterHasThreadFromDeliveryContext` (threadId in delivery context for suffixless Discord threads) - `hasSessionLocalHeartbeatRelayRoute` now returns `{ usable, parentThreadId }` so the delivery-context thread fallback can reuse the already-loaded session store entry without an extra read - `types.acp.ts` / `zod-schema.ts`: `progressForward?: boolean` added to the ACP config type and schema - `schema.help.ts` / `schema.labels.ts`: help text and label added - `docs.acp.md`: new "Progress Forwarding to Parent Session" section with prerequisites, configuration, and explicit-control guidance - `docs/.generated/config-baseline.json` / `.jsonl`: regenerated to include `acp.progressForward` (addressing the previously flagged drift-check failure) **Issues from prior review cycles:** - ✅ Config baseline artifacts now regenerated - ✅ Documentation now includes heartbeat + relay-route prerequisites - ✅ Test for `progressForward: false` opt-out path added - ✅ Thread-binding and session-key-marker tests now correctly enable heartbeat and provide a full delivery context, ensuring they exercise the intended guard rather than the heartbeat gate

Confidence Score: 5/5

- This PR is safe to merge — all three prior review concerns are addressed and no new logic or test issues were found. - All previously flagged gaps (config baseline drift, missing prerequisite docs, missing opt-out test, and tests passing for the wrong reason due to the heartbeat gate) are resolved in this iteration. The four new tests properly configure heartbeat and a usable delivery context, so each test exercises the specific guard it is named for. The multi-path thread detection logic (binding record, session-key marker, delivery-context threadId) is correctly implemented, the `hasSessionLocalHeartbeatRelayRoute` return-type extension is clean, and the schema/docs/baseline changes are consistent. No regressions or new bugs were identified. - No files require special attention. missing ownership metadata fails safe (no archive) ## Implementation - Thread binding metadata now records whether the thread was spawned by ACP - `/acp close` checks that metadata before requesting Discord thread archive - Discord outbound payload handling supports archive-after-reply so the success message is sent before the thread is archived ## Tests Added/updated tests for: - ACP-created Discord thread bindings storing spawned metadata - `/acp close` archiving ACP-created Discord threads - `/acp close` not archiving manually attached Discord threads - fail-safe behavior when ownership metadata is incomplete - non-Discord behavior unchanged - greptile-apps at 2026-03-09T06:01:31Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds Discord thread archiving to the `/acp close` flow: when the command is issued from a thread that OpenClaw itself spawned for an ACP session, the thread is archived after the success reply is sent. The implementation is well-scoped and introduces a clean separation between ACP-created threads and manually-attached ones via the new `spawnedThread`/`spawnedBy` metadata. Archive failures degrade gracefully by editing the success message to a partial-success warning. **One logic issue found:** - The archive early-return path in `handleAcpCloseAction` discards `runtimeNotice` from `closeSession`. When `closeSession` signals a partial/degraded close (e.g., backend unavailable), the normal path surfaces this as `` `✅ Closed … (${runtimeNotice})` ``, but the archive path returns hardcoded strings — silently dropping any operator-facing warning. The fix is to interpolate `runtimeNotice` into both the success and archive-failure texts.

The one identified issue — dropping `runtimeNotice` in the archive early-return — causes silent loss of backend-warning information that operators rely on, but does not break the core archive or close behavior. - Type: github_issue
- Number: 69669
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/69669
- Title: ACP: keep thread-bound follow-ups parent-orchestrated by default, not raw pass-through
- State: OPEN
- Author: V0hgg
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary
Thread-bound ACP follow-ups are currently too easy to treat as raw pass-through to the child session, instead of remaining parent-orchestrated by default.

In practice, the first prompt is often synthesized correctly by the parent assistant, but later shorthand follow-ups like:
- "ừa bắt nó làm tiếp như m suggest đi"
- "ok triển khai phase 2 đi"
- "bảo nó làm tiếp theo hướng đó"

can end up being forwarded as-is to the ACP child harness.

## Expected behavior
Thread binding should preserve child session continuity, but **not** imply raw-message forwarding semantics.

Default behavior should be:
1. parent assistant reads the user's shorthand follow-up
2. parent resolves references from parent-thread context
3. parent rewrites the follow-up into an explicit child task
4. parent sends the rewritten task to the bound ACP child session

Raw verbatim relay should happen **only** when the user explicitly asks for exact forwarding.

## Why this matters
Users often speak to the parent orchestrator in shorthand. If the raw message is passed through directly, the child receives low-context prompts and the orchestration UX breaks down.

This is especially visible in ACP thread-bound coding workflows where the user expects:
- user message -> parent interprets and expands
- parent prompt -> child executes

not:
- user message -> raw forward -> child guesses

## Evidence / current architecture notes
Observed locally:
- initial ACP thread spawn used `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp", thread=true, mode="session")`
- first child prompt was explicitly synthesized by the parent
- later shorthand follow-up was effectively treated as pass-through
- child saw the raw user text rather than a rewritten explicit task

Potentially relevant code paths:
- `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts`
- `src/gateway/session-subagent-reactivation.ts`
- thread-bound session continuation / reactivation behavior after `sessions.send`

The current accepted note for ACP session spawns says:
> "thread-bound ACP session stays active after this task; continue in-thread for follow-ups."

That note is correct for continuity, but ambiguous enough that it can lead to a pass-through mental model in parent orchestration.

## Proposed direction
- Keep thread-bound ACP continuity
- Clarify policy: continuity != verbatim relay
- Ensure parent remains the default interpreter/orchestrator for follow-ups
- Only relay exact raw messages when the user explicitly requests verbatim forwarding

## Acceptance criteria
- existing thread-bound ACP child session exists
- user sends shorthand continuation request
- parent rewrites it into a self-contained child task
- child receives rewritten task, not raw user message
- exact raw relay occurs only on explicit request

```

This PR fixes stale ACP bound sessions that remained attached when the runtime working directory (`cwd`) was gone. Yes for the classifier and dispatch shape: it preserves the `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` gate and reuses the existing binding-service unbind seam. The remaining issue is merge proof, plus clarity that reset cleanup reaches an active user path if reset recovery is part of the intended fix. Label justifications: - `P2`: This is a normal-priority ACP bound-session recovery bugfix with limited blast radius but real user impact when conversations wedge on stale bindings. - `merge-risk: 🚨 session-state`: Merging changes when persisted ACP session-binding records are removed, and false stale classific [truncated] ``` Changed files (5): ```text src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.ts src/acp/runtime/stale-session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts ```

GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 54647 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/54647 - Title: Fix ACP session model reporting in sessions list - State: OPEN - Author: cyan-ember - Labels: gateway, size: M, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary - read the ACP runtime model from session entry ACP metadata when resolving session model identity - avoid falling back to the configured default model for ACP session rows when no explicit ACP model is known - add regression coverage for ACP session model reporting in sessions list Closes #54640 ## Testing - pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts -t ACP - full src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts currently has one unrelated existing failure in uses persisted active subagent runs when the local worker only has terminal snapshots ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-25T19:19:00Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes ACP session model reporting in the sessions list by reading the runtime model from `acp.runtimeOptions.model` in session metadata and intentionally avoiding the fallback to the configured default model for ACP sessions when no explicit model is known. Key changes: - Three new private helpers (`resolveSessionModelIdentityLiteral`, `resolveSessionModelIdentityLiteralWithProvider`, `resolveExplicitSessionModelIdentityRef`) factor out model-resolution logic cleanly, keeping each piece small and testable. - `buildGatewaySessionRow` now dispatches on `isAcpSessionKey(key)`: ACP sessions use `resolveExplicitSessionModelIdentityRef` (returns `undefined` when no model is recorded), while regular sessions continue using `resolveSessionModelIdentityRef` (always returns a model, falling back to `DEFAULT_MODEL`). - `resolveSessionModelIdentityRef` itself also gains the ACP `runtimeOptions.model` check, making it useful as a general-purpose resolver when called directly. - Two new test suites provide regression coverage: one confirming ACP runtime model takes priority over configured defaults, another confirming that ACP sessions without a known model leave `modelProvider`/`model` as `undefined` rather than inheriting the default. - One minor style nit: `isAcpSessionKey(key)` is evaluated twice in `buildGatewaySessionRow` and could be cached in a local variable.

Confidence Score: 5/5

- Safe to merge — the fix is narrowly scoped to ACP session key detection, backed by targeted regression tests, and the refactor leaves all existing behaviour unchanged. - The change is small and well-contained: a new dispatch path for ACP keys plus three private helpers. Existing tests continue to pass, two new tests directly cover the bug and the regression. The only finding is a trivial style nit (duplicate `isAcpSessionKey` call) that has no correctness impact. - No files require special attention.
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/gateway/session-utils.ts Line: 1180-1184 Comment: **Duplicate `isAcpSessionKey` call** `isAcpSessionKey(key)` is called twice with the same argument on lines 1180 and 1184. Since the result is pure (string ops only — `trim`, `toLowerCase`, `startsWith`), it's cheap, but caching it in a local variable would make the intent clearer and avoid any future drift if the key value were somehow mutable. ```suggestion const isAcp = isAcpSessionKey(key); const resolvedModel = isAcp ? resolveExplicitSessionModelIdentityRef(cfg, entry, subagentRun?.model) : resolveSessionModelIdentityRef(cfg, entry, sessionAgentId, subagentRun?.model); const modelProvider = resolvedModel?.provider; const model = resolvedModel?.model ?? (isAcp ? undefined : DEFAULT_MODEL); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 51268 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/51268 - Title: feat(acp): add --no-device-identity flag for token-only auth - State: OPEN - Author: dinakars777 - Labels: cli, size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary - Add `--no-device-identity` CLI flag to `openclaw acp` command - Passes `deviceIdentity: null` to `GatewayClient` for token-only authentication - Reset `gatewayDeviceIdentity` in `beforeEach` for test isolation ## Issue Fixes #50671 ## Testing - Tests verify both `null` and `undefined` behavior ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-20T21:00:13Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a `--no-device-identity` flag to the `openclaw acp` command that passes `deviceIdentity: null` to `GatewayClient`, opting out of device-identity auto-loading in favour of token-only authentication. The implementation is clean and consistent: the type, server logic, standalone `parseArgs` parser, Commander CLI registration, and help text are all updated in lock-step, and the `null`-vs-`undefined` distinction correctly maps onto the existing `GatewayClient` semantics (null = disable, undefined = auto-load). **Key changes:** - `AcpServerOptions.noDeviceIdentity?: boolean` added in `types.ts` - `server.ts` passes `deviceIdentity: opts.noDeviceIdentity ? null : undefined` to `GatewayClient`; both `parseArgs` and `printHelp` handle the new flag - `acp-cli.ts` registers `.option("--no-device-identity", ..., false)` in Commander and forwards `Boolean(opts.noDeviceIdentity)` to `serveAcpGateway` — pattern matches the existing `--no-prefix-cwd` approach - Two new tests cover the `null` (flag set) and `undefined` (flag unset) paths; `gatewayDeviceIdentity` is reset in `beforeEach` for proper test isolation - `docs/cli/acp.md` Options section is **not updated** — the new flag is missing from the user-facing reference page - The `undefined` test case omits the `mockState.gatewayDeviceIdentity[0]` assertion that the `null` case includes (minor inconsistency)

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Safe to merge; implementation is logically correct and well-tested. One documentation gap and a minor test inconsistency should be addressed. - The core logic (null/undefined distinction, parseArgs, Commander registration) is correct and consistent with existing patterns. GatewayClient correctly interprets null as "skip device identity". Tests cover both branches and use proper beforeEach isolation. The only gaps are a missing entry in docs/cli/acp.md and a slightly asymmetric test assertion — neither affects runtime behaviour. - docs/cli/acp.md — the Options section does not list the new `--no-device-identity` flag

input.title: BUG: OpenClaw-managed Codex ACP uses isolated CODEX_HOME without auth bridge and sends unsupported timeout config
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #73910: BUG: OpenClaw-managed Codex ACP uses isolated CODEX_HOME without auth bridge and sends unsupported timeout config
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 73910
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/73910
- Title: BUG: OpenClaw-managed Codex ACP uses isolated CODEX_HOME without auth bridge and sends unsupported timeout config
- State: OPEN
- Author: kAIborg24
- Labels: bug, bug:behavior

Body:
```markdown
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

OpenClaw-managed Codex ACP sessions fail even though direct ACPX to Codex succeeds. The managed path prepares an isolated Codex home without Codex auth, and after auth is bridged locally it still sends an unsupported timeout config option that Codex ACP rejects.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use OpenClaw 2026.4.26 with ACP enabled and Codex available as an ACP agent.
2. Verify direct ACPX to Codex can complete a trivial prompt using the normal Codex home.
3. Run direct ACPX with an isolated CODEX_HOME that contains only an OpenClaw-style generated config file; observe that Codex reports Authentication required.
4. Add only a local auth.json bridge/symlink into that isolated Codex home and rerun direct ACPX; observe that Codex succeeds.
5. Run an OpenClaw-managed ACP Codex session with a trivial prompt.

### Expected behavior

OpenClaw-managed Codex ACP should either reuse/bridge existing Codex authentication safely or provide a documented setup flow for the isolated Codex home, then run the prompt without sending unsupported generic config options.


### Actual behavior

OpenClaw-managed Codex ACP initially fails because the isolated Codex home has no authentication. After adding a local auth bridge, the managed session advances but fails before the prompt with: Agent rejected session/set_config_option for "timeout"="120": Invalid params (ACP -32602).


### OpenClaw version

2026.4.26 (be8c246)

### Operating system

Ubuntu 26.04 LTS

### Install method

npm global install

### Model

Codex ACP default / OpenClaw-managed ACP Codex agent

### Provider / routing chain

OpenClaw managed ACP runtime -> ACPX -> Codex ACP adapter

### Additional provider/model setup details

Direct ACPX to Codex works on the same machine and authentication. The failure appears specific to OpenClaw-managed ACP session preparation/config handoff.


### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Direct ACPX with normal Codex home succeeds.
Direct ACPX with isolated Codex home lacking auth fails with: Authentication required.
Direct ACPX with isolated Codex home plus only auth.json bridge succeeds.
OpenClaw-managed Codex ACP then fails with: Agent rejected session/set_config_option for "timeout"="120": Invalid params (ACP -32602).
Removing the user-configured timeout value does not stop timeout=120 from being sent because the ACPX plugin schema applies timeoutSeconds: 120 as a default.
```

### Impact and severity

OpenClaw-managed Codex ACP is unusable in this setup even though direct ACPX and Codex authentication are healthy. This blocks using Codex through OpenClaw ACP sessions.


### Additional information

This may be the same compatibility class as unsupported generic ACP config options being sent to other ACP adapters, but Codex also has a separate isolated-auth-home issue.

```

Comments/context:
```markdown
- clawsweeper at 2026-04-29T01:19:36Z:
Codex review: keeping this open for maintainer follow-up; there is still a little grit to resolve.

**Summary**
Keep open: current main fixes the unsupported ACP timeout forwarding, but the Codex ACP authentication half remains unresolved because the managed wrapper still forces an isolated Codex home without auth reuse or a documented setup path. The open explicit-home override PR is the current candidate lane, but it still needs maintainer/security review before this issue can close.

Reproducibility: yes. Source and dependency contracts show current main still launches Codex ACP with an isolated CODEX_HOME that lacks auth while the pinned adapter reads auth from that resolved home; the issue comments provide matching real-world Ubuntu and macOS symptoms.

**Next step**
Maintainer/security review should choose the Codex ACP auth boundary and either rework or close https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77006; this is not a safe automatic fix lane while the credential contract is unsettled.

**Security**
Needs attention: The remaining work changes Codex credential boundaries and should not be automated without maintainer/security review.


<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Keep the timeout suppression and land a reviewed ACPX auth path, likely an explicit Codex-home override or documented isolated-home login flow, without default credential materialization into OpenClaw state.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes. Source and dependency contracts show current main still launches Codex ACP with an isolated CODEX_HOME that lacks auth while the pinned adapter reads auth from that resolved home; the issue comments provide matching real-world Ubuntu and macOS symptoms.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

No complete solution is on main yet. The timeout guard is the right narrow fix, but the remaining auth behavior should be solved through a maintainer/security-approved contract such as the explicit-home override lane, not by unreviewed credential copying.

Security concerns:

- [medium] Choose a reviewed Codex ACP credential boundary
  Closed bridge PRs attempted to expose or materialize Codex auth into the isolated ACPX state path, while current main deliberately avoids auth materialization; any fix needs to avoid unexpected token copies, backup exposure, or out-of-owner refresh paths.
  Confidence: 0.88

Acceptance criteria:

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.test.ts extensions/acpx/src/runtime.test.ts
- node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
- Real redacted ACPX/Codex run using the selected auth path or explicit home override

What I checked:

- **Issue discussion confirms two independent symptoms:** The reporter describes direct ACPX with normal Codex auth working, isolated CODEX_HOME failing with Authentication required, a local auth bridge getting past auth, and then Codex ACP rejecting timeout; a second macOS commenter independently confirmed both symptoms and a symlink workaround.
- **Current wrapper still forces isolated Codex home:** buildCodexAcpWrapperScript generates the Codex wrapper with CODEX_HOME set to ./codex-home before launching the Codex ACP adapter. ([`extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts:573`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/ba8a6499f02f/extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts#L573), [ba8a6499f02f](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/ba8a6499f02f))

input.title: Explicit ACP agent runs: embedded backend visibility failure and stale final JSON state after sessions_yield
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #67244: Explicit ACP agent runs: embedded backend visibility failure and stale final JSON state after sessions_yield
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 67244
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/67244
- Title: Explicit ACP agent runs: embedded backend visibility failure and stale final JSON state after sessions_yield
- State: OPEN
- Author: sazora
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, impact:message-loss, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
OpenClaw explicit `agent --json` ACP runs currently hit two separate bugs in the embedded explicit-run path. Both are fixed locally for now, but only as hotfixes in installed Homebrew `dist` files.

## Environment

- OpenClaw installed via Homebrew
- Patched runtime files currently under `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/`
- Repro workspace: `/Users/stevebot/.openclaw/workspace`
- Repro target path: `/Volumes/4TB-SSD/Projects/wrap-business`

## Repro command

```bash
openclaw agent \
  --session-id 'agent:main:discord:channel:1493603713426788494' \
  --message 'Use Codex via ACP. Create exactly one file at /Volumes/4TB-SSD/Projects/wrap-business/oc-acp-write-test-smoke.txt containing exactly: ACP SSD smoke test ok.' \
  --json \
  --timeout 180
```

Optional verification:

```bash
cat /Volumes/4TB-SSD/Projects/wrap-business/oc-acp-write-test-smoke.txt
```

## Expected behavior

- The explicit ACP run should succeed when the embedded ACP backend is healthy.
- The target file should be created with the requested contents.
- Final JSON should reflect the completed state, including:
  - `status: "ok"`
  - `finalAssistantVisibleText`
  - `replayInvalid: false`
  - `livenessState: "completed"`

## Actual behavior

Two different failures were observed:

1. **Embedded explicit ACP backend visibility failure**
   - Explicit `agent --json` ACP runs could fail with:
     - `ACP runtime backend is not configured. Install and enable the acpx runtime plugin.`
   - At the same time, gateway logs showed the backend was already healthy, e.g.:
     - `embedded acpx runtime backend ready`

2. **Stale final JSON state after `sessions_yield`**
   - Even when the ACP descendant work completed successfully and the file existed on disk, explicit `agent --json` could still return stale metadata such as:
     - `replayInvalid: true`
     - `livenessState: "working"`

## Root cause summary

### Bug 1: explicit embedded process could not see ACP backend
ACP backend registration was process-local. The gateway process had registered the embedded ACPX backend, but the explicit embedded run process performed backend lookup without ensuring local registration first.

### Bug 2: explicit final-state reconciliation depended on initial assistant text
In the explicit agent RPC path, descendant completion reconciliation after `sessions_yield` was gated on `initialAssistantText`. If the parent yielded before producing assistant-visible text, reconciliation was skipped, leaving stale final JSON metadata even though descendant ACP work had already completed.

## Minimal fix summary

### Bug 1
Before runtime backend lookup in embedded explicit ACP initialization:

1. Check whether the ACP backend is already registered in the current process.
2. If not, lazily import/register the embedded ACPX runtime.
3. Then continue with normal backend lookup.

### Bug 2
In explicit agent RPC final reconciliation:

1. Do not require `initialAssistantText` before attempting descendant follow-up reconciliation.
2. If `livenessState === "working"` or `replayInvalid === true`, still wait for descendant ACP completion.
3. If final reply text is resolved, populate final text fields and mark:
   - `livenessState = "completed"`
   - `replayInvalid = false`
4. Even if no final text exists, clear stale completion metadata once descendant work has drained.

## Verified good result after local hotfix

After local patching, the same repro succeeds:

- file created at `/Volumes/4TB-SSD/Projects/wrap-business/oc-acp-write-test-smoke.txt`
- contents: `ACP SSD smoke test ok.`
- final JSON returns completed state, including:
  - `status: "ok"`
  - `summary: "completed"`
  - `finalAssistantVisibleText: "Done."`
  - `replayInvalid: false`
  - `livenessState: "completed"`

## Local hotfix note

The current fixes are **only local hotfixes in installed Homebrew `dist` files**, not source-level upstream fixes.

Patched files locally were:

- `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/manager-szyCqvou.js`
- `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/server.impl-BbJvXoPb.js`

## Suggested follow-up

Please patch the source that compiles into those runtime files and add regression coverage for:

- explicit `openclaw agent --json`
- `runtime: "acp"`
- `sessions_yield`
- descendant completion reconciliation
- final JSON metadata cleanup
- outside-workspace/SSD ACP write smoke path

```

**Summary**
Keep open. Current main and the latest release still preserve the ACPX backend visibility gap and the raw terminal JSON response path; the related open reports do not supersede the ACP-specific backend failure.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main still has a JSON agent path that skips ACPX plugin preload before embedded fallback, a process-local ACP backend registry, and a Gateway final response path that returns raw agent metadata without descendant-drained reconciliation.

**Next step**
A maintainer should decide whether to split the narrow ACPX backend activation from the broader terminal liveness/completed JSON contract before a repair lane changes public run metadata semantics.


<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Patch source-level ACPX activation for explicit embedded ACP execution, then implement terminal JSON reconciliation after yielded descendants drain in the shared Gateway/agent lifecycle contract rather than as a dist-only hotfix.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes, at source level. Current main still has a JSON agent path that skips ACPX plugin preload before embedded fallback, a process-local ACP backend registry, and a Gateway final response path that returns raw agent metadata without descendant-drained reconciliation.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

Partly. Lazy ACPX activation is the narrow source fix for the backend visibility failure; the final JSON cleanup should be aligned with the related foreground descendant lifecycle contract before adding a new `completed` liveness state.

Label justifications:

- `P2`: This is a source-backed ACP explicit-run bug with real user impact but a limited ACP/agent JSON surface.
- `impact:message-loss`: The final assistant-visible text can be absent from the explicit JSON result even after descendant ACP work completed.
- `impact:session-state`: The issue is centered on stale `replayInvalid` and `livenessState` metadata after yielded descendant session work drains.

What I checked:

- **live-issue-context:** The issue is open, has no labels, and live GitHub reports no closing pull requests; the same-title predecessor was closed by the reporter only to repost from a browser-verifiable path.
- **json-agent-skips-plugin-preload:** Current main still loads plugins for `agent` only when `--local` is present or output is not JSON, so plain `openclaw agent --json` does not preload ACPX in the CLI process. ([`src/cli/command-catalog.ts:61`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/ecb6da9289b1/src/cli/command-catalog.ts#L61), [ecb6da9289b1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/ecb6da9289b1))
- **startup-policy-pins-json-skip:** The startup policy test still asserts that `openclaw agent --json` does not load plugins while `openclaw agent --json --local` does, preserving the backend visibility risk. ([`src/cli/command-startup-policy.test.ts:98`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/ecb6da9289b1/src/cli/command-startup-policy.test.ts#L98), [ecb6da9289b1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/ecb6da9289b1))
- **embedded-fallback-has-no-acpx-activation:** When gateway transport fails or times out, `agentCliCommand` falls back to `agentCommand(...)` without any source-level ACPX backend activation before the embedded run. ([`src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts:270`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/ecb6da9289b1/src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts#L270), [ecb6da9289b1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/ecb6da9289b1))
- **backend-registry-still-process-local:** `requireAcpRuntimeBackend()` still reads the current process registry and throws the reported missing-backend error when no backend is registered locally. ([`src/acp/runtime/registry.ts:88`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/ecb6da9289b1/src/acp/runtime/registry.ts#L88), [ecb6da9289b1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/ecb6da9289b1))
- **gateway-final-response-returns-raw-result:** The Gateway `agent` method still builds the final payload directly from `agentCommandFromIngress` result and responds with that payload; no descendant-drained JSON metadata reconciliation is visible in this path. ([`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts:514`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/ecb6da9289b1/src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts#L514), [ecb6da9289b1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/ecb6da9289b1))

Likely related people:

- **@vincentkoc:** Authored the ACPX lazy-load startup backend work touching bundled ACPX registration and the plugin-sdk ACP runtime backend facade, directly adjacent to missing local backend activation. (role: recent ACPX backend contributor; confidence: high; commits: [6d7901f5c893](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/6d7901f5c893a997f917a07ff963b65fb37d4dcf); files: `extensions/acpx/register.runtime.ts`, `extensions/acpx/src/service.ts`, `src/plugin-sdk/acp-runtime-backend.ts`)
- **@steipete:** Authored ACPX startup-probe readiness and recent Gateway agent/server-methods work near backend availability and final response emission. (role: recent ACPX readiness and gateway-area contributor; confidence: high; commits: [c18cc769ce71](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c18cc769ce7191093d972b530fcdd1fe18e1e594), [c6ddb1afb7bc](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c6ddb1afb7bc823aafe4697366bbd890e3d32da6); files: `extensions/acpx/register.runtime.ts`, `extensions/acpx/src/service.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts`)
- **@tyler6204:** Authored descendant gating and frozen-result refresh work around `sessions_yield` completion delivery, adjacent to stale final metadata after yielded descendants settle. (role: subagent/yield behavior contributor; confidence: medium; commits: [81b93b9ce04b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/81b93b9ce04b7b1af6c9e6e8c88b657bc79b12b7); files: `src/agents/subagent-registry.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-announce.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-registry.lifecycle-retry-grace.e2e.test.ts`)

(role: adjacent ACP/Gateway contributor; confidence: medium; commits: [86c1622a3a3c](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/86c1622a3a3ccf9b5f1d940fbad5eef5f6431dce); files: `src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts`, `src/agents/agent-command.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts`)

Greptile Summary

This PR introduces distributed ACP session locking with a clean three-tier abstraction (`LocalSessionLockManager` → `RedisSessionLockManager` → `FailClosedSessionLockManager`). The overall design is solid: owner-checked Lua scripts ensure atomic release/renew, the singleton factory correctly fails-closed when Redis is configured but broken, and the wiring in `tryDispatchAcpReply` (acquire before the turn, best-effort renewal, guaranteed release in `finally`) is correct. Test coverage is thorough. **Issues found:** - **No per-command timeout after TCP connection** (`session-lock-manager.ts` ~line 731): `rawSocket.setTimeout(10_000)` is removed by `cleanup()` once the socket connects. Subsequent `sendCommand` calls for AUTH, SELECT, and the actual lock operations have no timeout, so a Redis instance that accepts connections but then hangs will block `acquire` indefinitely — bypassing the fail-closed path and stalling ACP dispatch. - **New TCP connection per Redis command** (`session-lock-manager.ts` ~line 771): `createRedisCommandRunner` opens a fresh connection for each `acquire`, `release`, and `renew` call, serializing TCP handshake + AUTH + optional SELECT + command. This is expensive on the lock hot path; a shared persistent connection would reduce overhead significantly. - **Undocumented silent fallback to `REDIS_URL`** (`session-lock-manager.ts` ~line 948): If `OPENCLAW_ACP_SESSION_LOCK_REDIS_URL` is unset, the code silently falls back to the generic `REDIS_URL` env var. In environments where `REDIS_URL` points to a shared cache or queue, this would unexpectedly enable Redis-backed locking against the wrong instance without any warning.

Confidence Score: 3/5

- Functional with correctness caveats — a hanging Redis connection will bypass fail-closed and stall ACP dispatch indefinitely, which needs fixing before production use at scale. - The logic for lock ownership, fail-closed behavior, and release-in-finally is correct and well-tested. However, the custom RESP implementation lacks per-command timeouts after TCP connection, meaning a slow/hung Redis instance degrades into an indefinite stall rather than a fast fail. The per-command connection creation also adds meaningful latency on every ACP turn. These issues lower confidence from a would-be 4–5 to a 3. - src/auto-reply/reply/session-lock-manager.ts — specifically the connectRedis/sendCommand timeout handling and createRedisCommandRunner connection lifecycle.

Comments Outside Diff (3)

 1. `src/auto-reply/reply/session-lock-manager.ts`, line 731-782 ([link](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d0d33ebc2215540f5a23b3b1c2ac69b6e3d1415d/src/auto-reply/reply/session-lock-manager.ts#L731-L782)) **No per-command timeout after TCP connection** `rawSocket.setTimeout(10_000)` only guards the TCP handshake phase. Once the socket emits `connect`, `cleanup()` removes the timeout handler — so all subsequent `sendCommand` calls (AUTH, SELECT, and the actual lock operations) have **no timeout**. If Redis accepts the TCP connection but then stalls (network partition, Redis backlog, or a slow instance), `sendCommand` will `await` a Promise that never resolves, blocking the caller indefinitely. In `dispatch-acp.ts` this means every ACP dispatch will hang — worse than a hard failure, which would at least trigger the fail-closed path. Consider adding a per-command timeout (e.g., via `AbortSignal` or a `Promise.race` with a timeout) for each `sendCommand` call so that a hanging Redis response is treated as an error and the fail-closed path is taken: ```typescript async sendCommand(args: string[], timeoutMs = 10_000): Promise { // ...existing code... return await Promise.race([ new Promise((resolve, reject) => { this.pending = { resolve, reject }; }), new Promise((_, reject) => setTimeout(() => reject(new Error("Redis command timed out")), timeoutMs) ), ]); } ```

Path: src/auto-reply/reply/session-lock-manager.ts Line: 731-782 Comment: **No per-command timeout after TCP connection** `rawSocket.setTimeout(10_000)` only guards the TCP handshake phase. Once the socket emits `connect`, `cleanup()` removes the timeout handler — so all subsequent `sendCommand` calls (AUTH, SELECT, and the actual lock operations) have **no timeout**. If Redis accepts the TCP connection but then stalls (network partition, Redis backlog, or a slow instance), `sendCommand` will `await` a Promise that never resolves, blocking the caller indefinitely. In `dispatch-acp.ts` this means every ACP dispatch will hang — worse than a hard failure, which would at least trigger the fail-closed path. Consider adding a per-command timeout (e.g., via `AbortSignal` or a `Promise.race` with a timeout) for each `sendCommand` call so that a hanging Redis response is treated as an error and the fail-closed path is taken: ```typescript async sendCommand(args: string[], timeoutMs = 10_000): Promise { // ...existing code... return await Promise.race([ new Promise((resolve, reject) => { this.pending = { resolve, reject }; }), new Promise((_, reject) => setTimeout(() => reject(new Error("Redis command timed out")), timeoutMs) ), ]); } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
2. `src/auto-reply/reply/session-lock-manager.ts`, line 771-782 ([link](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d0d33ebc2215540f5a23b3b1c2ac69b6e3d1415d/src/auto-reply/reply/session-lock-manager.ts#L771-L782)) **New TCP connection per Redis command is expensive** `createRedisCommandRunner` opens a fresh TCP (or TLS) connection for each call to `acquire`, `release`, or `renew`. Because `connectRedis` also sends AUTH and SELECT before the actual command, a single `acquire` costs at least 3 serial round trips (connect → AUTH → actual command). With `renewEveryMs = Math.floor(lockTtlMs / 2)` (default 60 s), that's another 2–3 RTTs every minute while a turn is in flight. On the critical path to ACP dispatch this adds measurable latency and connection churn. Sharing a single long-lived `RedisSocketConnection` per `RedisSessionLockManager` instance (reconnecting on error) would cut this down to a single round trip per operation.

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

- Let Commander own the negated option default for --no-prefix-cwd.
- Read the positive prefixCwd option that Commander actually populates.
- Add ACP CLI coverage for both the explicit opt-out and the default behavior.

Closes #83901.

## Verification

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/acp-cli.option-collisions.test.ts src/acp/translator.prompt-prefix.test.ts
- Crabbox AWS cbx_1689d0ad78e9, run run_a406418db6fe: regression failed before the source patch, then the focused ACP CLI and translator tests passed after the patch (15/15).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: openclaw acp --no-prefix-cwd now forwards prefixCwd=false into the ACP bridge so prompts are not prefixed with [Working directory: ...]; the default path still forwards prefixCwd=true.
Real environment tested: Fresh checkout of this PR branch after pnpm install and pnpm build, using a live ACP translator harness from the checkout; maintainer follow-up also verified focused behavior on AWS Crabbox Linux lease cbx_1689d0ad78e9.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Build the PR checkout, run the ACP translator harness once with the default prefixCwd path and once with the --no-prefix-cwd / prefixCwd=false path, then run node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/acp-cli.option-collisions.test.ts src/acp/translator.prompt-prefix.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose.
Evidence after fix: The live ACP translator harness printed this after-fix output:

    default prefixCwd=true
    [Working directory: ~/openclaw-test]

    hello
    ---
    with --no-prefix-cwd / prefixCwd=false
    hello

Observed result after fix: The default path still prefixes the working directory, and the --no-prefix-cwd path suppresses that prefix. Focused tests also passed with 15 passed; the same regression test failed before the source patch with prefixCwd received as true.
What was not tested: No long-running interactive ACP server was left running; the live proof used a bounded ACP translator harness and the maintainer proof used focused CLI/translator tests.

```

input.title: ACP session/update usage_update notification fails validation when used=null (Invalid params / expected object, received undefined)
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #66467: ACP session/update usage_update notification fails validation when used=null (Invalid params / expected object, received undefined)
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 66467
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66467
- Title: ACP session/update usage_update notification fails validation when used=null (Invalid params / expected object, received undefined)
- State: OPEN
- Author: fisher1006
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Discord-bound ACP sessions can fail during `session/update` notification handling when the ACP runtime emits a `usage_update` notification with `used: null`.

In the observed failure, OpenClaw logs:

```text
Error handling notification {
  jsonrpc: '2.0',
  method: 'session/update',
  params: {
    sessionId: 'ba706432-d8f3-431b-ad57-bd0f0a5dc131',
    update: {
      sessionUpdate: 'usage_update',
      used: null,
      size: 200000,
      cost: [Object]
    }
  }
} {
  code: -32602,
  message: 'Invalid params'
}
```

And the validation payload includes repeated schema errors such as:

```text
Invalid input: expected object, received undefined
sessionUpdate: { _errors: [Array] }
title: { _errors: [Array] }
toolCallId: { _errors: [Array] }
entries: { _errors: [Array] }
availableCommands: { _errors: [Array] }
currentModeId: { _errors: [Array] }
configOptions: { _errors: [Array] }
used: { _errors: [Array] }
```

This appears to be a runtime/schema mismatch in OpenClaw's ACP notification handling, not a generic "session missing" issue.

## Why this seems distinct from existing ACP/Discord issues

I checked existing issues around:

- Discord-bound ACP persistent resume failures / binding restore
- generic `ACP_TURN_FAILED`
- Discord slash/interaction `40060 Interaction has already been acknowledged`

Those are related symptoms in the same area, but this repro is narrower:

- the ACP session **does exist**
- the runtime **does emit** `session/update`
- the failure happens when OpenClaw handles a specific `usage_update` payload
- the bad payload shape includes `used: null`

So this looks like a notification schema/validator bug, not just a Discord binding problem.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: local main install on macOS arm64
- OS: macOS Darwin 25.x (Apple Silicon)
- Channel: Discord
- ACP backend: bundled `acpx`
- Runtime context: Discord-bound ACP thread/session path

## Evidence

### 1) ACP sessions are being created

`openclaw status` showed Discord ACP binding sessions being created successfully, so this is not a total runtime outage and not simply "session was never created".

### 2) Runtime code suggests `usage_update` should be accepted

In bundled ACPX runtime code:

`/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/acpx/node_modules/acpx/dist/runtime.js`

there is explicit handling for `usage_update`:

```js
case "usage_update": {
  const used = asOptionalFiniteNumber(payload.used);
  const size = asOptionalFiniteNumber(payload.size);
  return {
    type: "status",
    text: used != null && size != null ? `usage updated: ${used}/${size}` : "usage updated",
    tag: "usage_update",
    ...used != null ? { used } : {},
    ...size != null ? { size } : {}
  };
}
```

This implies `usage_update` is a known notification type and `used` may be absent/non-finite without being fatal at the runtime projection layer.

### 3) But gateway notification handling rejects the incoming notification

From `gateway.err.log` on 2026-04-14:

```text
2026-04-14T11:46:11.775+08:00 Error handling notification {
  jsonrpc: '2.0',
  method: 'session/update',
  params: {
    sessionId: 'ba706432-d8f3-431b-ad57-bd0f0a5dc131',
    update: {
      sessionUpdate: 'usage_update',
      used: null,
      size: 200000,
      cost: [Object]
    }
  }
} {
  code: -32602,
  message: 'Invalid params',
  data: {
    _errors: [
      'Invalid input: expected object, received undefined',
      ...
    ],
    sessionUpdate: { _errors: [Array] },
    title: { _errors: [Array] },
    toolCallId: { _errors: [Array] },
    entries: { _errors: [Array] },
    availableCommands: { _errors: [Array] },
    currentModeId: { _errors: [Array] },
    configOptions: { _errors: [Array] },
    used: { _errors: [Array] }
  }
}
```

The same error repeated several times for the same session id.

## Suspected root cause

The likely root cause is that OpenClaw validates `session/update` notifications against an overly strict or wrong schema shape for ACP session updates.

More specifically, one of these is probably true:

1. The gateway expects `used` to be an object/number in a stricter union, but ACPX can emit `used: null`.
2. The gateway is validating a `usage_update` payload against a union/schema intended for multiple update variants, and missing optional fields are being treated as required object fields.
3. There is a mismatch between the `session/update` envelope shape emitted by bundled `acpx` and the schema expected by the OpenClaw ACP notification handler.

The repeated `_errors` for unrelated fields (`title`, `toolCallId`, `entries`, `availableCommands`, `currentModeId`, `configOptions`) suggest a broken discriminated-union parse or wrong schema selection, not just one bad field.

## Related symptom (probably secondary, not root cause)

In the same investigation window, Discord also logged:

```text
DiscordError: Interaction has already been acknowledged.
discordCode: 40060
```

and slow `INTERACTION_CREATE` listener warnings.

That looks like a downstream delivery/interaction symptom, but not the root cause of the ACP notification parse failure above.

## Expected behavior

OpenClaw should accept ACP `session/update` notifications for `usage_update` when fields like `used` are `null` or omitted, or at minimum ignore non-critical usage fields instead of failing the whole notification.

## Actual behavior

OpenClaw rejects the notification with:

```text
code: -32602
message: Invalid params
```

and the ACP turn/path becomes unstable in Discord-bound sessions.

## What would help

- Identify the exact schema/parser used for `session/update` notifications in the gateway ACP integration.
- Confirm whether `usage_update.used = null` is intended/allowed from bundled `acpx`.
- Relax or correct the schema so optional/variant fields do not make the entire notification invalid.
- If this is a bundled version skew between OpenClaw and ACPX, document the compatible versions or pin the bundled pair.

```

input.title: [Bug]: ACP configured binding uses parent channel ID for session key — all threads under same channel share one persistent Claude Code process
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #64199: [Bug]: ACP configured binding uses parent channel ID for session key — all threads under same channel share one persistent Claude Code process
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 64199
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/64199
- Title: [Bug]: ACP configured binding uses parent channel ID for session key — all threads under same channel share one persistent Claude Code process
- State: OPEN
- Author: hoh-dev-bot
- Labels: P1, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, impact:security, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Bug

When an agent uses `runtime.type: \"acp\"` with `mode: \"persistent\"` and a Discord channel binding, **all Discord threads spawned from the same parent channel share a single persistent Claude Code process**. This causes cross-thread context contamination — thread A's agent is aware of thread B's conversation history.

## Root Cause

`buildConfiguredAcpSessionKey` hashes the **configured channel's `peer.id`** (parent channel ID) as `conversationId`, not the individual thread ID:

\`\`\`javascript
// dist/binding-registry-R_XxXX6M.js:26-35
function buildBindingHash(params) {
    return createHash("sha256")
        .update(\`\${params.channel}:\${params.accountId}:\${params.conversationId}\`)
        .digest("hex").slice(0, 16);
}
function buildConfiguredAcpSessionKey(spec) {
    const hash = buildBindingHash({ channel, accountId, conversationId });
    return \`agent:\${agentId}:acp:binding:\${channel}:\${accountId}:\${hash}\`;
}
\`\`\`

Since `conversationId` = the configured `peer.id` (parent channel), every thread from that channel resolves to the **same session key** → same `acpxRecordId` → same entry in `AcpRuntimeManager.pendingPersistentClients` → **same Claude Code process**.

Verified by hashing sima's two configured Discord parent channels:

\`\`\`python
import hashlib
hashlib.sha256("discord:default:1471512512175407297".encode()).hexdigest()[:16]
# → "17afdbb6be5eba6c"  ✅ matches actual acpx state record on disk

hashlib.sha256("discord:default:1475705305584373780".encode()).hexdigest()[:16]
# → "a709dafc3a7c44dc"  ✅ matches actual acpx state record on disk
\`\`\`

Despite 20+ Discord threads each having their own entry in `sessions.json` (`agent:sima:discord:channel:<threadId>`), the acpx state directory only contains **2 sima binding records** — one per parent channel, not one per thread.

## Contrast with CLI backend

CLI and native backends use `buildAgentPeerSessionKey` which correctly uses the thread ID as `peerId`:

\`\`\`javascript
// session-key-BMb3Kc4r.js:173
return \`agent:\${agentId}:\${channel}:\${peerKind}:\${peerId}\`;
// → "agent:sima:discord:channel:<threadId>"  — per thread ✅
\`\`\`

Only `runtime.type: "acp"` with a configured channel binding is affected.

## Layer comparison

| Layer | Key format | Granularity |
|-------|-----------|-------------|
| OpenClaw session store (sessions.json) | `agent:sima:discord:channel:<threadId>` | per thread ✅ |
| ACP binding session (acpx state) | `agent:sima:acp:binding:discord:default:<hash>` | per **parent channel** ⚠️ |
| AcpRuntimeManager process map | same as above | per **parent channel** ⚠️ |

## Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.4.10 (ab9be8d)
- Discord plugin
- macOS Darwin 25.3.0

## Config

\`\`\`json
"runtime": {
  "type": "acp",
  "acp": {
    "agent": "claude",
    "backend": "acpx",
    "mode": "persistent"
  }
}
\`\`\`

Bindings in `channels.discord` routes:
\`\`\`json
{ "agentId": "sima", "match": { "channel": "discord", "peer": { "kind": "channel", "id": "<parentChannelId>" } } }
\`\`\`

## Expected

Each Discord thread should resolve to its own ACP session key (using thread ID), so `mode: "persistent"` gives each thread an independent Claude Code process with isolated context.

## Actual

All threads under the same configured parent channel share one persistent Claude Code process. Context accumulates across threads.

## Workaround

Setting `mode: "oneshot"` isolates each turn but loses persistent session efficiency.

`spawnAcpSessions: true` appears designed to solve this but is currently broken on Discord (#63927, #64017).

## Related

- #63927 — ACP sessions_spawn thread binding fails on Discord
- #64017 — `/acp spawn --thread auto` fails with binding adapter error
- #62685 — Discord ACP child thread binding passes channel ID into REST lookup
- #60737 — Feature: Per-DM/per-chat default ACP binding for auto-spawning on new topics
```

input.title: [Feature]: Support ACP configured bindings for Telegram DMs (not just groups/topics)
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #62769: [Feature]: Support ACP configured bindings for Telegram DMs (not just groups/topics)
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 62769
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/62769
- Title: [Feature]: Support ACP configured bindings for Telegram DMs (not just groups/topics)
- State: OPEN
- Author: richardmqq
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, impact:message-loss, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Telegram ACP configured bindings (`bindings[]` with `type: "acp"`) only work for group chats and forum topics, not for direct messages (DMs). This prevents using persistent ACP sessions in Telegram DM conversations with dedicated agent bots.

## Root Cause

In `normalizeTelegramAcpConversationId()`, the function explicitly rejects any `chatId` that does not start with `"-"` (i.e., group chat IDs):

```javascript
function normalizeTelegramAcpConversationId(conversationId) {
    const parsed = parseTelegramTopicConversation({ conversationId });
    if (!parsed || !parsed.chatId.startsWith("-")) return null;
    // ...
}
```

This means DM peer IDs (positive integers like `6689123501`) are filtered out, and the binding is silently ignored.

Similarly, `matchTelegramAcpConversation()` checks `incoming.chatId.startsWith("-")`, which also rejects DM conversations.

## Expected Behavior

A configured binding like this should work for Telegram DMs:

```json
{
  "type": "acp",
  "agentId": "claude",
  "match": {
    "channel": "telegram",
    "accountId": "claude",
    "peer": { "kind": "direct", "id": "6689123501" }
  },
  "acp": { "mode": "persistent" }
}
```

When a user sends a message to the dedicated agent bot in DM, it should route through the ACP binding and create/resume an ACP harness session, just like it does for group topics.

## Actual Behavior

- The binding passes `openclaw config validate` ✅
- But at runtime, `normalizeTelegramAcpConversationId()` returns `null` for DM peer IDs
- The binding is silently ignored
- Messages fall through to native embedded runtime instead of ACP harness

## Use Case

We run dedicated Telegram bots for different ACP agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.). Each bot has its own `accountId`. Users DM these bots expecting to interact with the actual ACP harness (e.g., Claude Code CLI). Without DM binding support, there is no way to persistently bind a Telegram DM conversation to an ACP session through config.

Currently, the only workaround is `/acp spawn` in a group topic, which defeats the purpose of having dedicated DM bots.

## Additional Context

- Gateway restart can cause existing ACP session metadata to be lost from `sessions.json`, making the DM → ACP routing even less reliable
- `agents.list[].runtime.type: "acp"` does NOT automatically route DM messages through ACP — it only provides defaults for `/acp spawn`
- The `acpManager.resolveSession()` check in `agentCommandInternal()` returns `kind: "none"` for DM sessions without `.acp` metadata, falling through to native embedded runtime

## Environment

- OpenClaw version: latest (2026.4.5+)
- Channel: Telegram
- ACP backend: acpx (embedded)
```

input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #60979: feature: sessions_spawn ACP delivery to channel (stream output to Zulip/Discord topic)

GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 60979
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/60979
- Title: feature: sessions_spawn ACP delivery to channel (stream output to Zulip/Discord topic)
- State: OPEN
- Author: app/spark-of-imagination
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:message-loss, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

When spawning an ACP session via `sessions_spawn` (e.g. OpenCode/Codex), there is no way to direct the session's output to a specific channel conversation or topic. This makes it impossible to observe long-running agent tasks in real-time from a chat surface without the parent agent acting as a relay.

## Desired Workflow

```
User ↔ Mimiron (main agent) — discuss task
Mimiron → spawns Jeeves
Jeeves → spawns OpenCode ACP session, output bound to Zulip stream topic
User watches progress live in Zulip topic
Jeeves/Mimiron free to do other things
```

## Current State

- `streamTo: "parent"` exists but only relays events back to the spawning agent (not to a channel)
- `cron` jobs support `delivery.mode: "announce"` which routes output to a channel — no equivalent exists for `sessions_spawn` ACP
- The Zulip channel plugin already implements `ChannelConversationBindingSupport` with `supportsCurrentConversationBinding: true` and a full `SessionBindingAdapter` — the adapter infrastructure is ready

## Proposed Solution

Add a `delivery` option to `sessions_spawn` for `runtime="acp"` sessions, analogous to cron job delivery:

```json
{
  "runtime": "acp",
  "agentId": "opencode",
  "task": "...",
  "delivery": {
    "mode": "announce",
    "channel": "zulip",
    "to": "stream:Projects:agent-output"
  }
}
```

This would wire the ACP session output to the specified channel conversation, leveraging the existing `SessionBindingAdapter` infrastructure already present in channel plugins.

## References

- Internal research issue: whizzlelabs/sparkofimagination#23 (options A/B/C analysis)
- Zulip plugin binding adapter: `whizzlelabs/openclaw-zulip` — `src/bindings.ts` — `createZulipSessionBindingAdapter`, `zulipConversationBindingsSupport`
- Cron delivery precedent: `cron` jobs support `delivery.mode: "announce"` for channel output routing

```

input.title: ACP/Codex child tasks can be marked succeeded with progress-only output and no final deliverable
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #83863: ACP/Codex child tasks can be marked succeeded with progress-only output and no final deliverable
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 83863
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/83863
- Title: ACP/Codex child tasks can be marked succeeded with progress-only output and no final deliverable
- State: OPEN
- Author: chac4l
- Labels: P1, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, impact:message-loss, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

ACP/Codex child sessions can terminate with only interim/progress text (for example "I'll inspect...", "I'm checking...", "I'm verifying now...") and OpenClaw records the durable task as `succeeded` / `outcome.status=ok` even though no final deliverable was captured and the completion announcement later fails.

This makes the parent agent correctly distrust the child ("Codex returned only progress, not delivery"), but the task ledger and UI still present the child run as successful. Operationally this looks like Codex/OpenClaw silently succeeded while no PR/diff/final report was delivered.

## Evidence Pattern

Observed on a real background ACP workflow. Operational identifiers and deployment details are intentionally redacted.

The affected child sessions had transcripts shaped like:

```text
user: <task requiring concrete deliverables>
assistant: I'll inspect/check/verify/merge now... <progress-only text>
```

The durable task state then showed rows equivalent to:

```text
runtime=acp       status=succeeded  deliveryStatus=session_queued  progressSummary=<progress-only text>
runtime=subagent  status=succeeded  deliveryStatus=failed          progressSummary=<progress-only text>
runtime=cli       status=succeeded  deliveryStatus=not_applicable
```

A completion retry path also logged retry exhaustion with `completion agent did not deliver through the message tool`, but the top-level task status still stayed `succeeded`.

## Expected

For child sessions that explicitly expect a completion message/final deliverable:

- progress-only output should not be treated as a clean successful deliverable;
- durable task status should surface `blocked`/`failed`/`needs final deliverable`, not plain `succeeded`;
- parent flow and UI should make it obvious the child did not complete the contract;
- if completion delivery fails after retries, the task should not appear as success without an error surface.

## Actual

- ACP manager treats terminal done event as `status=succeeded` using interim `progressSummary`.
- Subagent registry freezes latest assistant text as `frozenResultText` even when it is progress-only.
- Delivery failure is visible in `deliveryStatus=failed`, but top-level `status=succeeded` remains, so UI and task ledger look successful.

## Local Operational Mitigation

A temporary installed-build mitigation was applied on the affected deployment to classify likely progress-only ACP/subagent summaries as a blocked terminal outcome instead of a clean success. This is an operational mitigation only; upstream should replace it with a structured completion contract.

Validation used syntax checks on the modified runtime files plus a gateway restart and health/channel checks. Deployment-specific paths, session ids, channel ids, and host details are intentionally omitted.

## Suggested Durable Fix

1. Track final vs interim assistant output in the ACP/Codex bridge instead of relying on the last assistant text blob.
2. When `expectsCompletionMessage=true`, require either an explicit final answer, final tool result, or completion artifact before marking the task as successful.
3. Make delivery failure participate in terminal task state or flow status, not only `deliveryStatus`, so UI cannot display success while completion delivery failed.
4. Add regression tests for:
   - assistant emits only "I'll do X" and ACP ends;
   - assistant emits "I'm verifying now" and ACP ends;
   - delivery fails after retry-limit;
   - real final report remains `succeeded`.

## Related

This is distinct from issue #83836 (`invalid_encrypted_content` replay failure). Both affect completion reliability, but this issue is specifically the progress-only / false-success completion contract.
```

- Maintainers can comment `@clawsweeper explain` to ask for more context, or `@clawsweeper stop` to stop active automation.

</details>

**Summary**
Keep open. Current main has partial startTurn-result handling for Codex ACP, but the durable task contract still permits legacy ACP done events and subagent ok outcomes to surface progress text as successful task state, and completion delivery failure remains represented as deliveryStatus rather than a blocked terminal outcome; the related invalid-encrypted-content report is adjacent but distinct: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/83836.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and incident evidence, though not by executing a local replay in this read-only pass. Simulating a legacy ACP turn that emits only progress text then done, or a subagent ok outcome whose required completion delivery exhausts retries, follows current code to succeeded unless blocked terminal metadata is explicitly set.

**Next step**
The source path is narrow enough for a focused repair across ACP/subagent task finalization and regression tests, without a new config option or product decision.


<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Carry an explicit final-deliverable and completion-delivery contract through ACP and subagent task finalization, mapping missing final output or exhausted required completion delivery to the existing blocked terminal outcome while preserving real final reports as succeeded.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes from source and incident evidence, though not by executing a local replay in this read-only pass. Simulating a legacy ACP turn that emits only progress text then done, or a subagent ok outcome whose required completion delivery exhausts retries, follows current code to succeeded unless blocked terminal metadata is explicitly set.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

Yes, the requested direction is the maintainable fix if it uses structured completion and delivery state rather than broad last-text guessing. The repair should reuse terminalOutcome=blocked and add regressions for missing final output, failed required delivery, and valid final output.

Label justifications:

- `P1`: A live ACP/Codex child-task workflow can show success even when the parent receives no final deliverable.
- `impact:message-loss`: The reported failure is a missing completion message or final deliverable being hidden behind successful task state.
- `impact:session-state`: Durable task and flow state can diverge from the child session's actual completion contract.

Acceptance criteria:

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.capture-completion-reply.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts src/tasks/task-flow-registry.test.ts src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts src/tasks/task-executor.test.ts
- git diff --check

What I checked:

- **Issue evidence:** The issue body and follow-up comment report real ACP/subagent child runs on v2026.5.18 where transcripts contained only progress text, durable rows stayed status=succeeded, and completion announce retry failed or hit missing ACP metadata.
- **ACP progress can become terminal success:** ACP output text is accumulated as progressSummary, and after a terminal ACP event current main calls markBackgroundTaskTerminal with status succeeded unless resolveBackgroundTaskTerminalResult identifies a narrow blocked pattern. ([`src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts:914`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a559ccc0844a/src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts#L914), [a559ccc0844a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a559ccc0844a))
- **ACP blocked detection is narrow:** resolveBackgroundTaskTerminalResult only recognizes permission/writable-session/apply_patch blockers, not generic progress-only text such as “I'll inspect” or “I'm verifying now.”. ([`src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts:124`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a559ccc0844a/src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts#L124), [a559ccc0844a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a559ccc0844a))
- **Legacy ACP done still means completed:** The acpx legacy runTurn adapter settles any done event as status=completed, so runtimes without structured startTurn failure metadata can still send progress text plus done into the success path. ([`extensions/acpx/src/service.ts:150`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a559ccc0844a/extensions/acpx/src/service.ts#L150), [a559ccc0844a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a559ccc0844a))
- **Subagent ok finalization completes before delivery result:** safeFinalizeSubagentTaskRun marks outcome.status ok with completeTaskRunByRunId and uses frozenResultText as progressSummary; completeSubagentRun calls this before the announce cleanup/delivery path runs. ([`src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts:189`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a559ccc0844a/src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts#L189), [a559ccc0844a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a559ccc0844a))
- **Delivery failure stays separate from success:** When final delivery is suspended after retry exhaustion, the lifecycle code sets deliveryStatus failed but does not change the already completed subagent terminal status into blocked/failed. ([`src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts:391`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a559ccc0844a/src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts#L391), [a559ccc0844a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/a559ccc0844a))

Likely related people:

- **Tyler Yust:** Authored the major subagent completion-delivery change that introduced descendant gating, frozen result refresh, and cron retry behavior in the affected subagent announce/finalization area. (role: feature-history contributor; confidence: medium; commits: [81b93b9ce04b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/81b93b9ce04b7b1af6c9e6e8c88b657bc79b12b7); files: `src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-announce-output.ts`)
- **Mariano:** Authored the blocked ACP task outcome and blocked-flow state work that defines how terminalOutcome=blocked maps into task and flow status. (role: task-state feature contributor; confidence: medium; commits: [5ef42fc8568e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/5ef42fc8568eb51eaf3e9cb32ab10e525df9f238), [8d942000c980](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/8d942000c980c9f3b1ed5bc896af045ba1b645af); files: `src/tasks/task-flow-registry.ts`, `src/tasks/task-registry.ts`, `src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts`)

input.title: sessions_spawn lacks --bind here equivalent — agent cannot bind ACP session to existing Discord thread
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #58411: sessions_spawn lacks --bind here equivalent — agent cannot bind ACP session to existing Discord thread

- Title: sessions_spawn lacks --bind here equivalent — agent cannot bind ACP session to existing Discord thread
- State: OPEN
- Author: brew-agent
