GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 48834 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/48834 - Title: feat(feishu): enable /focus and /unfocus commands + fix ACP block delivery - State: OPEN - Author: David553 - Labels: channel: feishu, size: L, extensions: acpx, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary The core Feishu session binding adapter and subagent hooks were landed in #46819. This PR adds the remaining pieces to make `/focus` fully functional on Feishu: - **`/focus` and `/unfocus` commands**: Allow Feishu as a valid channel, add `resolveFeishuFocusConversationId` for stable conversation binding (rejects group chats), update command registry - **ACP block delivery fix**: Add `promoteAcpBlocksToFinals` dispatcher capability so Feishu can opt-in to promoting ACP blocks to finals (needed because Feishu suppresses block streaming), while preserving Discord's native block handling - **Gateway lifecycle**: Initialize thread binding manager on `startAccount`, clean up on `stopAccount` - **Config**: Add `threadBindings` schema (enabled, idleHours, maxAgeHours, spawnAcpSessions) - **Plugin SDK**: Export thread-bindings helpers for Feishu - **Provider prefix normalization**: Strip `feishu:`/`lark:` prefixes before resolving targets ## Changed files (16 files, +263/-18) | Area | Files | |------|-------| | /focus commands | `action-focus.ts`, `action-unfocus.ts`, `shared.ts`, `feishu-context.ts` (new), `channel-context.ts`, `session.ts`, `commands-registry.data.ts` | | ACP delivery | `dispatch-acp-delivery.ts`, `reply-dispatcher.ts`, `feishu/reply-dispatcher.ts` | | Gateway/config | `feishu/channel.ts`, `feishu/config-schema.ts` | | SDK/misc | `plugin-sdk/feishu.ts`, `session-binding-service.ts`, `acpx/process.ts` | ## Context Supersedes #40936 (clean rebase, same changes). ## Test plan - [x] `startup-memory` passes - [x] Local typecheck + lint + format pass - [x] `extension-fast (acpx)` passes - [x] Manual test: `/focus` in Feishu DM binds ACP session - [x] Manual test: ACP block replies reach Feishu user 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-17T07:45:00Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR completes Feishu's `/focus` and `/unfocus` integration by adding conversation-ID resolution, channel-surface detection, thread-binding lifecycle management in the gateway, and an ACP block-promotion mechanism (`promoteAcpBlocksToFinals`) to compensate for Feishu's suppression of block streaming. It also migrates the session-binding-adapter registry to `globalThis` to survive module-loading boundary splits, and strips the `CLAUDECODE` env var from spawned ACP child processes. - The new `resolveFeishuConversationId` logic correctly rejects bare group chats, strips known provider/type prefixes, and composes `chatId:topic:threadId` for topic threads. - The `promoteAcpBlocksToFinals` dispatcher flag is a clean opt-in; Discord's native block handling is unchanged. - `state.blockCount` is incremented even when a block payload is promoted to a final reply, which may mislead callers of `getBlockCount()` on Feishu sessions. - The `target` argument added to `/unfocus` is defensively useful but its `"Ignored"` description may surface confusingly in slash-command UIs. - The `resolveFeishuConversationId` function silently returns `undefined` for unknown `ChatType` / unrecognised ID prefixes with no diagnostic logging, which could complicate future debugging.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- This PR is safe to merge; no critical runtime bugs were found, and the edge-case concerns are low-risk. - The implementation follows existing patterns (Telegram parity), `createFeishuThreadBindingManager` is idempotent on restart, and the `globalThis` adapter registry fix is correct. The `blockCount` vs. promoted-final discrepancy is a minor observability gap rather than a delivery bug, and the silent rejection in `resolveFeishuConversationId` for unknown types errs on the safe side. - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` — verify that no caller uses `getBlockCount()` to gate block-specific post-processing for Feishu sessions.

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `src/auto-reply/commands-registry.data.ts`, line 387-396 ([link](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/8b3a8e0e2017417479e556eab2263d7b15f07522/src/auto-reply/commands-registry.data.ts#L387-L396)) **Ignored argument description is misleading** The `target` arg on `/unfocus` is labelled `"Ignored (unfocus always applies to the current conversation)"`. While this is useful defensively (prevents the command parser from rejecting `/unfocus `), surfacing it as a documented argument in the command registry may confuse users or bot-UI clients that render arg descriptions as hints. Consider using a hidden/suppressed flag (if the registry supports it), or replacing the description with something less user-facing, like `"(internal, not used)"`, so it doesn't appear as a visible hint in slash command UIs.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/auto-reply/commands-registry.data.ts Line: 387-396 Comment: **Ignored argument description is misleading** The `target` arg on `/unfocus` is labelled `"Ignored (unfocus always applies to the current conversation)"`. While this is useful defensively (prevents the command parser from rejecting `/unfocus `), surfacing it as a documented argument in the command registry may confuse users or bot-UI clients that render arg descriptions as hints. Consider using a hidden/suppressed flag (if the registry supports it), or replacing the description with something less user-facing, like `"(internal, not used)"`, so it doesn't appear as a visible hint in slash command UIs. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts Line: 183-190 Comment: **`blockCount` incremented even when promoted to final** When `promoteAcpBlocksToFinals` is set, blocks are delivered via `sendFinalReply`, but `state.blockCount` (and `state.accumulatedBlockText`) are still incremented at lines 122–128 because that tracking happens unconditionally before the routing/dispatch decision. Callers relying on `getBlockCount() === 0` to confirm no block payloads were delivered will get misleading results for Feishu sessions where every block was actually delivered as a final message. If downstream logic uses `getBlockCount()` to decide whether to apply any block-specific post-processing, this discrepancy could lead to incorrect behavior. Consider either skipping the `blockCount` increment when `promoteAcpBlocksToFinals` is true, or documenting clearly that `blockCount` tracks attempted block deliveries regardless of the actual dispatch path. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/auto-reply/commands-registry.data.ts Line: 387-396 Comment: **Ignored argument description is misleading** The `target` arg on `/unfocus` is labelled `"Ignored (unfocus always applies to the current conversation)"`. While this is useful defensively (prevents the command parser from rejecting `/unfocus `), surfacing it as a documented argument in the command registry may confuse users or bot-UI clients that render arg descriptions as hints. Consider using a hidden/suppressed flag (if the registry supports it), or replacing the description with something less user-facing, like `"(internal, not used)"`, so it doesn't appear as a visible hint in slash command UIs. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/auto-reply/reply/feishu-context.ts Line: 67-75 Comment: **Silent rejection for unknown ChatType with unrecognized ID prefix** When `ChatType` is not `"direct"`, `"p2p"`, `"private"`, `"group"`, and the `chatId` doesn't start with `ou_` or `oc_`, the function silently returns `undefined`. This means that any future Feishu chat type (e.g. `"supergroup"`, `"channel"`, or a partner-tenant type) will quietly fail to resolve, producing a generic `/focus` error with no indication that the chat type is the problem. Consider logging a verbose/debug note when this fallback fires so operators can diagnose unexpected rejections without having to instrument the code themselves: ```ts // For unrecognised ChatType, only allow known DM identifiers (ou_ prefix). if (chatId.toLowerCase().startsWith("ou_")) { return chatId; } // Unrecognised combination — log to aid future debugging. logVerbose(`resolveFeishuConversationId: rejecting unknown chatType=${chatType} / chatId prefix`); return undefined; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

input.title: fix: CompletionDeliveryGate to prevent duplicate ACP completion delivery
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #65242: fix: CompletionDeliveryGate to prevent duplicate ACP completion delivery
input.github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 65242 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/65242 - Title: fix: CompletionDeliveryGate to prevent duplicate ACP completion delivery - State: OPEN - Author: richardclawbot - Labels: channel: discord, agents, size: L, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, mantis: telegram-visible-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Summary - Introduces a `CompletionDeliveryGate` module that uses first-writer-wins compare-and-swap to ensure exactly one delivery path handles each ACP/subagent completion event - Integrates gate checks into the three competing delivery paths: task registry banner, subagent announce flow, and farewell hook emission - Promotes `shouldSilentWakeAcpChildSession` from dist-bundle patch to source, giving ACP child completions a clean heartbeat-wake path - Feature-flagged via `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_GATE` env var (off/shadow/on) for safe incremental rollout ## Problem When an ACP child session completes, up to 5 independent code paths can each independently attempt to notify the user with no shared coordination point: 1. **Task registry banner** (`maybeDeliverTaskTerminalUpdate`) — formats and sends "Background task done: ..." directly 2. **Subagent announce flow** (`runSubagentAnnounceFlow`) — reads child output, synthesizes assistant-voice reply 3. **Farewell hook** (`emitSubagentEndedHookOnce`) — fires `subagent_ended` hook with `sendFarewell: true` 4. **System event queue** — queues raw banner text as prompt prefix 5. **Silent wake** — heartbeat-only parent wake (correct path, but doesn't always win the race) This caused: duplicate "Background task done" messages, raw internal event text in Telegram/Discord, stale completion replays, and messages appearing as if authored by the user. ## Solution A new `CompletionDeliveryGate` serves as the single coordination point. All delivery paths call `claimCompletionDelivery(key, source, mode)` before producing user-visible output. The first caller wins; subsequent callers for the same `runtime:runId:ownerSessionKey` key are blocked (or shadow-logged). ### Files changed | File | Change | |------|--------| | `src/tasks/completion-delivery-gate.ts` | **New** — gate module with claim/check/resolve API | | `src/tasks/completion-delivery-gate.test.ts` | **New** — 14 unit tests covering all gate modes | | `src/tasks/task-registry.ts` | Gate checks before silent wake and visible banner paths | | `src/tasks/task-executor-policy.ts` | `shouldSilentWakeAcpChildSession` promoted from dist patch | | `src/agents/subagent-announce.ts` | Gate check at announce flow entry | | `src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.ts` | Gate check suppresses farewell when already claimed | | `src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts` | Tests for `shouldSilentWakeAcpChildSession` | | `src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts` | Tests for silent wake integration in task registry | ## Rollout strategy 1. **Phase 1** — Deploy with `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_GATE=0` (default, transparent — all paths work as today) 2. **Phase 2** — `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_GATE=shadow` (logs would-be blocks to stderr, doesn't enforce) 3. **Phase 3** — `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_GATE=1` (enforces single-owner delivery) 4. **Phase 4** — Remove dist-bundle patches and prompt-level rules once stable ## Test plan - [x] 14 unit tests for CompletionDeliveryGate (transparent/shadow/enforce modes, idempotency, independence) - [x] 6 tests for `shouldSilentWakeAcpChildSession` predicate - [x] 45 existing task-registry tests pass with gate integration - [x] 6 existing subagent-announce tests pass with gate integration - [x] `pnpm tsgo` — clean typecheck - [x] `pnpm check` — clean lint - [ ] Manual: enable shadow mode on staging, verify no false blocks - [ ] Manual: enable enforce mode, verify single delivery per completion on Telegram 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-12T08:02:37Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR introduces a `CompletionDeliveryGate` to coordinate the five competing ACP child-session completion delivery paths (task registry banner, announce flow, farewell hook, system event queue, silent wake) using first-writer-wins compare-and-swap. The gate is off by default and supports shadow and enforce modes for safe rollout. - **P1**: The `CompletionKey` includes a `runtime` field, but the three delivery paths disagree on its value for ACP completions: the task registry correctly derives it from `task.runtime` (`\"acp\"`), while the announce flow (`subagent-announce.ts:233`) and farewell hook (`subagent-registry-completion.ts:79`) both hardcode `\"subagent\"`. This means `buildCanonicalKey` produces different strings for the same completion event (`completion:acp:…` vs `completion:subagent:…`), and the gate never detects a conflict between these paths — exactly the race the PR is intended to fix.

Confidence Score: 3/5

The gate defaults to off so existing behaviour is unchanged, but the P1 runtime-key mismatch means the deduplication mechanism is broken for ACP completions (the primary use case) when the gate is promoted to enforce mode. Two P1 findings (announce flow and farewell hook both use runtime 'subagent' while the task registry uses runtime 'acp') mean the gate provides zero cross-path protection for ACP completions when enabled. Since the rollout is gated behind an env flag this is not immediately breaking, but the bug defeats the purpose of the PR and needs to be resolved before promotion to shadow or enforce mode. src/agents/subagent-announce.ts (line 233 — hardcoded runtime) and src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.ts (line 79 — hardcoded runtime)
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/agents/subagent-announce.ts Line: 231-238 Comment: **Runtime mismatch breaks cross-path deduplication for ACP completions** The announce flow always sets `runtime: "subagent"` (or `"cron"`) in the CompletionKey, but the task registry builds its key from `resolveCompletionKeyFromTask(task)` which uses `task.runtime` — which is `"acp"` for ACP child session tasks. Because `buildCanonicalKey` includes the runtime, the resulting canonical keys are different strings (`completion:acp::` vs `completion:subagent::`), so the gate never sees them as the same completion event. When the gate is enabled, both the silent wake and the announce flow will succeed independently, reproducing the exact duplicate delivery the PR is designed to prevent. The same mismatch applies in `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` in `subagent-registry-completion.ts`, where `isCompletionClaimed` is called with `runtime: "subagent"` — it will never find a claim that was written by the task registry under `"acp"`. To fix: either drop the `runtime` dimension from the key (since `runId + ownerSessionKey` already identifies the event), or pass the actual task runtime into the announce flow so all paths agree on the canonical key. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.ts Line: 77-85 Comment: **Hardcoded `"subagent"` runtime never matches ACP task registry claims** `isCompletionClaimed` is called with `runtime: "subagent"` regardless of whether the underlying run was an ACP task. The task registry writes its claims with the task's actual runtime (`"acp"` for ACP child sessions). Since the canonical key differs (`completion:subagent:…` vs `completion:acp:…`), `claimed` will be `false` for any ACP completion already gated by the task registry, and the farewell hook will still fire with the original `sendFarewell` value — defeating the suppression logic entirely for ACP runs. This is the same root cause as the runtime mismatch in `subagent-announce.ts`. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/tasks/completion-delivery-gate.ts Line: 161-163 Comment: **`getCompletionClaim` silently returns `undefined` in transparent mode** In gate mode `"off"`, `claimCompletionDelivery` never writes to the `claims` map, so this function always returns `undefined` regardless of how many claims were "made". Any future caller who uses `getCompletionClaim` to check delivery status after enabling the gate may get stale results if they don't also consult `isCompletionClaimed` (which does gate-mode-guard). Adding a `resolveGateMode() === "off"` guard here, matching the pattern in `isCompletionClaimed`, would make the behaviour consistent and prevent surprises when the gate transitions from off to shadow/on. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

- State: OPEN
- Author: zhangguiping-xydt
- Labels: size: XS, proof: supplied, mantis: telegram-visible-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary, status: 📣 needs proof

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

- Problem: Active memory plugin crashes on Telegram forum (topic-based) group sessions because the session peer ID (e.g. `-1003803644436:topic:35526`) contains colons that fail `normalizeBundledPluginDirName` validation when the value flows through `resolveAgentHookChannelId` → `buildAgentHookContextChannelFields` → channel contract API loading.
- Solution: Sanitize colons to dashes in `normalizeBundledPluginDirName` instead of throwing, so the directory lookup fails gracefully (no matching directory) rather than crashing.
- What changed: `src/plugins/public-surface-runtime.ts` (colon sanitization in `normalizeBundledPluginDirName`), `src/plugins/public-surface-runtime.test.ts` (updated test + regression test).
- What did NOT change: All other path-traversal guards (`/`, `\`, `..`, empty) remain strict. No changes to session key parsing, hook context resolution, or channel contract API logic.

Fixes #84789

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Active memory crashes with "Bundled plugin dirName must be a single directory: -1003803644436:topic:35526" on Telegram forum topic sessions
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node 24.x, OpenClaw worktree SHA 45cd1bc, shallow clone
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  pnpm install
  npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts src/plugins/public-surface-runtime.test.ts
  node --import tsx -e "import { normalizeBundledPluginDirName } from './src/plugins/public-surface-runtime.ts'; console.log(normalizeBundledPluginDirName('-1003803644436:topic:35526'));"
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  $ node --import tsx -e "import { normalizeBundledPluginDirName } from './src/plugins/public-surface-runtime.ts'; console.log(normalizeBundledPluginDirName('-1003803644436:topic:35526'));"
  -1003803644436-topic-35526

  $ npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts src/plugins/public-surface-runtime.test.ts
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  8 passed (8)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** Colons in dirName are replaced with dashes. Path traversal (`../`), slashes, and backslashes are still rejected. Regular plugin names (`telegram`, `active-memory`) are unchanged.
- **What was not tested:** Did not start a live OpenClaw instance with a real Telegram bot; only exercised the patched function locally via `node --import tsx` and ran the unit test suite.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `src/plugins/public-surface-runtime.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: `normalizeBundledPluginDirName` sanitizes colon-containing Telegram forum topic peer IDs (e.g. `-1003803644436:topic:35526` → `-1003803644436-topic-35526`) instead of throwing
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The test directly exercises the exact function that was crashing, with the exact input pattern from the issue report.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `resolveAgentHookChannelId` (hook-agent-context.ts) returns `parseRawSessionConversationRef(sessionKey).rawId` which for Telegram forum topics is `-1003803644436:topic:35526`. This flows as `channelId` through `buildAgentHookContextChannelFields` into channel contract API functions that pass it as `dirName` to `loadBundledPluginPublicArtifactModuleSync`, which calls `normalizeBundledPluginDirName` — and colons triggered a hard throw.
- Missing detection / guardrail: `normalizeBundledPluginDirName` rejected colons outright (to prevent Windows drive-letter / ADS paths) instead of sanitizing them, which is overly strict for scoped conversation IDs that happen to reach this function.
```

**Risk before merge**

**Maintainer options:**
1. **Keep Loader Validation Strict (recommended)**  
   Remove the colon-to-dash normalization and repair the caller so invalid conversation IDs are either converted to the owning channel/provider id or handled as a missing channel artifact.
2. **Require Maintainer Boundary Decision**  
   Pause this PR if maintainers intentionally want scoped IDs normalized at the loader boundary, because that needs an explicit collision and security contract.

**Next step before merge**
This needs contributor or maintainer handling because the code change weakens a shared loader boundary and the PR still needs real Telegram behavior proof.

**Security**
Needs attention: The diff weakens a plugin public-artifact path boundary by translating invalid dirName input into another directory name before lookup.

**Review findings**
- [P1] Keep dirName validation strict — `src/plugins/public-surface-runtime.ts:46`

<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Keep `normalizeBundledPluginDirName` strict and fix the specific caller path so scoped conversation IDs never become plugin directory selectors, with regression coverage around the active-memory/channel-contract flow and redacted live Telegram topic proof.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Partially yes: source inspection confirms raw topic IDs still contain colons and the current loader rejects them if they reach dirName normalization. I did not establish a full current-main active-memory crash path because current active-memory already has a prior guard for this class of scoped channel IDs.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

No: replacing colons inside the shared loader normalizer is broader than the bug and can alias invalid IDs to real bundled plugin directories. The safer fix is at the caller/channel-contract boundary that is passing conversation IDs as plugin directory names.

- State: CLOSED
- Author: nxmxbbd
- Labels: docs, gateway, commands, docker, size: M, clawsweeper:automerge, proof: supplied, proof: sufficient, P2, rating: 🐚 platinum hermit, status: 👀 ready for maintainer look

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

- Problem: sandboxed agents could hide configured MCP server tools even though the MCP servers loaded successfully, and there was no clear `openclaw doctor` warning for the missing sandbox tool allow gate.
- Solution: add a doctor warning when `mcp.servers` are configured but active sandbox tool policy does not allow `bundle-mcp`, `group:plugins`, or a matching server glob such as `outlook__*`.
- What changed: mirror runtime sandbox policy fallback for per-agent partial overrides, preserve warnings when a separate agent intentionally denies MCP, polish warning grammar/source labels, and document sanitized server globs plus the current bundled-plugin/Claude `.mcp.json` diagnostic limitation.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no sandbox defaults, provider serialization, MCP runtime behavior, runtime tool policy enforcement, or bundled-plugin/`.mcp.json` MCP discovery logic changed.

## Motivation

- Related to #80909: the reporter confirmed adding `"bundle-mcp"` to `tools.sandbox.tools.alsoAllow` made the real MCP tools visible again in sandboxed turns.
- The failure mode is confusing because the MCP server can load successfully while the sandbox tool policy filters the provider-visible tool list before the request.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations

Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/80909-sandbox-mcp-docs`, rebased onto current `upstream/main`, using a temp state dir at `/tmp/openclaw-80909-doctor-state` and running the real `pnpm openclaw doctor` CLI.

```bash
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-80909-doctor-state pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive --no-workspace-suggestions
```

- Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture from this branch, copied live output:

```text
◇  Doctor warnings ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮

│    Sandboxed agents will filter bundled MCP tools before provider       │
│    requests. Add "bundle-mcp" to tools.sandbox.tools.alsoAllow (or use  │
│    "group:plugins" / server globs) if those MCP tools should be         │
│    visible; use tools.sandbox.tools.allow: [] only when you             │
│    intentionally want no sandbox allow gate.                            │
│                                                                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

The command exited `0`. The run also printed unrelated local temp-state doctor findings such as missing UI assets, gateway auth/connect noise, and existing build warnings from bundled zod locale `.d.cts` files.

`openclaw doctor` prints the targeted sandbox/MCP warning in the Doctor warnings panel instead of silently allowing the misconfiguration to pass.

An end-to-end sandboxed provider turn against a live MCP server was not rerun in this checkout; the reporter's real setup already confirmed `"bundle-mcp"` fixes the missing provider-visible MCP tools. This PR also intentionally does not make doctor enumerate MCP servers loaded from bundled plugin manifests or Claude `.mcp.json`; the docs call that limitation out.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: sandbox tool policy is a second allow gate for sandboxed sessions. Configured MCP servers can load under `bundle-mcp`, but if `tools.sandbox.tools` lacks `bundle-mcp`, `group:plugins`, or a matching `<server>__*` pattern, those MCP tools are filtered before provider requests.
- Missing detection / guardrail: `openclaw doctor` did not flag the configuration shape, so users could diagnose it as a provider serialization or MCP loading bug.
- Contributing context: per-agent sandbox tool overrides fall back field-by-field at runtime, so the diagnostic must mirror that behavior to avoid false positives.

`openclaw doctor` can now warn when sandbox policy is likely hiding configured MCP server tools from sandboxed agents. Docs now clarify which sandbox allow entries expose MCP/plugin tools, how sanitized server globs are formed, and that bundled-plugin/Claude `.mcp.json` MCP sources use the same gate but are not enumerated by this diagnostic yet.

After:

Body:
```markdown
# MCP server tools never reach outbound `tools[]` across 4.26 → 5.7 (cluster previously closed + locked as "resolved")

## Summary

Configured `mcp.servers` entries do not appear in the agent's outbound LLM request body's `tools[]` array, on every stable release tested from **2026.4.26 through 2026.5.7** (latest at filing). The MCP servers themselves are healthy — they spawn, register, and respond to direct stdio `tools/list` and `tools/call` cleanly with real results. Only the path from "openclaw materializes the catalog" to "OpenAI-format chat-completions request body" fails.

This issue exists because the predecessor cluster (#74387, #74726, #74844) was closed three times — twice as "already implemented" by automated review, then **locked as `resolved`** — despite wire-level evidence to the contrary posted by two independent users including at least one additional environment beyond a single-machine repro.

## Why a fresh issue

All three predecessor issues are now `closed + locked` with `lock_reason: "resolved"`:

- #74387 — "MCP tools not accessible to main agent — only to embedded Pi/coding sessions" (closed/locked 2026-04-29)
- #74726 — "MCP tools not accessible to main agent despite bundle-mcp configuration" (closed/locked 2026-04-30)
- #74844 — "MCP server tools missing from agent's request body in 2026.4.26" (closed/locked 2026-04-30)

Each was auto-closed by `clawsweeper` after static-analysis review citing the same code paths that ship with the bug intact. Locking eliminated the only feedback channel into the cluster. The bug is still present; opening a fresh canonical issue is the only available path.

## Environment

- **OpenClaw:** `2026.5.7 (eeef486)`
- **Node.js:** `v22.22.2`
- **macOS:** `26.4.1`
- **Install method:** `npm i -g openclaw@2026.5.7`
- **Agent runtime:** embedded (Pi)
- **MCP servers configured:** two stdio servers, both verified healthy
- **`tools.profile`:** `coding`
- **`plugins.allow`:** contains explicit plugin ids; allow list verified loaded via `openclaw plugins list`

## Reproduction

1. Configure two stdio MCP servers under `mcp.servers`.
2. Verify each starts and responds:
   ```bash
   echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize",...}' | <command from mcp.servers.<id>.command>
   ```
   Returns full `tools/list` (50+ tools across the two servers in our case).
3. `openclaw mcp list` confirms both servers configured.
4. `openclaw plugins inspect <pluginId>` shows `Status: loaded` for each configured MCP server.
5. Start the gateway and trigger one agent turn (any normal channel — iMessage/BlueBubbles in our case).
6. Capture the outbound `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions` request body via the built-in debug proxy (`OPENCLAW_DEBUG_PROXY_ENABLED=1 + OPENCLAW_DEBUG_PROXY_URL=...`).
7. Inspect `body.tools[]` — **no `server__*` entries**, only bundled openclaw tools (`read, write, exec, sessions_*, web_search, web_fetch`, etc.).

## Longitudinal data (7 stable releases)

Each row is an independently verified test run, configuration unchanged, MCP servers verified healthy each time:

| Version | Tested | `tools[]` contains `server__*` | Agent behavior |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2026.4.26 | yes | ❌ | "I don't have access to outlook tools" (honest failure) |
| 2026.4.27 (cited as "fixed", cbc2ba0) | yes | ❌ | Silent confabulation — fabricates plausible-sounding email lists with no overlap to reality |
| 2026.5.2 (cited as "fixed", 8b2a6e5) | yes | ❌ | Silent confabulation |
| 2026.5.7 (current, eeef486) | yes | ❌ | Reverts to honest admission — tries shell exec workaround, hits "openclaw: not found" |

Cost-tracker confirms **one LLM call per agent turn** in every test (no tool round-trip occurred). Input token count stable at ~48–72k across versions — consistent with no MCP tool definitions in the prompt (which would add ~20–30k for the configured server count).

## Wire evidence

The captured outbound request body from 2026.4.26 (redacted, `messages[]` stripped) is inlined in #74844 (it's locked but still readable). The `tools[]` array contains exactly 15 entries — all bundled OpenClaw tools. Zero `server__*` entries.

I can re-capture on 5.7 if it would help maintainers — we observed event-loop stalls with the debug proxy attached on some recent releases, so we've been using cost-tracker telemetry as the cleaner signal in subsequent tests. Same evidence shape either way.

## What works at every layer

Every layer between config and provider serialization passes its own test:

- `mcp.servers` populated, valid JSON, `dmPolicy` and credential resolution working
- `openclaw mcp list` lists both servers
- MCP server child processes spawn (visible in `ps aux`)
- Direct stdio probe of each server returns full `tools/list` (verified each test)
- Direct stdio probe of `tools/call outlook_list_inbox` returns real inbox data (verified each test)
- `openclaw plugins inspect <id>` reports `Status: loaded` for every configured MCP plugin
- Built-in OpenClaw tools (`read/write/exec/sessions_*/web_search/web_fetch`) DO appear in outbound `tools[]` — so the serializer works, just not for MCP tools
- Gateway log shows `BlueBubbles` (or whichever channel) catchup, webhook, and inbound delivery all working

The failure is silent: no warning in `gateway.log`, no error in `gateway.err.log`, `openclaw doctor` reports the system clean.

## Code paths cited by previous closures

The `clawsweeper` static review of each predecessor cited these as the implementation:

- `src/agents/bundle-mcp-config.ts:49` — `loadMergedBundleMcpConfig`
- `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts:457` — `runEmbeddedPiAgent`
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:908` — embedded attempt materializing bundle-MCP tools and appending to `effectiveTools`
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:1358` — converting `effectiveTools` into Pi `customTools` and session allowlist
- `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts:64` — `materializeBundleMcpToolsForRun`
- `src/agents/tool-catalog.ts:316` — `coding`/`messaging` profile includes `bundle-mcp`

These code paths likely exist and run, but something between materialization and the final OpenAI-format serialization drops the MCP tools. The cost-tracker single-LLM-call-per-turn pattern across 7 versions rules out "tool was attempted but execution failed."

## Things I have ruled out (tested each)

- `tools.profile` — tried `coding`, `messaging`, `full`. Same result on all.
- `tools.alsoAllow: ["outlook__*","gmail__*"]` at root and under `tools.sandbox.tools`. No effect.
- `plugins.allow` containing `outlook`, `gmail`, `mcp` — flagged as stale-not-a-plugin and ignored.
- `mcp.sessionIdleTtlMs: 0` (prevent idle eviction). MCP processes stay resident; tools still missing.
- Multiple gateway restarts.
- `openclaw doctor`, `openclaw doctor --fix`. No MCP-related findings.
- Bundled vs external plugin: the BlueBubbles plugin moved from bundled to npm in 5.x; reinstalling and reconnecting did not affect MCP tool exposure.

## Ask

@steipete @vincentkoc — `clawsweeper` named both of you as the owners of the embedded Pi MCP exposure path across the closure comments on #74387 and #74844. This bug has now persisted through **seven stable releases** with **two automated "already implemented" closures** and a **lock as "resolved"** despite consistent wire-level falsifying evidence from at least two independent users (myself and @luuq2026).

A human pair of eyes on this would be valuable. Concrete options:

1. **Quick check:** add an integration test that asserts `body.tools[]` includes configured `server__*` entries when `mcp.servers` is populated. If such a test already exists and is passing, the test does not exercise the same code path users hit.
2. **Diagnostic ask:** what specific config shape or environment is the existing integration coverage exercising? My setup uses iMessage/BlueBubbles inbound, Gemini 2.5 Pro via OpenRouter, `tools.profile: coding`, two stdio MCP servers. If yours uses something different, that's the clue.
3. **Reproduce:** I'm happy to capture a fresh debug-proxy wire capture on 5.7, or any other artifact you'd like. Just say what's most useful.

The three locked predecessors should ideally be unlocked and linked back to this issue once a human triage decision is made — but that's an optional cleanup.

```

Acceptance criteria:

- Capture a current-main OpenAI-compatible provider request body for a run with `mcp.servers` and `tools.profile: coding`, with Tool Search/Code Mode config recorded.
- Add or update an integration that asserts the actual provider-bound `body.tools[]` or equivalent Tool Search catalog behavior for a real stdio MCP server.
- Add an equivalent minimal native `api.registerTool()` plugin fixture to prove arbitrary plugin tools reach the same provider boundary.

- **Documented MCP expectation:** The MCP docs state that embedded Pi exposes configured MCP tools in normal `coding` and `messaging` profiles, with `minimal` and `tools.deny: ["bundle-mcp"]` hiding them. Public docs: [`docs/cli/mcp.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli/mcp). ([`docs/cli/mcp.md:363`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli/mcp), [639107b7db55](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/639107b7db55))

([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:1299`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/639107b7db55/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts#L1299), [639107b7db55](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/639107b7db55))

([`src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-tools.request-boundary.test.ts:11`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/639107b7db55/src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-tools.request-boundary.test.ts#L11), [639107b7db55](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/639107b7db55))
- **Docker E2E scope:** The packaged Docker smoke starts a real stdio MCP probe and verifies materialization, execution, policy filtering, and `customTools`; it still does not assert the final outbound provider `body.tools[]`. ([`scripts/e2e/pi-bundle-mcp-tools-docker-client.ts:140`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/639107b7db55/scripts/e2e/pi-bundle-mcp-tools-docker-client.ts#L140), [639107b7db55](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/639107b7db55))

input.title: fix(mcp): auto-reinitialize streamable-http session on -32000 Server not initialized
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #71545: fix(mcp): auto-reinitialize streamable-http session on -32000 Server not initialized
input.github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: github_pr - Number: 71545 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/71545 - Title: fix(mcp): auto-reinitialize streamable-http session on -32000 Server not initialized - State: OPEN - Author: Vianne-droid - Labels: agents, size: M, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 availability, status: 📣 needs proof Body: ```markdown ## Problem Streamable-HTTP MCP servers maintain stateful sessions via `Mcp-Session-Id`. Sessions can be lost due to server redeploys, container restarts, idle TTL expiry, or network blips. The MCP SDK client does not auto-reinitialize — subsequent `callTool` calls fail permanently with: ``` Streamable HTTP error: Error POSTing to endpoint: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","error":{"code":-32000,"message":"Bad Request: Server not initialized"},"id":null} ``` The only workaround today is a full gateway restart (`openclaw gateway restart`), which interrupts the active session. ## Fix Wrap `callTool` in `createSessionMcpRuntime` with transparent reconnect-and-retry logic: 1. On any `callTool` error, detect if it looks like a lost streamable-http session (`-32000 / Server not initialized`, `Session not found`, HTTP 400/404). 2. If so: dispose dead client+transport → build fresh via existing `resolveMcpTransport` → reconnect via `connectWithTimeout` → retry the original call once. 3. If reconnect itself fails, re-throw the original error unchanged. 4. SSE and stdio transports are completely unaffected (guard checks `session.transportType === "streamable-http"`). ## Repro 1. Configure a stateful streamable-http MCP server (e.g. any server using `sessionIdGenerator: () => randomUUID()`) 2. Call any tool successfully 3. Restart/redeploy the MCP server (drops sessions) 4. Call any tool again → **before:** `Server not initialized`, gateway restart required; **after:** transparent reconnect, call succeeds ## Notes - No changes to SSE or stdio paths - The retry is exactly once — if the server is genuinely down, the reconnect fails and the original error propagates normally - Tested against a live Railway-hosted VianneFlow MCP server (stateful streamable-http) ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-25T11:05:27Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR wraps `callTool` in `createSessionMcpRuntime` with a reconnect-and-retry path that triggers when a streamable-HTTP session is detected as lost (JSON-RPC `-32000`, `Session not found`, etc.), transparently rebuilding the transport and retrying once. The approach is well-scoped (SSE/stdio paths are untouched) but has one definite regression: the reconnected `Client` is constructed without the `jsonSchemaValidator: createBundleMcpJsonSchemaValidator()` option that the catalog-time client carries, causing Draft 2020-12 input schemas to fall back to the SDK's default (Draft-07) validator after a reconnect.

Confidence Score: 3/5

Not safe to merge without fixing the missing jsonSchemaValidator in the reconnected client, which is a silent regression for Draft 2020-12 schemas. One clear P1 defect (reconnected client drops the custom schema validator), one P2 race condition (concurrent reconnects can tear down each other's sessions), and one P2 false-positive risk (HTTP 4xx regex firing on tool-level errors). The P1 pushes the ceiling to 4; the additional P2s and the fact that this touches a core runtime path bring the score to 3. src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts — specifically the reconnectStreamableSession function and the isStreamableSessionLost helper.
Prompt To Fix All With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts Line: 390-393 Comment: **Reconnected client missing custom JSON schema validator** The reconnected `Client` is constructed with `{}` (empty options), omitting the `jsonSchemaValidator: createBundleMcpJsonSchemaValidator()` option present in the original catalog-time client. After a reconnect, any tool whose input schema uses JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 will be validated by the SDK's default (Draft-07) validator instead of the custom Ajv2020 validator, causing incorrect validation rejections or missed errors for those schemas. ```suggestion const client = new Client( { name: "openclaw-bundle-mcp", version: "0.0.0" }, { jsonSchemaValidator: createBundleMcpJsonSchemaValidator() }, ); ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts Line: 381-423 Comment: **No guard against concurrent reconnect races** If two concurrent `callTool` calls both fail with a session-lost error at the same time, both will independently enter `reconnectStreamableSession`. The first call disposes the old session and establishes a fresh one; when the second call then runs, it sees the new session in `sessions`, disposes it, and attempts another reconnect — leaving the first call's successful retry operating against a transport that has now been torn down. Consider a simple in-flight reconnect promise guard (e.g., store a `reconnectInFlight: Promise | null` on the runtime and share it) so parallel losers await the single reconnect instead of each launching their own. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. --- This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts Line: 374-379 Comment: **HTTP 4xx regex may false-positive on tool-level error payloads** `/HTTP\s*4(?:00|04)/i.test(msg)` will match any error message that contains the literal text "HTTP 400" or "HTTP 404" — including error *responses returned by tools themselves* (e.g., a tool that calls a downstream API and surfaces "HTTP 400: validation error" in its error message). This would trigger a spurious session teardown and reconnect for a tool-level application error, not a transport-level session loss. Tightening the match (e.g., anchoring to the transport error format, or removing this heuristic in favour of the more-specific string checks already present) would reduce false positives. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

input.title: Benchmark spawner scripts use bare "node" instead of process.execPath, risking version mismatch
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #83921: Benchmark spawner scripts use bare "node" instead of process.execPath, risking version mismatch
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 83921
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/83921
- Title: Benchmark spawner scripts use bare "node" instead of process.execPath, risking version mismatch
- State: CLOSED
- Author: davinci282828
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:current-main-repro, issue-rating: 🦀 challenger crab

Body:
```markdown
**Severity:** medium / **Confidence:** high / **Category:** bug
**Triage:** confirmed-bug
**Detected against:** openclaw v2026.5.18 (latest stable at time of scan, 2026-05-18)
**Tooling:** clawpatch 0.3.0 + acpx/claude-sonnet-4-5 via [Brad Mills protocol](https://x.com/bradmillscan/status/2056377217437909178)

## Evidence

- `scripts/test-cli-startup-bench-budget.mjs:88-97` (resolveCurrentReportPath)

```
const run = spawnSync("node", args, {
    cwd: process.cwd(),
    stdio: "inherit",
    env: process.env,
  });
```

- `scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs:46-52` (None)

```
const run = spawnSync("node", args, {
  cwd: process.cwd(),
  stdio: "inherit",
  env: process.env,
});
```

## Reasoning
Both scripts call spawnSync("node", ...) which resolves "node" through PATH. In environments using version managers (nvm, fnm, volta, asdf), the PATH-resolved "node" may differ from the Node.js instance currently executing the script (process.execPath). This means the bench runner and the fixture updater could execute bench-cli-startup.ts on a different Node version than intended, producing measurements that are not comparable to the baseline or to what CI uses. bench-cli-startup.ts itself correctly uses process.execPath when spawning the CLI under test, making the inconsistency more subtle.

## Reproduction
Use nvm to set node 20 as default, then run the scripts while nvm shell activation points to node 22 — the spawned bench will run on node 20 while the parent runs on node 22.

## Recommendation
Replace `spawnSync("node", args, ...)` with `spawnSync(process.execPath, args, ...)` in both scripts, matching the pattern used in bench-cli-startup.ts and check-cli-startup-memory.mjs.

## Why existing tests miss this
No tests exist for these scripts. The version mismatch only becomes visible in polyglot Node environments.

## Minimum fix scope
Change the first argument of spawnSync from "node" to process.execPath in scripts/test-cli-startup-bench-budget.mjs (resolveCurrentReportPath) and scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs.

---
_Standardized clawpatch finding. Persistent in v2026.5.18 (not resolved by upgrading from v2026.5.12). Finding ID: `fnd_sig-feat-cli-command-23edfe2554-_2dbd4973be`._
```

```markdown
- clawsweeper at 2026-05-19T02:33:01Z:

**Workflow note:** Future ClawSweeper reviews update this same comment in place.

<details>
<summary>How this review workflow works</summary>

- ClawSweeper keeps one durable marker-backed review comment per issue or PR.

- Maintainers can comment `@clawsweeper explain` to ask for more context, or `@clawsweeper stop` to stop active automation.

</details>

**Summary**
Keep open: the benchmark wrapper scripts still use a PATH-resolved `node` child process on current main, and a no-PATH current-main probe reproduces the same resolver defect without needing a full version-manager setup.

Reproducibility: yes. A current-main run of the updater with an absolute parent Node and `PATH=/nonexistent` exits 1 because the wrapper tries to resolve bare `node` from PATH.

**Next step**
Safe repair candidate: the fix is limited to two benchmark wrapper spawns plus focused validation, with no product or config decision required.


<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Change the two wrapper spawns to `process.execPath` and add a narrow regression check proving the wrappers keep working when the parent Node is absolute but `node` is unavailable or different on PATH.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes. A current-main run of the updater with an absolute parent Node and `PATH=/nonexistent` exits 1 because the wrapper tries to resolve bare `node` from PATH.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

Yes. Replacing the two child-process command strings with `process.execPath` is the narrow maintainable fix and matches the existing benchmark harness behavior.

Label justifications:

- `P2`: This is a real tooling correctness bug with limited blast radius to benchmark/update scripts rather than a core runtime outage.

Acceptance criteria:

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/cli-startup-bench-spawner.test.ts
- node_bin="$(command -v node)"; PATH=/nonexistent "$node_bin" scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs --out /tmp/openclaw-cli-startup-bench-review.json --preset startup --runs 1 --warmup 0 --timeout-ms 10000
- node_bin="$(command -v node)"; PATH=/nonexistent "$node_bin" scripts/test-cli-startup-bench-budget.mjs --preset startup --runs 1 --warmup 0 --timeout-ms 10000 --max-duration-regression-pct 100000 --max-first-output-regression-pct 100000 --max-rss-regression-pct 100000

What I checked:

- **Current budget wrapper uses bare node:** `resolveCurrentReportPath()` builds the benchmark args, then calls `spawnSync("node", args, { cwd: process.cwd(), stdio: "inherit", env: process.env })`, so the child executable is resolved through PATH rather than the parent Node executable. ([`scripts/test-cli-startup-bench-budget.mjs:108`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/e9989f3a92e5/scripts/test-cli-startup-bench-budget.mjs#L108), [e9989f3a92e5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/e9989f3a92e5))
- **Current update wrapper uses bare node:** The fixture updater has the same `spawnSync("node", args, ...)` pattern before writing the benchmark fixture. ([`scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs:67`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/e9989f3a92e5/scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs#L67), [e9989f3a92e5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/e9989f3a92e5))
- **Adjacent benchmark harness already uses the stable executable:** `bench-cli-startup.ts` spawns the CLI under test with `process.execPath`, matching the issue's proposed contract for benchmark child processes. ([`scripts/bench-cli-startup.ts:580`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/e9989f3a92e5/scripts/bench-cli-startup.ts#L580), [e9989f3a92e5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/e9989f3a92e5))
- **Dependency contract supports the report:** Node's child_process documentation says command lookup uses `options.env.PATH`, while the process documentation says `process.execPath` is the absolute executable that started the current Node process. ([nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org/api/child_process.html))

input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #83030: feat(image-generation): Add ReCraft V4.1 model family support (Standard, Utility, Vector) via OpenRouter
input.github_context:
GitHub item:

- State: OPEN
- Author: Yachiyo1680
- Labels: P3, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, impact:auth-provider

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Add ReCraft V4.1 model family support to OpenClaw's image generation tool, enabling users to access ReCraft's design-oriented models (Standard, Utility, Vector) via the existing `image_generate` tool.

## Motivation

ReCraft V4.1, released in mid-May 2026, is a new generation of image/vector generation model that differentiates itself from existing options by:

1. **Design taste** — Makes aesthetic decisions about composition, color interaction, and visual flow, producing "art-directed" images rather than generic outputs
2. **Vector generation (SVG)** — Unlike all other image models that output raster images, ReCraft V4.1 Vector can generate native SVG files — crucial for logos, icons, typography, and UI design
3. **Utility line** — Optimized for consistency and predictability, ideal for high-volume production (e-commerce, product mockups)

These models fill a gap that no current OpenClaw image generation provider covers: professional vector output and design-quality raster generation.

## OpenRouter Availability

ReCraft V4.1 models are already available on OpenRouter (which OpenClaw already supports as an image generation provider), using the following model slugs:

| Model Name | OpenRouter Slug |
|-----------|----------------|
| Recraft V4.1 | `recraft/recraft-v4.1` |
| Recraft V4.1 Pro | `recraft/recraft-v4.1-pro` |
| Recraft V4.1 Utility | `recraft/recraft-v4.1-utility` |
| Recraft V4.1 Utility Pro | `recraft/recraft-v4.1-utility-pro` |
| Recraft V4.1 Vector | `recraft/recraft-v4.1-vector` |
| Recraft V4.1 Pro Vector | `recraft/recraft-v4.1-pro-vector` |

Current OpenRouter image model list in OpenClaw only includes `google/gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview`, `google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview`, and `openai/gpt-5.4-image-2`. Adding ReCraft would significantly expand the design capabilities.

## Proposed Scope

1. Add `recraft/recraft-v4.1` (and variants) to OpenClaw's OpenRouter image provider model catalog
2. Support SVG output format for Vector models (potentially as a `format: "svg"` option or new `outputFormat` value)
3. Support the full range of ReCraft's capabilities: aspect ratios, up to ~2048px resolution (Pro models), image-to-image editing

## Additional Notes

- ReCraft also supports an independent API at `https://api.recraft.ai` — could also be considered as a standalone provider plugin
- Vector SVG output is unique to ReCraft among major AI image generators
- Pricing on OpenRouter is approximately $0.04/image
```

- State: OPEN

- Labels: enhancement, P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, impact:security, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Add a **Private Mode** toggle in Mission Control that suppresses all workspace context, allowing users to use OpenClaw for demos, content creation, and public-facing work without exposing personal data or modifying their workspace.

## Use Case

Users creating content, running demos, or sharing their screen need to use OpenClaw without:
- Leaking personal context (memory files, user info, custom persona)
- Accidentally modifying their real workspace files
- Exposing private information stored in workspace

## Proposed Behavior

When **Private Mode is ON**:
- Suppress all workspace file injection: `SOUL.md`, `USER.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `MEMORY.md`, `memory/*`, `TOOLS.md`, `HEARTBEAT.md`
- Agent acts as stock Clawd — no persona, no user context, no history
- Session gets a **temporary scratchpad** for working memory (wiped when private mode ends or session closes)
- Existing workspace files remain **untouched** — no reads, no writes

When **Private Mode is OFF**:
- Resume normal operation with full workspace context

## UI

- Toggle switch in Mission Control
- Visual indicator when active (e.g., 🔒 icon or "Private" badge in session header)

## Implementation Considerations

1. **Gateway** — Flag to suppress workspace file injection at session start
2. **Session state** — Isolated temp memory that doesn't persist to disk
3. **Mission Control UI** — Toggle component + visual indicator

---

*Feature request submitted via OpenClaw agent*
```

Comments/context:
```markdown
- yipu3 at 2026-02-14T12:55:09Z:
+1 on this. Our use case: multiple family members share the same OpenClaw instance (via Discord bots), and they want the ability to have private conversations that are guaranteed not to be persisted to memory files or session history on disk.

The current workaround of instructing the agent "don't write memory" isn't enforceable — prompt injection, bugs, or context compaction summaries could still leak information. This needs to be a program-level guarantee, not a prompt-level one.

Specifically we'd want:
- A per-session or per-channel flag like `ephemeral: true` that prevents JSONL session history from being written to disk
- Suppresses all memory file reads/writes (MEMORY.md, memory/*.md)
- Could be triggered by a Discord command like `/private` or a config option per channel

This would also address the concerns in #12242 (closed as dup of this).

- steipete at 2026-04-26T00:22:42Z:
Codex review: keeping this open for maintainer follow-up; there is still a little grit to resolve.

Keep open. Current main still lacks an enforceable per-session Private/Secret/Incognito mode: the Gateway protocol, session config, persisted session state, and Control UI row model have no private/no-persist field, while session creation still writes durable store metadata and a JSONL transcript header. Existing global bootstrap-context controls can reduce injected workspace context, but they do not provide the requested program-level no-read/no-write/no-history guarantee.

Required change / next step:

This is valid but not a safe autonomous fix-PR candidate yet because it is a privacy/security-sensitive product and architecture change requiring maintainer decisions about threat model, protocol shape, persistence boundaries, channel semantics, UI semantics, and enforcement coverage.

Best possible solution:

Keep this as the canonical Private/Secret/Incognito mode tracker and design an additive private/ephemeral session contract across Gateway protocol, SessionEntry, session store/transcripts, agent bootstrap/memory, tool/media persistence, channel triggers, and Control UI, with regression tests proving private sessions neither read private workspace/memory context nor write durable history.

- pnpm test src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
- pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/run-controls.test.ts
- pnpm check:changed in Testbox after a design-approved implementation

- **prior_maintainer_review_kept_open:** The April 26 maintainer/Codex review in the issue comments already kept this open as the canonical tracker and identified the same missing private/no-persist surfaces; the current source check still matches that conclusion.
- **gateway_protocol_lacks_private_mode:** SessionsCreateParamsSchema accepts key, agentId, label, model, parentSessionKey, task, and message; SessionsPatchParamsSchema exposes runtime/session toggles but no private, ephemeral, incognito, no-persist, or memory-suppression field. ([`src/gateway/protocol/schema/sessions.ts:85`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/2d53b49b20e1/src/gateway/protocol/schema/sessions.ts#L85), [2d53b49b20e1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/2d53b49b20e1))

([`src/config/zod-schema.session.ts:27`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/2d53b49b20e1/src/config/zod-schema.session.ts#L27), [2d53b49b20e1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/2d53b49b20e1))

(role: introduced major session management/dashboard API surface; confidence: medium; commits: [8db543d2e](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/8db543d2e), [5fa3d04ce](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/5fa3d04ce), [0e3d1c2bb](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/0e3d1c2bb); files: `src/gateway/protocol/schema/sessions.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts`, `src/gateway/sessions-history-http.ts`)

(role: adjacent bootstrap/context mitigation owner; confidence: medium; commits: [cc0992564bc5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cc0992564bc5); files: `src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.context-engine-helpers.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`)

Remaining risk / open question:

Body:
```markdown
Fixes #84662.

## Summary

- stop prepending OpenClaw current-turn runtime context to the Codex `turn/start.input` user text
- pass that context through `collaborationMode.settings.reference_context` as non-authoritative reference data instead of `developer_instructions`
- sanitize stale mirrored Codex history entries that already contain old `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` user-message wrappers, and start a fresh native Codex thread when a bound thread would otherwise replay them
- update Codex prompt snapshot generation so MEMORY.md/current-turn context is no longer modeled as ordinary user input or developer instructions

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server turns should receive the actual user request as user input, while OpenClaw runtime/workspace context stays out of user history and out of developer-instruction authority. Existing mirrored histories with the old embedded runtime-context wrapper should not be replayed into a resumed native Codex thread.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in `/tmp/openclaw-pr-84790` on macOS, using the repository's Codex app-server runtime builders, mirrored session-history reader, Vitest app-server harness, prompt snapshot generator, and TypeScript test config.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-history.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `node /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --pretty false --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `node /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-history.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-history.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/protocol.ts test/helpers/agents/happy-path-prompt-snapshots.ts && git diff --check`
- `node --import /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`

Evidence after fix:

- Focused runtime tests passed: `Test Files 3 passed (3)`, `Tests 226 passed (226)`.
- Typecheck completed with exit code 0.
- Format/whitespace check completed with `All matched files use the correct format` and `git diff --check` clean.
- Prompt snapshot check completed with `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- The new stale-history runtime test builds a real mirrored session file containing `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` plus `Current user request: prior real request`, writes an existing native Codex binding, runs `runCodexAppServerAttempt`, and verifies no `thread/resume` request is sent, a fresh `thread/start` is sent, the `turn/start` input contains `prior real request` and `new request`, and the stale runtime-context marker is absent.
- The turn-lifecycle test verifies `turn/start.input` is exactly `user request only`, `collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` is `null`, and `collaborationMode.settings.reference_context` has `authority: "reference"` with the OpenClaw runtime context text.
- Regenerated prompt snapshots now show `reference_context` carrying the OpenClaw runtime context and show the developer-instructions layer empty for ordinary user turns.

Observed result after fix: Current-turn OpenClaw runtime context is no longer serialized into Codex user input or developer instructions, and stale history entries that already contain the old wrapper are stripped before mirrored history projection. If such stale history exists with a saved native thread binding, the app-server clears the binding and starts a new native Codex thread instead of resuming a thread that could replay the old privileged-looking user message.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Discord/Telegram production turn against the installed OpenClaw gateway from this PR branch. The proof above exercises the repository's Codex app-server runtime path locally, including the request payloads sent to the app-server client harness and the mirrored session-history reader.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-history.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `node /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --pretty false --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `node /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-history.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-history.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/protocol.ts test/helpers/agents/happy-path-prompt-snapshots.ts && git diff --check`
- `node --import /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`

If this PR is squash-merged or reworked, please preserve author attribution or include:

`Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>`

```

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Codex app-server transcript projection had its own hardcoded tool-output cap (`12_000` chars), so configured OpenClaw limits such as `agents.defaults.contextLimits.toolResultMaxChars = 8000` did not apply before tool output was mirrored into the native app-server thread.

This PR makes `CodexAppServerEventProjector` resolve the configured default/per-agent `toolResultMaxChars` and apply it to both streamed `outputDelta` aggregation and final snapshot tool-result transcript messages. The truncation notice includes the original size and configured limit, with guidance to rerun using narrower tool arguments.

Scope boundary: this does not change dynamic tool execution payloads, the live/display sanitizer, or read-tool behavior. It only bounds the text that gets projected into the Codex app-server/native transcript.

## Motivation / Problem

On a local OpenClaw/Quill WebChat setup, repeated large `bash`/tool results were entering the native app-server transcript at roughly 20K-25K JSON-wrapped characters even though the OpenClaw config set `toolResultMaxChars` to `8000`. Those oversized transcript entries caused avoidable context bloat and lag during WebChat turns.

The latest beta still contains the separate hardcoded path:

- `dist/run-attempt-BMcMyAoZ.js`
- `const TOOL_TRANSCRIPT_OUTPUT_MAX_CHARS = 12e3`
- `appendToolOutputDeltaText(..., delta)` and `truncateToolTranscriptText(text)` both use that local cap

Related to #84659, but not the same bug: #84659 is about live/display/read output clipping, while this PR addresses the Codex app-server/native transcript projection path.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Tests only
- [ ] Chore / maintenance

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] CLI
- [ ] Web UI / frontend
- [ ] Memory / QMD
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Agents / routing
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Other:

## Linked Issues

Related #84659

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression.
- [ ] This PR implements a requested feature.
- [ ] No issue exists yet; details are in the evidence section below.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** The Codex app-server/native transcript path now honors configured default/per-agent `contextLimits.toolResultMaxChars` instead of always using the projector's hardcoded 12K cap for mirrored tool output.

**Real environment tested:** Local macOS OpenClaw setup running OpenClaw `2026.5.19` with the same projector logic backported into the installed runtime, plus latest npm beta `2026.5.20-beta.1` inspected for the pre-fix hardcoded cap.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Reapplied the local runtime preservation script, restarted Gateway, then checked OpenClaw health and the Quill/LinkedIn session budget with live OpenClaw commands:

```bash
/Users/han.kim/.openclaw/workspace/systems/reapply-openclaw-latency-patches.js --restart
openclaw health --json
openclaw tasks list --status running --json
node /Users/han.kim/.openclaw/workspace/systems/linkedin-session-budget.js --agent linkedin --fail-on-risk
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live terminal output from the local OpenClaw setup after the backport and Gateway restart:

```text
$ /Users/han.kim/.openclaw/workspace/systems/reapply-openclaw-latency-patches.js --restart
- codex-app-server-tool-transcript-cap: already patched or pattern not present (run-attempt-DI0_-QFr.js)
- codex-plugin-app-server-tool-transcript-cap: already patched or pattern not present (run-attempt-BqRPAaHk.js)
Done. Changed files: 0.
Restarted LaunchAgent: gui/501/ai.openclaw.gateway

$ openclaw health --json
{
  "ok": true,
  "eventLoop": {
    "degraded": false,
    "reasons": [],
    "delayP99Ms": 24.1,
    "utilization": 0.076
  },
  "plugins": {
    "errors": []
  }
}

$ openclaw tasks list --status running --json
{
  "count": 0,
  "runtime": null,
  "status": "running",
  "tasks": []
}

$ node /Users/han.kim/.openclaw/workspace/systems/linkedin-session-budget.js --agent linkedin --fail-on-risk
LinkedIn session budget check
Agent: linkedin
Main: agent:linkedin:main | status=done | aborted=no | tokens=36683/80000 (45.9%) | risk=none
Risk flags: none
Historical notices: completedSubagentOverThreshold, completedLatestDomainSessionIsNotMain
Guidance: No session-budget risk detected for live LinkedIn work.
```

Additional upstream source validation after patch:

```text
event-projector.test.ts: 60 passed
oxfmt --check: passed
oxlint: Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
pnpm tsgo:extensions:test: exited 0
git diff --check: no whitespace errors
```

**Observed result after fix:** The installed local OpenClaw runtime kept the app-server transcript-cap patch after reapply/restart, Gateway returned healthy after settling, there were no live stuck tasks, and the LinkedIn/Quill budget monitor reported no live over-budget risk. The upstream source tests verify both streamed `outputDelta` and final snapshot tool-result transcript projection use configured `toolResultMaxChars` rather than the old hardcoded cap.

**What was not tested:** I did not install and run a full OpenClaw build from this PR branch in a separate clean WebChat instance. The projector behavior is covered by targeted upstream tests and was locally backported to the installed OpenClaw runtime for live setup validation.

## Root Cause

`CodexAppServerEventProjector` maintained a separate hardcoded transcript cap instead of resolving the existing OpenClaw `contextLimits.toolResultMaxChars` configuration. The dynamic tool/runtime cap and the app-server/native transcript cap had drifted into two different policy paths.

## Regression Test Plan

Added targeted tests in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts`:

- streamed `outputDelta` aggregation honors `agents.defaults.contextLimits.toolResultMaxChars`
- final snapshot/aggregated tool-result transcript projection honors per-agent `contextLimits.toolResultMaxChars`
- truncation notice includes original size and configured limit

## User-visible Behavior

Long tool-output entries mirrored into the Codex app-server/native transcript will now be shorter when OpenClaw is configured with a lower `toolResultMaxChars`. The transcript includes an explicit truncation notice and suggests rerunning with narrower tool arguments when omitted output is needed.

## Architecture / Data Flow

Before:

```text
command output -> app-server projector hardcoded 12K cap -> JSON-wrapped oversized toolResult transcript message
```

After:

```text
command output -> configured default/per-agent toolResultMaxChars -> bounded transcript result with truncation notice
```

## Security / Privacy

- [ ] Handles secrets, tokens, credentials, or auth state
- [ ] Changes external network calls
- [ ] Changes file-system access scope
- [ ] Changes subprocess execution behavior
- [x] None of the above

Notes: This only changes transcript text truncation inside the existing app-server projection path.

## Human Verification

Commands run successfully:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
git diff --check
```

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Backward Compatibility

- [x] Backward compatible
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Requires config or environment changes
- [ ] Requires migration

Notes: Existing behavior is preserved unless users have configured a lower or higher `contextLimits.toolResultMaxChars`, in which case the app-server transcript now follows that configuration.

## Risk / Rollback

Risk: users may see less tool-output text in the native transcript when their configured cap is lower than the previous hardcoded 12K cap.

Mitigation: truncation now includes the original size, the applied limit, and a clear instruction to rerun with narrower tool arguments for omitted output. Rollback is reverting this patch to restore the previous fixed 12K app-server transcript cap.

```

- 🧂 unranked krab: not merge-ready because proof is missing/unusable or there are serious correctness or safety concerns.

Shiny media proof means a screenshot, video, or linked artifact directly shows the changed behavior. Runtime, network, CSP, and security claims still need visible diagnostics.

input.title: [Bug]: Codex harness sessions stall with active_work_without_progress and stale running/hasActiveRun=false state on 2026.5.19
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #84755: [Bug]: Codex harness sessions stall with active_work_without_progress and stale running/hasActiveRun=false state on 2026.5.19
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
